name: Albert Einstein
facts: Developed the theory of relativity, one of the two pillars of modern physics alongside quantum mechanics.
Published the famous equation E=mc², describing the relationship between energy and mass in 1905.
Won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for the discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect.
Born on March 14, 1879, in Ulm, in the Kingdom of Württemberg in the German Empire.
Worked as a patent clerk in Bern, Switzerland, from 1902 to 1909 while developing groundbreaking theories.
Published four revolutionary papers in 1905, known as the 'Annus Mirabilis' papers, covering the photoelectric effect, Brownian motion, special relativity, and mass-energy equivalence.
Proposed the general theory of relativity in 1915, fundamentally changing the understanding of gravity.
Fled Nazi Germany in 1933 due to his Jewish heritage and settled in the United States.
Joined the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton, New Jersey, where he spent the rest of his career.
Advocated for civil rights in the U.S. and was a member of the NAACP.
Wrote a letter to President Franklin D. Roosevelt in 1939 urging the development of an atomic bomb before Nazi Germany.
Suffered from an abdominal aortic aneurysm and died on April 18, 1955, in Princeton, New Jersey.
Requested that his brain be preserved for scientific study after his death, leading to numerous research projects on its structure.
Known for his iconic wild hair, mustache, and informal style, which became symbols of genius in popular culture.
Often cited as one of the most influential scientists of the 20th century and a symbol of intellectual achievement.

image.author: Ferdinand Schmutzer / Adam Cuerden

summary.fr: Ce scientifique allemand, né en 1879, a révolutionné la physique avec sa théorie de la relativité. Il est célèbre pour l'équation E=mc², qui explique le lien entre l'énergie et la masse. Prix Nobel de physique en 1921, il a marqué l'histoire des sciences modernes.
summary.en: This German scientist, born in 1879, transformed physics with his theory of relativity. He is best known for the equation E=mc², which describes the relationship between energy and mass. A Nobel Prize winner in 1921, he shaped modern science.

name: Leonardo da Vinci
facts: Born on April 15, 1452, in Vinci, Italy, as the illegitimate son of a notary and a peasant woman.
Apprenticed under the artist Andrea del Verrocchio in Florence, where skills in painting, sculpture, and engineering were honed.
Created the iconic painting *Mona Lisa* between 1503 and 1506, which remains one of the most famous artworks in the world.
Painted *The Last Supper* on the wall of the Convent of Santa Maria delle Grazie in Milan between 1495 and 1498.
Designed numerous inventions, including flying machines, armored vehicles, and a prototype for a helicopter, centuries ahead of their time.
Kept extensive notebooks filled with sketches, scientific observations, and theories on subjects like anatomy, optics, and hydraulics.
Dissected human corpses to study anatomy, producing detailed drawings of muscles, bones, and organs that advanced medical knowledge.
Worked as a military engineer for Cesare Borgia, designing fortifications and weapons for his campaigns in Italy.
Spent his final years in France under the patronage of King Francis I, who provided him with a residence at Clos Lucé.
Died on May 2, 1519, in Amboise, France, at the age of 67, with his legacy spanning art, science, and engineering.
Often left paintings unfinished, including *The Adoration of the Magi* and *Saint Jerome in the Wilderness*, due to his perfectionism and diverse interests.
Developed a technique called *sfumato*, which uses subtle gradations of light and shadow to create soft, lifelike transitions in paintings.
Was ambidextrous and wrote many of his notes in mirror script, a form of backward writing that could only be read with a mirror.
Influenced countless artists and scientists, including Michelangelo, Raphael, and later figures like Thomas Edison and the Wright brothers.

image.author: Attributed to Francesco Melzi

summary.fr: Ce génie italien de la Renaissance était à la fois peintre, inventeur et scientifique. Il a créé des œuvres célèbres comme la Joconde et La Cène, tout en imaginant des machines révolutionnaires bien avant leur époque. Son héritage marque à la fois l'art et la science.
summary.en: This Italian Renaissance genius was a painter, inventor, and scientist. He created famous works like the Mona Lisa and The Last Supper while designing groundbreaking machines centuries ahead of their time. His legacy shaped both art and science.

name: Napoleon Bonaparte
facts: Born on August 15, 1769, in Ajaccio, Corsica, to a minor noble family of Italian origin.
Rose to prominence during the French Revolution, becoming a general at the age of 24.
Led a successful coup d'état in 1799, establishing the French Consulate and becoming First Consul.
Crowned himself Emperor of the French in 1804 in a lavish ceremony at Notre-Dame Cathedral.
Reformed French law with the Napoleonic Code, which influenced legal systems worldwide.
Won a series of military victories known as the Napoleonic Wars, expanding French territory across Europe.
Established the Continental System in 1806 to blockade Britain, aiming to cripple its economy.
Invaded Russia in 1812 with a Grande Armée of over 600,000 men, but suffered a catastrophic retreat due to harsh winter conditions.
Abdicated the throne in 1814 after the Allied forces captured Paris, leading to his exile to Elba.
Escaped Elba in 1815 and briefly regained power during the Hundred Days before his final defeat at Waterloo.
Exiled again to the remote island of Saint Helena, where he died on May 5, 1821, at the age of 51.
Known for his strategic genius, charisma, and ability to inspire loyalty among his troops.
His military tactics are still studied in military academies around the world today.
Commissioned the Arc de Triomphe in Paris to honor his Grande Armée and its victories.
His remains were returned to France in 1840 and interred in a grand tomb at Les Invalides in Paris.

summary.fr: Je suis un célèbre empereur français du début du XIXe siècle, connu pour mes victoires militaires et mes réformes juridiques. J'ai dirigé la France après la Révolution et établi le Code Napoléon, qui influence encore les lois aujourd'hui. Mon règne a marqué l'Europe par mes campagnes militaires et ma chute après la défaite de Waterloo.
summary.en: I was a famous French emperor in the early 19th century, known for my military victories and legal reforms. I led France after the Revolution and created the Napoleonic Code, which still influences laws worldwide. My rule shaped Europe through my campaigns and ended with defeat at Waterloo.

name: William Shakespeare
facts: Born in Stratford-upon-Avon, England, in April 1564, traditionally celebrated on the 23rd.
Wrote at least 39 plays, 154 sonnets, two long narrative poems, and a few other verses.
Married Anne Hathaway at the age of 18, with whom he had three children: Susanna, and twins Hamnet and Judith.
Became a managing partner in the Lord Chamberlain's Men, an acting company in London, later known as the King's Men.
Performed for Queen Elizabeth I and later for King James I, who was a patron of the King's Men.
Plays include tragedies like 'Hamlet,' 'Othello,' 'King Lear,' and 'Macbeth,' which are among the most performed and studied works in literature.
Comedies such as 'A Midsummer Night's Dream,' 'Twelfth Night,' and 'Much Ado About Nothing' showcase a mastery of wit and humor.
Histories like 'Henry IV' and 'Richard III' blend drama with historical events, often reflecting on power and leadership.
Invented or popularized over 1,700 words in the English language, including 'eyeball,' 'fashionable,' and 'swagger.'
Coined many phrases still in use today, such as 'wild-goose chase,' 'heart of gold,' and 'break the ice.'
The Globe Theatre, where many of his plays were performed, was built in 1599 and burned down in 1613 during a performance of 'Henry VIII.'
Retired to Stratford-upon-Avon around 1613 and died on April 23, 1616, at the age of 52.
Left his 'second-best bed' to his wife in his will, a bequest that has sparked much speculation and debate.
First Folio, a collection of his plays published in 1623, is one of the most influential books in English literature.
Works have been translated into every major living language and are performed more often than those of any other playwright.

image.author: Attributed to John Taylor

summary.fr: Cet écrivain anglais du XVIe siècle est célèbre pour ses pièces de théâtre et ses poèmes. Il a écrit des tragédies comme Hamlet et des comédies comme Le Songe d'une nuit d'été. Considéré comme le plus grand dramaturge de tous les temps, il a marqué la littérature mondiale.
summary.en: This 16th-century English writer is famous for his plays and poetry. He authored tragedies like Hamlet and comedies such as A Midsummer Night's Dream. Widely regarded as the greatest playwright of all time, he shaped world literature.

name: Cleopatra
facts: Was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt, reigning from 51 BC to 30 BC.
Spoke multiple languages, including Egyptian, which was rare among Ptolemaic rulers who typically spoke only Greek.
Formed a political and romantic alliance with Julius Caesar, which helped secure her throne in 48 BC.
Gave birth to Caesar's son, Caesarion, who was later declared co-ruler of Egypt.
After Caesar's assassination in 44 BC, aligned with Mark Antony, another key Roman leader, to maintain power.
Used her relationship with Mark Antony to secure territories and influence in the eastern Mediterranean.
Famously presented herself wrapped in a carpet to meet Julius Caesar, bypassing her brother's guards.
Commissioned a golden statue of herself to be placed in the Temple of Venus Genetrix in Rome.
Faced defeat alongside Mark Antony at the Battle of Actium in 31 BC against Octavian's forces.
Committed suicide in 30 BC, reportedly by allowing an asp (Egyptian cobra) to bite her, after Octavian's victory.
Her death marked the end of the Hellenistic Era and the beginning of Roman Egypt as a province of the Roman Empire.
Was known for her intelligence, political acumen, and charm, which she used to navigate complex alliances.
Featured prominently in ancient Roman propaganda, often portrayed as a seductress who corrupted Roman leaders.
Her life and reign have been the subject of numerous plays, films, and literary works, including Shakespeare's *Antony and Cleopatra*.
The exact location of her tomb remains one of history's unsolved mysteries, despite extensive searches.

image.author: G. Oberholzer & J. A. Van Eeden

summary.fr: Je fus la dernière reine d'Égypte antique, régnant de 51 à 30 av. J.-C. Connue pour mon intelligence et mes alliances politiques avec Rome, comme avec Jules César et Marc Antoine, j'ai marqué l'histoire par mon influence et ma fin tragique. Mon règne a marqué la fin de l'Égypte indépendante avant sa domination par l'Empire romain.
summary.en: I was the last active ruler of ancient Egypt, reigning from 51 to 30 BC. Famous for my political alliances with Roman leaders like Julius Caesar and Mark Antony, I used my intelligence and charm to secure power. My death marked the end of Egypt’s independence and the start of Roman rule.

name: Michael Jackson
facts: Born on August 29, 1958, in Gary, Indiana, as the seventh of nine children in the Jackson family.
Began performing professionally at age five as part of the Jackson 5, alongside siblings Jackie, Tito, Jermaine, Marlon, and later Randy.
Released the best-selling album of all time, 'Thriller,' in 1982, which won eight Grammy Awards in 1984.
Innovated the music video format with groundbreaking short films for songs like 'Billie Jean,' 'Beat It,' and 'Thriller.'
Known for iconic dance moves, including the moonwalk, which was first publicly performed during a 1983 Motown 25 television special.
Won 13 Grammy Awards and received the Grammy Legend Award and Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award.
Sold over 400 million records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling music artists of all time.
Created the Heal the World Foundation in 1992 to improve the lives of children globally through health care, education, and disaster relief.
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame twice: once as a member of the Jackson 5 in 1997 and again as a solo artist in 2001.
Known for his signature single white glove, fedora hat, and black loafers, which became part of his iconic image.
Faced numerous controversies and legal issues, including two highly publicized trials on charges of child molestation, both resulting in acquittals.
Planned a series of comeback concerts titled 'This Is It' in London, scheduled to begin in July 2009, but died on June 25, 2009, from acute propofol intoxication.
Received the Presidential Humanitarian Award from Ronald Reagan in 1984 for his support of charities helping people overcome alcohol and drug abuse.
Wrote and performed the charity single 'We Are the World' in 1985 with Lionel Richie to raise funds for famine relief in Africa.
Posthumously awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom by Barack Obama in 2014, the highest civilian honor in the United States.

image.author: Matthew Rolston; Distributed by Epic Records

summary.fr: Ce chanteur et danseur américain est une légende de la musique pop. Né en 1958, il a marqué l'histoire avec des albums comme *Thriller*, le plus vendu au monde. Connu pour ses pas de danse iconiques comme le moonwalk, il a révolutionné les clips musicaux et inspiré des générations d'artistes.
summary.en: This American singer and dancer is a pop music legend. Born in 1958, he made history with albums like *Thriller*, the best-selling of all time. Famous for iconic moves like the moonwalk, he transformed music videos and inspired generations of artists.

name: Marilyn Monroe
facts: Born Norma Jeane Mortenson on June 1, 1926, in Los Angeles, California.
Spent much of her childhood in foster homes and an orphanage due to her mother's mental health struggles.
Began her career as a model before signing a film contract with 20th Century Fox in 1946.
Dyed her naturally brunette hair blonde and changed her name to Marilyn Monroe in 1946.
Starred in the 1953 film 'Niagara,' which showcased her as a serious dramatic actress.
Became a major sex symbol of the 1950s with iconic performances in 'Gentlemen Prefer Blondes' and 'How to Marry a Millionaire.'
Sang 'Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend' in the 1953 film 'Gentlemen Prefer Blondes,' a performance that became legendary.
Founded her own production company, Marilyn Monroe Productions, in 1955 to gain more control over her career.
Married baseball legend Joe DiMaggio in 1954, though the marriage lasted only nine months.
Studied method acting at the Actors Studio under Lee Strasberg, which influenced her later performances.
Won a Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Comedy or Musical for her role in 'Some Like It Hot' (1959).
Delivered a memorable performance of 'Happy Birthday, Mr. President' to John F. Kennedy in 1962, just months before her death.
Struggled with mental health issues, insomnia, and substance abuse throughout her adult life.
Died on August 5, 1962, at the age of 36 from a barbiturate overdose, ruled as a probable suicide.

image.commons_file: Marilyn Monroe in How to Marry a Millionaire.jpg
image.author: Sam Shaw

summary.fr: Cette actrice américaine est devenue une icône du cinéma dans les années 1950. Surnommée la « blonde platine », elle a marqué l’histoire grâce à ses rôles dans des films comme *Certains l’aiment chaud* et *Les Hommes préfèrent les blondes*. Symbole de glamour, elle reste l’une des figures les plus célèbres d’Hollywood.
summary.en: This American actress became a cinema icon in the 1950s. Known as the 'blonde bombshell,' she starred in legendary films like *Some Like It Hot* and *Gentlemen Prefer Blondes*. A symbol of glamour, she remains one of Hollywood’s most famous figures.

name: Cristiano Ronaldo
facts: Holds the record for the most official goals scored in football history, surpassing 850 career goals for club and country.
Won five Ballon d'Or awards, recognizing the world's best football player, with the first awarded in 2008.
Became the first player to win league titles in England, Spain, and Italy, achieving this feat with Manchester United, Real Madrid, and Juventus.
Scored a record 14 goals in a single UEFA European Championship tournament across 2016 and 2020 editions.
Is the all-time top scorer for the Portuguese national team with over 120 international goals.
Holds the Guinness World Record for the most followers on Instagram, surpassing 600 million across social media platforms.
Won the UEFA Champions League five times, with victories at Manchester United (2008) and Real Madrid (2014, 2016, 2017, 2018).
Born on February 5, 1985, in Funchal, Madeira, Portugal, and began his professional career at Sporting CP.
Signed with Manchester United in 2003 for a then-record fee for a teenager, becoming a global superstar under Sir Alex Ferguson.
Joined Real Madrid in 2009 for a then-world record transfer fee of £80 million, where he became the club's all-time top scorer.
Scored 450 goals in 438 appearances for Real Madrid, making him the club's highest goal scorer in its history.
Won the European Golden Shoe four times, awarded to the top goal scorer in European leagues, during his career.
Led Portugal to their first major international trophy by winning UEFA Euro 2016, despite missing the final due to injury.
Founded the CR7 brand, encompassing clothing, footwear, fragrances, and hotels, expanding his influence beyond football.
Known for his rigorous training regimen, discipline, and dedication to fitness, often credited for extending his career at the highest level.

image.commons_file: Cristiano Ronaldo playing for Al Nassr FC against Persepolis, September 2023 (cropped).jpg
image.author: Mehrdad Esfahani/SNN

summary.fr: Ce footballeur portugais est l'un des meilleurs joueurs de l'histoire. Il détient le record du plus grand nombre de buts marqués en carrière et a remporté cinq Ballons d'Or. Connu pour son talent et sa longévité, il a joué dans les plus grands clubs européens comme Manchester United, le Real Madrid et la Juventus.
summary.en: This Portuguese footballer is one of the greatest players in history. He holds the record for the most career goals scored and has won five Ballon d'Or awards. Known for his skill and longevity, he has played for top European clubs like Manchester United, Real Madrid, and Juventus.

name: Queen Elizabeth II
facts: Ascended to the throne on February 6, 1952, following the death of her father, King George VI.
Was the longest-reigning British monarch, serving for 70 years until her death in 2022.
Crowned on June 2, 1953, in Westminster Abbey, the first coronation to be televised.
Served as Head of the Commonwealth, a political association of 56 member states, most former British territories.
Met with 13 of the last 14 U.S. presidents during her reign, missing only Lyndon B. Johnson.
Owned more than 30 corgis during her lifetime, a breed she was famously fond of.
Delivered an annual Christmas message to the UK and Commonwealth every year except 1969.
Survived an assassination attempt in 1981 when a teenager fired blanks at her during the Trooping the Colour ceremony.
Played a key role in the decolonization of Africa and the Caribbean during the 1960s and 1970s.
Was the first British monarch to address a joint session of the U.S. Congress in 1991.
Celebrated her Silver, Golden, Diamond, and Platinum Jubilees, marking 25, 50, 60, and 70 years on the throne, respectively.
Underwent a historic state visit to Ireland in 2011, the first by a British monarch since Irish independence.
Delivered a special address to the UK and the world during the COVID-19 pandemic in April 2020, invoking wartime spirit.
Had her reign marked by significant constitutional changes, including devolution in the UK and the Brexit referendum.
Died at Balmoral Castle in Scotland on September 8, 2022, at the age of 96.

image.author:
Original: Joel Rouse/ Ministry of Defence
Derivative: nagualdesign

summary.fr: Cette femme britannique a été la reine du Royaume-Uni pendant 70 ans, ce qui fait d'elle le monarque ayant régné le plus longtemps dans l'histoire du pays. Elle est devenue un symbole de stabilité et de tradition, tout en modernisant la monarchie au fil des décennies. Connue pour son amour des corgis et ses discours de Noël annuels, elle a marqué l'Europe et le Commonwealth.
summary.en: This British woman was the Queen of the United Kingdom for 70 years, making her the longest-reigning monarch in the country's history. She became a symbol of stability and tradition while modernizing the monarchy over the decades. Known for her love of corgis and annual Christmas speeches, she left a lasting impact on Europe and the Commonwealth.

name: Mozart
facts: Was a child prodigy who began composing music at the age of five.
Composed over 600 works, including symphonies, concertos, chamber music, operas, and choral pieces.
Performed for European royalty at the age of six, including before Empress Maria Theresa.
Wrote his first symphony at the age of eight, titled Symphony No. 1 in E-flat major, K. 16.
Created the opera 'The Marriage of Figaro' in 1786, which remains one of the most frequently performed operas today.
Composed the opera 'Don Giovanni' in 1787, blending comedy, drama, and supernatural elements.
Wrote the famous 'Eine kleine Nachtmusik' (A Little Night Music) in 1787, a serenade for strings.
Died at the age of 35 in Vienna on December 5, 1791, under mysterious circumstances, with theories ranging from illness to poisoning.
Was buried in a common grave, and the exact location of his remains is unknown.
Composed the Requiem in D minor, K. 626, which was left unfinished at the time of death and completed by his student Franz Xaver Süssmayr.
Had a playful and mischievous personality, often incorporating humor and wit into his compositions and correspondence.
Was a freemason and composed several works for masonic lodges, including the 'Maurerische Trauermusik' (Masonic Funeral Music).
Traveled extensively throughout Europe as a child, performing and studying with prominent musicians of the time.
Developed a close friendship and professional rivalry with composer Joseph Haydn, who called him the greatest composer he knew.
Struggled financially in his later years despite his fame, often relying on loans and patronage to support his family.

image.author: Barbara Krafft

summary.fr: Ce compositeur autrichien du XVIIIe siècle est l'un des plus grands génies de la musique classique. Enfant prodige, il a commencé à composer dès l'âge de cinq ans et a créé plus de 600 œuvres, dont des opéras célèbres comme 'Les Noces de Figaro'. Sa musique, toujours jouée aujourd'hui, a marqué l'histoire de la musique européenne.
summary.en: This 18th-century Austrian composer is one of the greatest musical geniuses of all time. A child prodigy, he began composing at age five and wrote over 600 works, including famous operas like 'The Marriage of Figaro'. His music remains widely performed and shaped the history of classical music.

name: Pablo Picasso
facts: Born on October 25, 1881, in Málaga, Spain, to an artistic family with his father being a painter and art professor.
Created over 50,000 artworks in his lifetime, including paintings, sculptures, ceramics, drawings, and prints.
Co-founded the Cubist movement, revolutionizing European painting and sculpture with Georges Braque.
Painted 'Les Demoiselles d'Avignon' in 1907, a seminal work in the development of Cubism and modern art.
Developed the collage technique in art, incorporating everyday materials like newspaper clippings into his work.
Spent most of his adult life in France, where he became a prominent figure in the Parisian art scene.
Created 'Guernica' in 1937, a powerful anti-war mural depicting the bombing of the Basque town during the Spanish Civil War.
Had a prolific 'Blue Period' (1901-1904) characterized by somber, monochromatic paintings in shades of blue and blue-green.
Followed by a 'Rose Period' (1904-1906), featuring warmer colors and themes of circus performers and acrobats.
Designed costumes and sets for several ballets, including 'Parade' (1917) for Sergei Diaghilev's Ballets Russes.
Awarded the Lenin Peace Prize in 1962 for his contributions to peace and his artistic achievements.
Had a tumultuous personal life, with multiple marriages and relationships that often influenced his artwork.
Died on April 8, 1973, in Mougins, France, at the age of 91, leaving behind a vast and influential artistic legacy.
His works are displayed in major museums worldwide, including the Musée Picasso in Paris and the Picasso Museum in Barcelona.
Once said, 'Every child is an artist. The problem is how to remain an artist once we grow up.'

summary.fr: Je suis un peintre et sculpteur espagnol né en 1881, célèbre pour avoir cofondé le mouvement cubiste. J’ai révolutionné l’art moderne avec des œuvres comme *Les Demoiselles d’Avignon* et *Guernica*, un tableau puissant contre la guerre. Mon travail a marqué l’histoire de l’art en Europe au XXe siècle.
summary.en: I am a Spanish painter and sculptor born in 1881, best known for co-founding the Cubist movement. My works, like *Les Demoiselles d’Avignon* and *Guernica*, changed modern art and addressed social issues. I created thousands of pieces, shaping 20th-century European art.

name: Charles Darwin
facts: Born on February 12, 1809, in Shrewsbury, England, into a wealthy and well-connected family.
Studied medicine at the University of Edinburgh but left without completing the degree due to a lack of interest in surgery.
Enrolled at Christ's College, Cambridge, to study theology, intending to become a clergyman.
Developed a passion for natural history and collected beetles during his time at Cambridge.
Served as a naturalist aboard HMS Beagle from 1831 to 1836, a voyage that lasted nearly five years and took him around the world.
Published 'Journal and Remarks' (later known as 'The Voyage of the Beagle') in 1839, detailing observations from the expedition.
Married his first cousin, Emma Wedgwood, in 1839, and they had ten children together.
Formulated the theory of natural selection in 1838 after reading Thomas Malthus's 'An Essay on the Principle of Population'.
Spent over 20 years refining his theory before publishing 'On the Origin of Species' in 1859.
Co-presented the theory of natural selection with Alfred Russel Wallace in 1858, after Wallace independently arrived at similar conclusions.
Published 'The Descent of Man, and Selection in Relation to Sex' in 1871, applying evolutionary theory to human evolution.
Received the Royal Medal in 1853, the Copley Medal in 1864, and the Wollaston Medal in 1859 for contributions to science.
Suffered from chronic illnesses throughout his adult life, possibly due to Chagas disease contracted during his travels.
Buried in Westminster Abbey in 1882, near scientists Isaac Newton and John Herschel, in recognition of his contributions to science.
Influenced not only biology but also fields like psychology, anthropology, and philosophy with his evolutionary theories.

image.author: Herbert Rose Barraud

summary.fr: Ce scientifique britannique du XIXe siècle est célèbre pour sa théorie de l'évolution par la sélection naturelle. Il a voyagé autour du monde à bord du HMS Beagle et a publié 'L'Origine des espèces' en 1859. Ses travaux ont révolutionné la biologie et notre compréhension de la vie sur Terre.
summary.en: This 19th-century British scientist is best known for his theory of evolution by natural selection. He traveled the world aboard HMS Beagle and published 'On the Origin of Species' in 1859. His work transformed biology and our understanding of life on Earth.

name: Mahatma Gandhi
facts: Born on October 2, 1869, in Porbandar, a coastal town in present-day Gujarat, India.
Studied law in London and was called to the bar in 1891.
Spent 21 years in South Africa, where he developed his political views and leadership skills.
Led the 1906 nonviolent protest against the Transvaal government's registration act targeting Indians.
Founded the Natal Indian Congress in 1894 to fight discrimination against Indians in South Africa.
Introduced the concept of Satyagraha, or nonviolent resistance, as a means of political protest.
Returned to India in 1915 and soon became a leader in the Indian National Congress.
Led the 1917 Champaran Satyagraha, his first major civil disobedience campaign in India.
Spearheaded the 1930 Salt March, a 24-day, 240-mile protest against the British salt monopoly.
Advocated for the rights of untouchables, whom he called Harijans, or 'children of God.'
Imprisoned multiple times by British authorities for his role in civil disobedience campaigns.
Played a key role in negotiations leading to the 1947 Indian Independence Act, which ended British rule in India.
Assassinated on January 30, 1948, by Nathuram Godse, a Hindu nationalist who opposed his tolerance toward Muslims.
Posthumously awarded the title 'Father of the Nation' for his role in India's struggle for independence.
His birthday, October 2, is celebrated as Gandhi Jayanti, a national holiday in India, and as the International Day of Non-Violence worldwide.

summary.fr: Ce leader indien, né en 1869, est célèbre pour avoir mené la lutte non violente pour l'indépendance de l'Inde contre la domination britannique. Il a introduit le concept de Satyagraha, une forme de résistance pacifique, et a inspiré des mouvements pour les droits civiques dans le monde. Son combat pour la justice sociale et la liberté a marqué l'histoire du XXe siècle.
summary.en: This Indian leader, born in 1869, is renowned for leading India's nonviolent struggle for independence from British rule. He pioneered the concept of Satyagraha, or peaceful resistance, and inspired civil rights movements worldwide. His advocacy for social justice and freedom left a lasting impact on 20th-century history.

name: Martin Luther King Jr.
facts: Delivered the iconic 'I Have a Dream' speech during the 1963 March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom, addressing over 250,000 supporters.
Led the 1955 Montgomery Bus Boycott, a pivotal event in the Civil Rights Movement, after Rosa Parks was arrested for refusing to give up her bus seat.
Founded the Southern Christian Leadership Conference (SCLC) in 1957 to coordinate nonviolent protests against racial segregation and discrimination.
Won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1964 at the age of 35, becoming the youngest recipient at the time for his work in combating racial inequality through nonviolent resistance.
Organized the 1963 Birmingham Campaign, which included sit-ins, marches, and boycotts, leading to the desegregation of public facilities in Birmingham, Alabama.
Wrote the 'Letter from Birmingham Jail' in 1963, defending the strategy of nonviolent resistance to racism and calling for civil disobedience against unjust laws.
Played a key role in the passage of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, which outlawed discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.
Advocated for the Voting Rights Act of 1965, which prohibited racial discrimination in voting, following the Selma to Montgomery marches.
Opposed the Vietnam War in a 1967 speech titled 'Beyond Vietnam: A Time to Break Silence,' arguing that the war diverted resources from domestic anti-poverty programs.
Launched the Poor People's Campaign in 1968 to address economic injustice and poverty among all races, aiming to pressure Congress for an 'Economic Bill of Rights.'
Assassinated on April 4, 1968, in Memphis, Tennessee, where he was supporting a sanitation workers' strike.
Posthumously awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 1977 and the Congressional Gold Medal in 2004 for his contributions to civil rights.
His birthday, January 15, was established as a U.S. federal holiday in 1986, known as Martin Luther King Jr. Day, celebrated on the third Monday of January.
Earned a Ph.D. in systematic theology from Boston University in 1955, with a dissertation titled 'A Comparison of the Conceptions of God in the Thinking of Paul Tillich and Henry Nelson Wieman.'
Inspired by Mahatma Gandhi's philosophy of nonviolent resistance, which he studied during a 1959 trip to India.

summary.fr: Ce leader américain a marqué l'histoire par son combat pour les droits civiques et l'égalité raciale dans les années 1950 et 1960. Il est célèbre pour son discours 'I Have a Dream' et son engagement en faveur de la non-violence. Prix Nobel de la paix en 1964, il a inspiré des millions de personnes à travers le monde.
summary.en: This American leader became a symbol of the civil rights movement in the 1950s and 1960s, fighting for racial equality. He is best known for his 'I Have a Dream' speech and his commitment to nonviolent protest. Awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1964, he remains an iconic figure in history.

name: Elvis Presley
aliases: The King of Rock and Roll

attributes.subfield: rock and roll, pop, gospel, country

facts: Born on January 8, 1935, in Tupelo, Mississippi, to Vernon and Gladys Presley.
Sold over 1 billion records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling solo music artists in history.
First gained fame in 1956 with the release of his hit single 'Heartbreak Hotel'.
Starred in 31 feature films, including 'Jailhouse Rock' and 'Blue Hawaii'.
Served in the U.S. Army from 1958 to 1960, stationed in Germany.
Known for his dynamic stage presence, including his signature hip movements, which sparked controversy in the 1950s.
Won three Grammy Awards, all for his gospel recordings, out of 14 nominations.
Performed the first globally broadcast concert via satellite, 'Aloha from Hawaii,' in 1973, watched by over 1 billion people.
Owned Graceland, a mansion in Memphis, Tennessee, which is now a museum and major tourist attraction.
Died on August 16, 1977, at the age of 42 due to a heart attack, likely exacerbated by prescription drug abuse.
Inducted into multiple music halls of fame, including the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame (1986), Country Music Hall of Fame (1998), and Gospel Music Hall of Fame (2001).
His 1968 comeback special, often called the '68 Comeback Special,' revitalized his career and is considered a landmark in television history.
Recorded over 600 songs, many of which became iconic hits, such as 'Hound Dog,' 'Love Me Tender,' and 'Suspicious Minds.'
Known for his generosity, often giving away cars, jewelry, and cash to friends, family, and even strangers.
Posthumously awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom by President Donald Trump in 2018.

image.commons_file: Elvis Presley 1973 RCA Records and Tapes publicity 2 - cropped.png
image.author: RCA Records

summary.fr: Ce chanteur américain est surnommé le 'Roi du Rock and Roll'. Il a marqué l'histoire de la musique dans les années 1950 avec des tubes comme Heartbreak Hotel et des mouvements de scène audacieux. Graceland, sa demeure à Memphis, est aujourd'hui un musée célèbre.
summary.en: This American singer is known as the 'King of Rock and Roll'. He became a music icon in the 1950s with hits like Heartbreak Hotel and his energetic stage performances. His home, Graceland in Memphis, is now a famous museum.

name: Nelson Mandela
facts: Born on July 18, 1918, in the village of Mvezo in Umtata, then part of South Africa's Cape Province.
Studied law at the University of Fort Hare and the University of Witwatersrand, becoming one of South Africa's first black lawyers.
Joined the African National Congress (ANC) in 1944 and co-founded its Youth League to combat racial oppression.
Led the Defiance Campaign in 1952, a nonviolent protest against apartheid laws that resulted in mass arrests.
Was arrested in 1956 and stood trial for treason alongside 155 other activists, though all were acquitted in 1961.
Co-founded Umkhonto we Sizwe (Spear of the Nation), the ANC's armed wing, in 1961 after the Sharpeville massacre.
Was sentenced to life imprisonment in 1964 during the Rivonia Trial for sabotage and conspiracy to overthrow the government.
Served 27 years in prison, primarily on Robben Island, where he became a global symbol of resistance to apartheid.
Rejected multiple offers of conditional release, insisting on the ANC's demands for an end to apartheid and racial equality.
Released from prison on February 11, 1990, following secret negotiations with the South African government.
Led negotiations with President F.W. de Klerk to dismantle apartheid and establish multiracial elections in 1994.
Became South Africa's first black president in 1994 after the ANC won the country's first democratic elections.
Established the Truth and Reconciliation Commission to investigate human rights abuses during apartheid and promote national healing.
Won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993 alongside F.W. de Klerk for their efforts to end apartheid peacefully.
Stepped down as president in 1999 after one term, setting a precedent for democratic transitions in Africa.

image.author: Kingkongphoto & www.celebrity-photos.com from Laurel

summary.fr: Cet homme sud-africain a lutté toute sa vie contre l'apartheid, un système de ségrégation raciale. Emprisonné pendant 27 ans pour ses convictions, il est devenu un symbole mondial de paix et de réconciliation. Après sa libération, il a été le premier président noir de l'Afrique du Sud en 1994.
summary.en: This South African leader fought against apartheid, a system of racial segregation. Imprisoned for 27 years, he became a global symbol of resistance and reconciliation. After his release, he became South Africa's first Black president in 1994.

name: Walt Disney
facts: Founded The Walt Disney Company in 1923 alongside his brother Roy O. Disney, originally named the Disney Brothers Studio.
Created the iconic character Mickey Mouse in 1928, which debuted in the short film *Steamboat Willie*.
Produced the first full-length animated feature film, *Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs*, in 1937, which took three years to complete.
Won 22 Academy Awards out of 59 nominations, holding the record for the most Oscars won by an individual.
Introduced synchronized sound in animation with the 1928 short *Steamboat Willie*, revolutionizing the industry.
Developed Disneyland, the first-ever theme park of its kind, which opened in Anaheim, California, in 1955.
Pioneered the use of Technicolor in animation, starting with the 1932 short *Flowers and Trees*.
Produced the first daily television series, *Disneyland*, in 1954, which helped finance the theme park.
Created the experimental multiplane camera to add depth to animated scenes, first used in *The Old Mill* (1937).
Was posthumously awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom by President Lyndon B. Johnson in 1964.
Envisioned and began development on Walt Disney World in Florida before his death in 1966, which opened in 1971.
Voiced Mickey Mouse from 1928 until 1947, when he passed the role to Jimmy MacDonald.
Faced bankruptcy early in his career after losing the rights to his first successful character, Oswald the Lucky Rabbit, in 1928.
Was known for his relentless work ethic, often working late into the night and encouraging his animators to innovate.
Received an honorary Oscar in 1939 for *Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs*, which included one full-sized statuette and seven miniature ones.

summary.fr: Cet Américain est un pionnier du cinéma d'animation. Il a créé Mickey Mouse et fondé une entreprise célèbre dans le monde entier. Il a révolutionné les dessins animés avec des techniques innovantes comme le son synchronisé et la couleur.
summary.en: This American was a pioneer in animation and film production. He created Mickey Mouse and founded a globally famous company. He revolutionized cartoons with innovations like synchronized sound and Technicolor.

name: Muhammad Ali
facts: Won the gold medal in light heavyweight boxing at the 1960 Rome Olympics at the age of 18.
Became the world heavyweight champion at age 22 by defeating Sonny Liston in 1964.
Changed his name from Cassius Clay to Muhammad Ali after joining the Nation of Islam in 1964.
Refused to be drafted into the U.S. military during the Vietnam War in 1967, citing religious beliefs and opposition to the war.
Was stripped of his boxing title and sentenced to five years in prison for draft evasion, though he remained free while appealing the decision.
Had his boxing license reinstated in 1970 and returned to the ring after a three-year hiatus.
Fought Joe Frazier in the
Fight of the Century
in 1971, losing for the first time in his professional career.
Regained the heavyweight title by defeating George Foreman in the
Rumble in the Jungle
in 1974, using the famous
rope-a-dope
Successfully defended his title against Joe Frazier in the
Thrilla in Manila
in 1975, considered one of the greatest boxing matches of all time.
Retired from boxing in 1981 with a professional record of 56 wins (37 knockouts) and 5 losses.
Was diagnosed with Parkinson's syndrome in 1984, which many attributed to boxing-related brain injuries.
Lit the Olympic cauldron at the 1996 Atlanta Games, receiving a replacement gold medal for the one lost decades earlier.
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President George W. Bush in 2005.
Founded the Muhammad Ali Center in Louisville, Kentucky, in 2005, promoting peace, social responsibility, and respect.
Passed away on June 3, 2016, at the age of 74 due to septic shock.

summary.fr: Ce célèbre boxeur américain a marqué l'histoire en devenant champion du monde des poids lourds à seulement 22 ans. Surnommé « The Greatest », il a refusé de servir dans l'armée pendant la guerre du Vietnam pour des raisons religieuses et politiques. Son style unique et ses combats légendaires, comme le « Rumble in the Jungle », ont fait de lui une icône mondiale.
summary.en: This legendary American boxer made history by becoming the world heavyweight champion at just 22 years old. Known as « The Greatest », he refused to fight in the Vietnam War due to his religious beliefs and opposition to the conflict. His unique style and iconic fights, like the « Rumble in the Jungle », cemented his status as a global sports icon.

name: Marie Curie
facts: Was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize and remains the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields: Physics (1903) and Chemistry (1911).
Coined the term 'radioactivity' and discovered two new elements, polonium (named after her native Poland) and radium, in 1898.
Conducted pioneering research on radioactivity alongside her husband, Pierre Curie, leading to groundbreaking advancements in medical treatments for cancer.
Born Maria Skłodowska on November 7, 1867, in Warsaw, Poland, which was then part of the Russian Empire.
Moved to Paris in 1891 to study physics and mathematics at the Sorbonne, where she earned her degrees and later became the first female professor.
Developed mobile radiography units, known as 'Little Curies,' to provide X-ray services to field hospitals during World War I.
Founded the Curie Institutes in Paris and Warsaw, which remain major centers of medical research today.
Died on July 4, 1934, from aplastic anemia, likely caused by prolonged exposure to radiation without adequate safety measures.
Her notebooks and personal belongings are still radioactive and are stored in lead-lined boxes, requiring protective gear to handle.
Became the first woman to be entombed on her own merits in the Panthéon in Paris, where she rests alongside her husband, Pierre.
Advocated for the use of radium in medicine, leading to the development of radiation therapy for cancer treatment.
Published over 30 scientific papers during her career, many of which laid the foundation for modern nuclear physics.
Faced significant gender discrimination in the scientific community but persisted, becoming a symbol of women's contributions to science.
Received numerous honorary degrees and memberships in scientific societies worldwide, including the Royal Society of London.

summary.fr: Je suis une scientifique polonaise célèbre pour mes travaux sur la radioactivité. J'ai découvert deux éléments chimiques, le polonium et le radium, et j'ai été la première femme à remporter un prix Nobel, en fait deux, en physique et en chimie. Mon travail a révolutionné la médecine et la science au début du 20e siècle.
summary.en: I am a Polish scientist famous for my research on radioactivity. I discovered two chemical elements, polonium and radium, and was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize—in fact, two, in Physics and Chemistry. My work transformed medicine and science in the early 20th century.

name: Steve Jobs
aliases: The Father of the Digital Revolution
facts: Co-founded Apple Inc. in 1976 with Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne in a garage in Los Altos, California.
Introduced the Apple I computer, one of the first personal computers, in 1976.
Launched the Apple II in 1977, which became one of the first highly successful mass-produced microcomputers.
Was ousted from Apple in 1985 after a power struggle with the board of directors.
Founded NeXT Computer in 1985, focusing on high-end workstations for the education and business markets.
Acquired the computer graphics division of Lucasfilm in 1986, which later became Pixar Animation Studios.
Returned to Apple in 1997 after the company acquired NeXT, becoming interim CEO and later permanent CEO.
Revitalized Apple with the introduction of the iMac in 1998, which featured a colorful, all-in-one design.
Launched the iPod in 2001, revolutionizing the music industry with digital music players and the iTunes Store.
Introduced the iPhone in 2007, which redefined the smartphone industry and became a cultural phenomenon.
Unveiled the iPad in 2010, creating a new category of mobile computing devices.
Delivered the famous Stanford commencement speech in 2005, urging graduates to 'stay hungry, stay foolish.'
Diagnosed with a rare form of pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor in 2003 and underwent a liver transplant in 2009.
Resigned as Apple CEO in August 2011 due to health issues and passed away on October 5, 2011.
Posthumously awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom by President Joe Biden in 2022.

image.author: MetalGearLiquid, based on File:Steve_Jobs_Headshot_2010-CROP.jpg made by Matt Yohe

summary.fr: Cet entrepreneur américain a cofondé Apple en 1976, révolutionnant l'informatique avec des produits comme l'iPhone et l'iPad. Visionnaire dans le domaine de la technologie, il a aussi dirigé Pixar et marqué l'industrie du divertissement. Son parcours inspire par son innovation et sa persévérance.
summary.en: This American entrepreneur co-founded Apple in 1976, transforming technology with groundbreaking products like the iPhone and iPad. A pioneer in business and innovation, he also led Pixar and reshaped the entertainment industry. His legacy is defined by creativity and determination.

name: Lionel Messi
facts: Won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022, scoring seven goals during the tournament, including two in the final against France.
Holds the record for the most goals scored in a calendar year, with 91 goals in 2012 for club and country.
Won a record eight Ballon d'Or awards, the most by any football player in history.
Spent 17 seasons at FC Barcelona, where he became the club's all-time top scorer with 672 goals in 778 appearances.
Led Barcelona to a historic sextuple in 2009, winning six major trophies in a single calendar year.
Diagnosed with growth hormone deficiency as a child, which required expensive medical treatment that Barcelona agreed to cover as part of his contract at age 13.
Made his professional debut for Barcelona at the age of 16 years and 145 days in a friendly match against Porto in 2003.
Scored his first hat-trick for Barcelona in a 3-0 win against Real Madrid in the 2006-07 season, becoming the youngest player to score in El Clásico at the time.
Won the Golden Boot at the 2014 FIFA World Cup as the tournament's top scorer, despite Argentina finishing as runners-up.
Joined Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) in 2021 after leaving Barcelona due to financial constraints faced by the club.
Became the first player to score five goals in a single UEFA Champions League match, achieving this feat against Bayer Leverkusen in 2012.
Holds the record for the most assists in La Liga history, with over 190 assists during his time at Barcelona.
Captained Argentina to victory in the 2021 Copa América, ending a 28-year trophy drought for the national team.
Scored 35 league goals in the 2011-12 season, setting a new record for most goals in a single European league season.
Founded the Leo Messi Foundation in 2007 to support vulnerable children through access to education and healthcare.

image.commons_file: Lionel Messi NE Revolution Inter Miami 7.9.25-178.jpg
image.author: Bryan Berlin

summary.fr: Ce footballeur argentin est considéré comme l'un des meilleurs joueurs de l'histoire. Il a remporté la Coupe du Monde avec l'Argentine en 2022 et détient le record de huit Ballons d'Or. Connu pour son passage légendaire au FC Barcelone, il a marqué plus de 600 buts pour le club.
summary.en: This Argentine footballer is widely regarded as one of the greatest players in history. He won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022 and holds the record for eight Ballon d'Or awards. Famous for his legendary career at FC Barcelona, he scored over 600 goals for the club.

name: Beyonce
facts: Born on September 4, 1981, in Houston, Texas, to Mathew Knowles and Tina Knowles.
Rose to fame in the late 1990s as the lead singer of Destiny's Child, one of the best-selling girl groups of all time.
Released her debut solo album, *Dangerously in Love*, in 2003, which won five Grammy Awards in a single night in 2004.
Married rapper and music mogul Jay-Z in 2008, and the couple is collectively known as 'The Carters'.
Performed at the 2009 MTV Video Music Awards while pregnant with her first child, Blue Ivy Carter, born in 2012.
Headlined the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival in 2018, becoming the first Black woman to do so, with a performance later released as *Homecoming* on Netflix.
Released the visual album *Lemonade* in 2016, which was critically acclaimed and explored themes of Black feminism, infidelity, and resilience.
Has won 32 Grammy Awards, making her the most awarded artist in Grammy history as of 2023.
Co-founded the entertainment company Parkwood Entertainment in 2010, which produces her music, films, and fashion lines.
Launched the activewear brand Ivy Park in 2016, in collaboration with Topshop, later partnering with Adidas in 2019.
Performed at the Super Bowl XLVII halftime show in 2013, delivering a show-stopping performance that became one of the most-watched in history.
Starred in and produced the 2019 film *The Lion King* as the voice of Nala, alongside contributing to its soundtrack, *The Gift*.
Advocates for social justice, including the Black Lives Matter movement, and has donated millions to various causes, including disaster relief and education.
Gave birth to twins, Sir and Rumi Carter, in June 2017, further expanding her family with Jay-Z.
Released the album *Renaissance* in 2022, the first installment of a three-part project, which debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 chart.

image.commons_file: Beyoncé - Tottenham Hotspur Stadium - 1st June 2023 (10 of 118) (52946364598) (best crop).jpg
image.author: Raph_PH

summary.fr: Cette artiste américaine est née en 1981 à Houston. Elle est devenue célèbre comme chanteuse du groupe Destiny's Child avant de lancer une carrière solo couronnée de succès. Avec plus de 30 Grammy Awards, elle est une icône de la musique R&B, pop et hip-hop, connue pour ses performances scéniques et son influence culturelle.
summary.en: This American artist was born in 1981 in Houston. She rose to fame as the lead singer of Destiny's Child before launching a highly successful solo career. With over 30 Grammy Awards, she is a global icon in R&B, pop, and hip-hop, celebrated for her groundbreaking performances and cultural impact.

name: Elon Musk
facts: Co-founded Zip2, a web software company, in 1995, which was sold to Compaq for $307 million in 1999.
Founded X.com in 1999, which later became PayPal and was acquired by eBay for $1.5 billion in stock in 2002.
Founded SpaceX in 2002 with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars.
Became the CEO and product architect of Tesla, Inc. in 2008, leading the company's efforts in electric vehicles and renewable energy.
Oversaw the launch of the Tesla Roadster in 2008, the company's first electric sports car.
Introduced the Tesla Model S, an all-electric luxury sedan, in 2012, which received widespread acclaim and awards.
Launched the Hyperloop concept in 2013, a high-speed transportation system using reduced-pressure tubes.
Founded Neuralink in 2016, a neurotechnology company focused on developing brain–computer interfaces.
Co-founded The Boring Company in 2016, which aims to reduce traffic through a network of underground tunnels.
Acquired Twitter (now rebranded as X) for $44 billion in October 2022, becoming its CEO shortly after.
Was named Time magazine's Person of the Year in 2021 for his influence on technology, space exploration, and energy.
Has expressed ambitions to establish a human colony on Mars, with SpaceX developing the Starship rocket for this purpose.
Was the inspiration for the Tony Stark character in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, as acknowledged by Robert Downey Jr.
Faced significant production challenges with Tesla's Model 3 in 2017-2018, often sleeping on the factory floor to oversee operations.
Has been involved in multiple controversies, including tweets about taking Tesla private in 2018, which led to an SEC lawsuit and his stepping down as Tesla chairman.

summary.fr: Cet entrepreneur sud-africain est connu pour avoir fondé des entreprises innovantes comme Tesla, qui révolutionne les voitures électriques, et SpaceX, qui vise à conquérir l’espace. Il est aussi à l’origine de projets futuristes comme le Hyperloop et Neuralink, et a marqué l’histoire en rachetant Twitter en 2022. Son influence dans la technologie et l’exploration spatiale en fait une figure majeure du XXIe siècle.
summary.en: This South African entrepreneur is famous for founding innovative companies like Tesla, which leads the electric car industry, and SpaceX, aiming to make space travel accessible. He also introduced futuristic projects like the Hyperloop and Neuralink, and made headlines by acquiring Twitter in 2022. His impact on technology and space exploration makes him a key figure of the 21st century.

name: Barack Obama
facts: Served as the 44th President of the United States from 2009 to 2017, becoming the first African American to hold the office.
Born on August 4, 1961, in Honolulu, Hawaii, to a Kenyan father and an American mother.
Graduated from Columbia University in 1983 with a degree in political science.
Worked as a community organizer in Chicago before earning a Juris Doctor from Harvard Law School in 1991.
Became the first African American president of the Harvard Law Review in 1990.
Taught constitutional law at the University of Chicago Law School from 1992 to 2004.
Served in the Illinois State Senate from 1997 to 2004, representing the 13th district.
Delivered the keynote address at the 2004 Democratic National Convention, which catapulted him into national prominence.
Elected to the U.S. Senate in 2004, representing Illinois, and served until 2008.
Won the 2009 Nobel Peace Prize for his extraordinary efforts to strengthen international diplomacy and cooperation between peoples.
Signed the Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) into law in 2010, expanding healthcare coverage to millions of Americans.
Authorized the operation that led to the death of Osama bin Laden in Abbottabad, Pakistan, on May 2, 2011.
Reelected in 2012, defeating Republican nominee Mitt Romney with 332 electoral votes.
Established the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program in 2012 to protect eligible immigrant youth from deportation.
Published several books, including the memoir *Dreams from My Father* (1995) and *The Audacity of Hope* (2006).

image.author: Official White House Photo by Pete Souza

summary.fr: Il a été le 44ème président des États-Unis de 2009 à 2017, devenant le premier Afro-Américain à occuper ce poste. Né à Hawaï en 1961, il a étudié le droit à Harvard et a marqué l'histoire en réformant le système de santé américain avec l'Obamacare.
summary.en: He served as the 44th President of the United States from 2009 to 2017, becoming the first African American to hold the office. Born in Hawaii in 1961, he studied law at Harvard and made history by expanding healthcare access through the Affordable Care Act.

name: Taylor Swift
facts: Born on December 13, 1989, in Reading, Pennsylvania, and raised in Wyomissing, Pennsylvania.
Began professional songwriting at the age of 12, learning to play guitar from a computer repairman.
Signed a songwriting deal with Sony/ATV Music Publishing at the age of 14, making her the youngest artist signed by the company.
Released a self-titled debut album in 2006, which peaked at number five on the Billboard 200 and stayed on the chart for 157 weeks.
Won the Grammy Award for Best New Artist in 2010, becoming one of the youngest winners in the category at age 20.
Known for writing or co-writing all of her own songs, often drawing from personal experiences and relationships.
Released the album *Fearless* in 2008, which won Album of the Year at the Grammy Awards, making her the youngest winner of the award at the time.
Has won 14 Grammy Awards out of 52 nominations as of 2024, including four Album of the Year wins, the most by any artist in history.
Transitioned from country to pop music with the release of the album *1989* in 2014, which won Album of the Year at the Grammy Awards.
Holds the record for the most American Music Awards won by any artist, with 40 wins as of 2024.
Re-recorded her first six albums to regain control of her master recordings, releasing *Fearless (Taylor's Version)* in 2021 and *Red (Taylor's Version)* later that year.
Wrote the hit song *Shake It Off* in response to media criticism and public scrutiny of her personal life.
Headlined the Eras Tour in 2023, which became the highest-grossing tour of all time, earning over $1 billion.
Named Time magazine's Person of the Year in 2023 for her cultural impact and advocacy for artists' rights.
Has sold over 200 million records worldwide, making her one of the best-selling music artists of all time.

image.commons_file: Taylor Swift at the 2023 MTV Video Music Awards (2) (cropped).png
image.author: iHeartRadioCA

summary.fr: Cette artiste américaine est l'une des chanteuses les plus célèbres au monde. Née en 1989, elle a commencé sa carrière dans la musique country avant de se tourner vers la pop. Elle détient le record du plus grand nombre de victoires aux Grammy Awards pour un album de l'année.
summary.en: This American singer-songwriter is one of the most famous musicians in the world. Born in 1989, she began her career in country music before transitioning to pop. She holds the record for the most Album of the Year wins at the Grammy Awards.

name: Leonardo DiCaprio
facts: Born on November 11, 1974, in Los Angeles, California.
Gained early fame as a child actor on the television show *Growing Pains* in the late 1980s.
Rose to international stardom with the 1997 blockbuster *Titanic*, directed by James Cameron.
Won the Academy Award for Best Actor for his role in *The Revenant* (2015) after six previous nominations.
Known for his collaborations with director Martin Scorsese, including *The Aviator*, *The Departed*, and *The Wolf of Wall Street*.
Founded the Leonardo DiCaprio Foundation in 1998, focusing on environmental conservation and climate change advocacy.
Starred in *Inception* (2010), a sci-fi thriller directed by Christopher Nolan, which grossed over $800 million worldwide.
Played the role of Jordan Belfort in *The Wolf of Wall Street* (2013), earning critical acclaim for his performance.
Has been an outspoken activist for environmental causes, addressing the United Nations on climate change multiple times.
Produced the documentary *Before the Flood* (2016), exploring the impacts of climate change globally.
Holds a long-standing record for being one of the highest-paid actors in Hollywood, earning $25 million for *The Beach* (2000).
Dated supermodel Gisele Bündchen in the early 2000s and has been linked to several high-profile relationships since.
Starred in *The Great Gatsby* (2013), directed by Baz Luhrmann, as the iconic character Jay Gatsby.
Received a Golden Globe Award for Best Actor for his role in *The Aviator* (2004).
Is known for his selective choice of film roles, often prioritizing projects with strong narratives or social messages.

summary.fr: Cet acteur américain est né en 1974 à Los Angeles. Il est devenu célèbre grâce au film *Titanic* en 1997 et a remporté un Oscar pour *The Revenant* en 2015. Engagé pour l'environnement, il milite activement contre le changement climatique.
summary.en: This American actor was born in 1974 in Los Angeles. He rose to fame with the 1997 film *Titanic* and won an Oscar for *The Revenant* in 2015. A dedicated environmental activist, he advocates for climate change awareness.

name: Usain Bolt
facts: Holds the world record for the 100 meters at 9.58 seconds, set in 2009 at the World Championships in Berlin.
Also holds the world record for the 200 meters at 19.19 seconds, achieved in the same 2009 World Championships.
Won eight Olympic gold medals, the most by any sprinter in history.
Became the first athlete to win three consecutive Olympic gold medals in the 100 meters (2008, 2012, 2016).
Nicknamed 'Lightning Bolt' due to his unmatched speed on the track.
First broke the 100 meters world record in 2008 at the Reebok Grand Prix in New York with a time of 9.72 seconds.
Known for his signature 'lightning bolt' pose, which became iconic in the world of sports.
Won gold in the 4x100 meters relay at three consecutive Olympics (2008, 2012, 2016).
Retired from athletics after the 2017 World Championships in London, where he won a bronze in the 100 meters.
Born on August 21, 1986, in Sherwood Content, a small town in Trelawny Parish, Jamaica.
Began his athletic career as a cricket player before transitioning to track and field.
Won the IAAF World Athlete of the Year award six times (2008, 2009, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2016).
Co-founded the

summary.fr: Cet athlète jamaïcain est considéré comme l'homme le plus rapide du monde. Il détient les records du monde du 100 mètres et du 200 mètres, établis en 2009. Surnommé 'L'Éclair', il a remporté huit médailles d'or olympiques en athlétisme.
summary.en: This Jamaican athlete is known as the fastest man in the world. He holds the world records for the 100 meters and 200 meters, set in 2009. Nicknamed Lightning Bolt, he won eight Olympic gold medals in track and field.

name: Oprah Winfrey
facts: Born on January 29, 1954, in Kosciusko, Mississippi, to an unmarried teenage mother.
Raised in poverty and moved to Milwaukee at age six to live with her mother.
Began her broadcasting career at age 17 while still in high school at Nashville's WVOL radio.
Became the youngest news anchor and the first black female news anchor at Nashville's WLAC-TV at age 19.
Moved to Baltimore in 1976 to co-anchor the six o'clock news at WJZ-TV.
Launched *The Oprah Winfrey Show* in 1986, which became the highest-rated talk show in American television history.
The show ran for 25 seasons, from 1986 to 2011, and was broadcast in 145 countries.
Founded Harpo Productions in 1986, making her the first black woman to own a major studio.
Produced and starred in the 1985 film *The Color Purple*, earning an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actress.
Established the Oprah Winfrey Leadership Academy for Girls in South Africa in 2007, providing education for underprivileged girls.
Named one of the 100 most influential people of the 20th century by *Time* magazine in 1998.
Launched the Oprah Winfrey Network (OWN) in 2011, a cable channel dedicated to self-improvement and inspirational programming.
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Barack Obama in 2013 for her contributions to broadcasting and philanthropy.
Interviewed over 37,000 people on her talk show, including world leaders, celebrities, and ordinary individuals with extraordinary stories.
Published *O, The Oprah Magazine* in 2000, which became one of the most successful magazine launches in recent history.

summary.fr: Cette personnalité américaine est une icône de la télévision et du cinéma. Née en 1954, elle a marqué l'histoire en devenant la première femme noire à posséder un grand studio de production. Son talk-show, diffusé dans le monde entier, a inspiré des millions de personnes.
summary.en: This American figure is a television and film icon. Born in 1954, she made history as the first Black woman to own a major production studio. Her globally syndicated talk show became one of the most influential programs in TV history.

name: Michael Jordan
facts: Widely regarded as the greatest basketball player of all time, dominating the NBA in the 1980s and 1990s.
Won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls, earning Finals MVP honors in each series.
Played 15 seasons in the NBA, primarily with the Chicago Bulls, and briefly with the Washington Wizards.
Earned five regular-season MVP awards and ten scoring titles during his career.
Known for his iconic dunk from the free-throw line in the 1988 NBA Slam Dunk Contest.
Led the 1992 U.S. Olympic basketball team, known as the 'Dream Team,' to a gold medal in Barcelona.
Retired from basketball in 1993 to pursue a career in professional baseball, playing for the Birmingham Barons.
Returned to the NBA in 1995 and led the Bulls to three more championships in 1996, 1997, and 1998.
Holds the NBA record for the highest career regular-season scoring average at 30.1 points per game.
Won two Olympic gold medals, in 1984 as a college player and in 1992 as a professional.
Inducted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in 2009 and again in 2010 as part of the 'Dream Team.'
Starred in the 1996 live-action/animated film *Space Jam*, alongside Bugs Bunny and other Looney Tunes characters.
Founded the Jordan Brand, a subsidiary of Nike, which became a multi-billion-dollar business.
Known for his competitive drive, famously stating, 'I've missed more than 9,000 shots in my career. I've lost almost 300 games.'
Owns the Charlotte Hornets, an NBA team, and is a prominent figure in sports business and philanthropy.

image.author: Joshua Massel. Cropped by en:User:Quadzilla99

summary.fr: Ce joueur américain est considéré comme le meilleur basketteur de tous les temps. Il a dominé la NBA dans les années 1980 et 1990 avec les Chicago Bulls, remportant six titres de champion. Connu pour ses dunks spectaculaires et son talent exceptionnel, il a marqué l’histoire du sport.
summary.en: This American athlete is widely regarded as the greatest basketball player of all time. He dominated the NBA in the 1980s and 1990s with the Chicago Bulls, winning six championships. Famous for his incredible skills and iconic dunks, he remains a legend in sports history.

name: Madonna
facts: Born on August 16, 1958, in Bay City, Michigan, to parents Silvio Anthony Ciccone and Madonna Louise Fortin.
Moved to New York City in 1978 with only $35 in her pocket to pursue a career in modern dance.
Released her self-titled debut album in 1983, which included hits like 'Holiday' and 'Borderline'.
Achieved global fame with the 1984 album 'Like a Virgin', which topped charts worldwide and included the iconic title track.
Starred in the 1985 film 'Desperately Seeking Susan', marking her breakthrough in cinema.
Married actor Sean Penn in 1985, though the couple divorced in 1989.
Released the critically acclaimed album 'Like a Prayer' in 1989, featuring the controversial title track and 'Express Yourself'.
Founded Maverick Records in 1992, a joint venture with Time Warner, which became a successful label.
Published the coffee table book 'Sex' in 1992, which sparked significant controversy due to its explicit content.
Starred in and directed the 1996 musical film 'Evita', winning a Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Motion Picture Musical or Comedy.
Became a mother for the first time in 1996 with the birth of her daughter Lourdes, fathered by Carlos Leon.
Released the album 'Ray of Light' in 1998, which won four Grammy Awards and is often regarded as one of her best works.
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2008, her first year of eligibility.
Has sold over 300 million records worldwide, making her the best-selling female recording artist of all time according to Guinness World Records.
Known for her constant reinvention and influence on fashion, music videos, and pop culture throughout her career.

summary.fr: Cette artiste américaine est une icône de la musique pop depuis les années 1980. Née en 1958, elle a marqué l'histoire avec des albums comme *Like a Virgin* et *Ray of Light*. Connue pour son style audacieux et ses clips révolutionnaires, elle a aussi joué au cinéma et fondé son propre label.
summary.en: This American artist is a pop music icon since the 1980s. Born in 1958, she became famous with albums like *Like a Virgin* and *Ray of Light*. Known for her bold style and groundbreaking music videos, she also acted in films and founded her own record label.

name: Charlie Chaplin
facts: Born on April 16, 1889, in London, England, into a family of music hall performers.
Created the iconic character 'The Tramp,' known for his bowler hat, toothbrush mustache, and waddling walk.
Directed, produced, wrote, scored, and starred in over 80 films during a career spanning more than 75 years.
Co-founded United Artists in 1919 with Mary Pickford, Douglas Fairbanks, and D.W. Griffith to control their own work.
Released 'The Kid' in 1921, a silent film that blended comedy with pathos and became one of his most acclaimed works.
Wrote, directed, and starred in 'Modern Times' (1936), a satirical critique of industrialization and labor conditions.
Produced 'The Great Dictator' (1940), a political satire mocking Adolf Hitler and fascism, which was his first true sound film.
Received an honorary Academy Award in 1929 for 'versatility and genius in acting, writing, directing, and producing.'
Won a competitive Oscar in 1973 for the score of 'Limelight' (1952), which was ineligible for the original release due to Cold War politics.
Was accused of communist sympathies during the Red Scare, leading to his exile from the United States in 1952.
Settled in Switzerland in 1953 and lived there until his death in 1977.
Received a knighthood from Queen Elizabeth II in 1975 for his contributions to the arts.
Married four times, with his last marriage to Oona O'Neill lasting over 30 years and producing eight children.
Was the first actor to appear on the cover of Time magazine in 1925.
Died on December 25, 1977, in Vevey, Switzerland, at the age of 88.

summary.fr: Ce célèbre acteur et réalisateur britannique est une légende du cinéma muet. Il a créé le personnage emblématique du Vagabond, reconnaissable à son chapeau melon et sa moustache. Connu pour ses comédies et ses films engagés, il a marqué l'histoire du cinéma au XXe siècle.
summary.en: This British actor and filmmaker became a legend of silent cinema. He created the iconic Tramp character, famous for his bowler hat and mustache. A pioneer in comedy and social commentary, he shaped film history in the 20th century.

name: Bruce Lee
facts: Born on November 27, 1940, in San Francisco, California, to parents from Hong Kong.
Debuted as a child actor in Hong Kong films, appearing in over 20 movies by the age of 18.
Developed the martial art philosophy and fighting style known as Jeet Kune Do, meaning 'The Way of the Intercepting Fist'.
Moved to the United States at the age of 18 to study philosophy at the University of Washington.
Opened his first martial arts school, the Lee Jun Fan Gung Fu Institute, in Seattle in 1960.
Starred as Kato in the 1966 TV series 'The Green Hornet,' which brought him widespread recognition in the U.S.
Appeared in several Hong Kong martial arts films in the early 1970s, including 'The Big Boss' (1971) and 'Fist of Fury' (1972).
Directed, wrote, and starred in the 1972 film 'Way of the Dragon,' which featured a famous fight scene with Chuck Norris.
Died on July 20, 1973, at the age of 32, from cerebral edema, just before the release of 'Enter the Dragon'.
Posthumously became a global icon after the release of 'Enter the Dragon' in 1973, which grossed over $200 million worldwide.
Influenced numerous martial artists and actors, including Jackie Chan, Jet Li, and Chuck Norris.
Featured in 'Time' magazine's list of the 100 most influential people of the 20th century.
Founded the Jun Fan Gung Fu Institute in Oakland, California, in 1964, which later expanded to Los Angeles.
Wrote several books on martial arts and philosophy, including 'Tao of Jeet Kune Do' published posthumously in 1975.
Inducted into the Martial Arts History Museum's Hall of Fame in 1999.

summary.fr: Ce célèbre acteur et maître d'arts martiaux est né aux États-Unis mais a grandi à Hong Kong. Il a révolutionné le cinéma d'action dans les années 1970 avec des films comme *Le Jeu de la mort* et *Opération Dragon*. Son style de combat, le Jeet Kune Do, a marqué l'histoire des arts martiaux.
summary.en: This iconic actor and martial artist was born in the U.S. but raised in Hong Kong. He transformed action cinema in the 1970s with films like *Enter the Dragon* and *Fist of Fury*. His fighting style, Jeet Kune Do, became a legendary martial arts philosophy.

name: Abraham Lincoln
facts: Served as the 16th President of the United States from 1861 until his assassination in 1865.
Led the country through the American Civil War, preserving the Union and ending slavery.
Issued the Emancipation Proclamation on January 1, 1863, declaring slaves in Confederate-held territory free.
Delivered the Gettysburg Address on November 19, 1863, a speech that redefined the purpose of the Civil War.
Signed the Homestead Act in 1862, providing 160 acres of public land to settlers for a small fee after five years of residence.
Established the U.S. National Banking System in 1863 to create a uniform national currency.
Was born in a one-room log cabin in Hardin County, Kentucky, on February 12, 1809.
Taught himself law and became a licensed attorney in Illinois in 1836.
Served one term in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1847 to 1849 as a member of the Whig Party.
Debated Stephen A. Douglas in a series of seven debates in 1858 during the Illinois senatorial campaign, focusing on slavery and states' rights.
Was the first U.S. president to be assassinated, shot by John Wilkes Booth on April 14, 1865, at Ford's Theatre in Washington, D.C.
Died the following day, April 15, 1865, at the age of 56.
Is consistently ranked by historians and the public as one of the greatest U.S. presidents.
His face is carved into Mount Rushmore alongside George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, and Theodore Roosevelt.
The Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C., was dedicated in 1922 in his honor.

image.commons_file: Abraham Lincoln O-77 matte collodion print.jpg
image.author: Alexander Gardner

summary.fr: Cet homme politique américain a été le 16e président des États-Unis au XIXe siècle. Il est célèbre pour avoir aboli l'esclavage et dirigé le pays pendant la guerre de Sécession. Son assassinat en 1865 a marqué l'histoire des États-Unis.
summary.en: This American leader served as the 16th U.S. president during the 19th century. He is best known for ending slavery and guiding the nation through the Civil War. His assassination in 1865 made him the first U.S. president to be killed in office.

name: Winston Churchill
facts: Served as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1940 to 1945 and again from 1951 to 1955.
Led Britain to victory during World War II with resolute leadership and inspiring speeches.
Delivered the famous 'We shall fight on the beaches' speech on June 4, 1940, during the Battle of France.
Won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1953 for his historical and biographical works, as well as his oratory.
Was a prolific writer, publishing over 40 books, including a six-volume history of World War II.
Served as an officer in the British Army, seeing action in India, Sudan, and the Second Boer War.
Switched political parties twice, starting as a Conservative, then joining the Liberals, and later returning to the Conservatives.
Played a key role in the Gallipoli Campaign during World War I, which ended in disaster and led to his temporary political exile.
Was an avid painter, creating over 500 paintings throughout his life, many of which were exhibited.
Introduced significant social reforms as Chancellor of the Exchequer, including the People's Budget of 1909.
Was the first person to be made an honorary citizen of the United States in 1963.
Had a well-documented love for cigars, brandy, and champagne, often indulging in them throughout the day.
Survived a plane crash in 1943 during World War II, which he later described as one of his most exciting experiences.
Was known for his sharp wit and humor, once remarking, 'I am ready to meet my Maker. Whether my Maker is prepared for the great ordeal of meeting me is another matter.'

image.author: Yousuf Karsh

summary.fr: Cet homme politique britannique a marqué l'histoire en tant que Premier ministre du Royaume-Uni pendant la Seconde Guerre mondiale. Connu pour ses discours inspirants, il a mené son pays à la victoire grâce à son leadership déterminé. Il a également reçu le prix Nobel de littérature en 1953 pour ses écrits historiques et ses talents d'orateur.
summary.en: This British statesman served as the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom during World War II, leading his country to victory with resolute leadership. Famous for his inspiring speeches, he also won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1953 for his historical works and oratory. He played a key role in 20th-century politics and was a prolific writer.

name: Ludwig van Beethoven
facts: Born in Bonn, Germany, on December 17, 1770, into a family of musicians.
Composed nine symphonies, five piano concertos, and 16 string quartets, among other works.
Began losing his hearing in his late 20s and was completely deaf by the last decade of his life.
Wrote the iconic Symphony No. 5 in C minor, which premiered in 1808.
Created the groundbreaking Symphony No. 9, which includes the choral finale 'Ode to Joy'.
Revolutionized the piano sonata with 32 compositions, including the 'Moonlight Sonata' (Piano Sonata No. 14).
Moved to Vienna in 1792 to study under Joseph Haydn and quickly gained a reputation as a virtuoso pianist.
Published his first major work, a set of three piano trios (Op. 1), in 1795.
Composed the 'Eroica' Symphony (No. 3) in 1804, originally dedicated to Napoleon Bonaparte before retracting the dedication.
Wrote the only opera, 'Fidelio,' which premiered in 1805 after multiple revisions.
Suffered from chronic health issues, including liver disease, which contributed to his death in 1827.
Died in Vienna on March 26, 1827, at the age of 56, with an estimated 20,000 people attending his funeral.
Left behind an unfinished tenth symphony, which has been partially reconstructed using sketches.
