Known for his fiery temper and eccentric personality, often breaking piano strings during performances.
His 'Heiligenstadt Testament,' written in 1802, revealed his despair over his deafness but also his determination to continue composing.

image.author: Joseph Karl Stieler

summary.fr: Ce compositeur allemand du XVIIIe et XIXe siècles est l'un des plus grands génies de la musique classique. Il a révolutionné la symphonie et la sonate pour piano malgré une surdité progressive. Ses œuvres comme la Symphonie n°5 ou la Sonate au Clair de Lune restent célèbres dans le monde entier.
summary.en: This German composer from the late 18th and early 19th centuries is one of the greatest figures in classical music. Despite losing his hearing, he transformed symphonies and piano sonatas, creating masterpieces like the Fifth Symphony and Moonlight Sonata.

name: Frida Kahlo
aliases: Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderón
facts: Was born on July 6, 1907, in Coyoacán, Mexico City, Mexico.
Suffered from polio as a child, which left her with a limp.
Was involved in a near-fatal bus accident at the age of 18, resulting in lifelong health issues and chronic pain.
Underwent over 30 surgeries throughout her life due to injuries sustained in the bus accident.
Began painting while recovering from the bus accident, using a specially made easel that allowed her to paint in bed.
Married fellow artist Diego Rivera in 1929, a relationship marked by both passion and infidelity.
Created around 200 paintings, drawings, and sketches, many of which were self-portraits.
Famous works include 'The Two Fridas' (1939), 'The Broken Column' (1944), and 'Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace and Hummingbird' (1940).
Was a key figure in the Mexican muralism movement, though her work was distinctively personal and symbolic.
Incorporated elements of Mexican folk art and indigenous culture into her paintings, often using vibrant colors.
Exhibited her work in Paris in 1939, where it was admired by artists like Pablo Picasso and André Breton.
Became an icon of feminist art due to her unapologetic depiction of female experience and form.
Died on July 13, 1954, at the age of 47, officially from a pulmonary embolism, though suicide was suspected.
Her former home, La Casa Azul, is now a museum dedicated to her life and work in Coyoacán.
Posthumously became one of the most celebrated artists of the 20th century, with her image and art achieving global recognition.

image.author: Guillermo Kahlo

summary.fr: Frida Kahlo était une peintre mexicaine célèbre pour ses autoportraits vibrants et symboliques. Elle a marqué l'histoire de l'art par son style unique, inspiré par la culture mexicaine et ses expériences personnelles douloureuses. Son œuvre, comme *Les Deux Fridas*, est devenue un symbole du féminisme et de la résilience.
summary.en: Frida Kahlo was a Mexican painter known for her vivid and symbolic self-portraits. She became a key figure in art history with her deeply personal style, reflecting Mexican culture and her own struggles. Her works, like *The Two Fridas*, remain iconic for their feminist and emotional depth.

name: Vincent van Gogh
facts: Born on March 30, 1853, in Groot-Zundert, Netherlands, to a middle-class family with ties to the art world.
Created over 2,100 artworks, including around 860 oil paintings, most of which were completed in the last two years of his life.
Struggled with mental illness throughout his life, including episodes of depression and psychosis, leading to his infamous self-mutilation incident in 1888.
Cut off part of his left ear with a razor during a breakdown and later gifted it to a woman at a brothel in Arles, France.
Spent time in psychiatric hospitals, including the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, where he painted 'The Starry Night' in 1889.
Developed a close friendship with fellow artist Paul Gauguin, which ended abruptly after a heated argument in December 1888.
Sold only one painting, 'The Red Vineyard,' during his lifetime, despite his prolific output and influence on modern art.
Known for his bold, expressive use of color and dramatic brushwork, which became foundational to the Expressionist movement.
Painted iconic works such as 'Sunflowers,' 'The Bedroom,' and 'Irises,' which are now among the most valuable and recognized artworks in the world.
Died on July 29, 1890, from a self-inflicted gunshot wound at the age of 37, though the exact circumstances remain debated.
His brother, Theo van Gogh, was an art dealer who provided financial and emotional support throughout his career, and their extensive correspondence was later published.
Posthumously gained widespread fame and critical acclaim, with his works breaking auction records, including 'Portrait of Dr. Gachet' sold for $82.5 million in 1990.
Influenced countless artists, including the Fauves and German Expressionists, with his innovative techniques and emotional intensity.
Spent formative years working for the art dealers Goupil & Cie before dedicating himself fully to painting in 1880.
Lived in poverty for much of his life, relying on Theo's financial assistance and often trading paintings for food or art supplies.

image.commons_file: Vincent van Gogh - Self-Portrait - Google Art Project (454045).jpg
image.author: Vincent van Gogh

summary.fr: Ce peintre néerlandais du XIXe siècle est célèbre pour ses œuvres vibrantes et expressives comme La Nuit étoilée. Malgré une vie marquée par la maladie mentale et la pauvreté, il a créé plus de 2 000 tableaux. Aujourd’hui, ses peintures sont parmi les plus chères et admirées au monde.
summary.en: This 19th-century Dutch painter is famous for vibrant, expressive works like The Starry Night. Despite struggles with mental illness and poverty, he created over 2,000 artworks. Today, his paintings are some of the most valuable and celebrated in the world.

name: Bob Marley
facts: Born on February 6, 1945, in Nine Mile, Saint Ann Parish, Jamaica, to a black teenage mother and a much older white father.
Formed the vocal group The Wailers in 1963 with Peter Tosh and Bunny Wailer, which became internationally known for reggae music.
Released the album 'Catch a Fire' in 1973, marking the first reggae album produced with rock music sensibilities for a global audience.
Wrote and performed the iconic song 'No Woman, No Cry,' which became one of the most famous reggae tracks of all time.
Survived an assassination attempt in 1976 at his home in Kingston, Jamaica, which led him to temporarily relocate to London.
Organized the 'One Love Peace Concert' in 1978, where political rivals Michael Manley and Edward Seaga joined hands on stage at his urging.
Awarded the United Nations' Peace Medal of the Third World in 1978 for his efforts in promoting peace and justice through music.
Diagnosed with acral lentiginous melanoma in 1977, a form of skin cancer that ultimately led to his death.
Released the album 'Exodus' in 1977, which was named 'Album of the Century' by Time magazine in 1999.
Performed at Zimbabwe's independence celebration in 1980, symbolizing his global influence and commitment to African liberation.
Died on May 11, 1981, in Miami, Florida, at the age of 36, and received a state funeral in Jamaica.
Posthumously inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1994 for his groundbreaking contributions to music.
His compilation album 'Legend,' released in 1984, became the best-selling reggae album of all time, with over 25 million copies sold.
Advocated for the Rastafari movement, which influenced his music, lifestyle, and spiritual beliefs, including the use of cannabis as a sacrament.
Left behind 11 acknowledged children from different relationships, many of whom followed in his musical footsteps.

image.author: Dennis Morris; Distributed by Island Records

summary.fr: Ce musicien jamaïcain est une légende du reggae, connu dans le monde entier. Il a popularisé ce genre musical dans les années 1970 avec des chansons comme No Woman, No Cry. Son engagement pour la paix et la justice a marqué l’histoire de la musique.
summary.en: This Jamaican musician is a global reggae icon who rose to fame in the 1970s. He brought reggae music to international audiences with hits like No Woman, No Cry. His messages of peace and unity made him a cultural and political symbol.

name: Princess Diana
facts: Born on July 1, 1961, into the British aristocracy as Diana Frances Spencer.
Married Prince Charles on July 29, 1981, in a globally televised ceremony watched by an estimated 750 million people.
Became the Princess of Wales upon marriage, a title held until her divorce in 1996.
Used her global influence to advocate for numerous charitable causes, including HIV/AIDS awareness and landmine eradication.
Played a pivotal role in changing public perceptions of HIV/AIDS by shaking hands with patients without gloves in 1987.
Founded and supported over 100 charities, focusing on children, the homeless, and the terminally ill.
Walked through an active minefield in Angola in 1997 to promote the International Campaign to Ban Landmines.
Her humanitarian work contributed to the signing of the Ottawa Treaty, which aimed to ban landmines globally.
Delivered powerful speeches on mental health, addiction, and social stigma, often drawing from personal experiences.
Established the Diana, Princess of Wales Memorial Fund in 1997 to continue her charitable legacy after her death.
Died in a car crash in Paris on August 31, 1997, alongside her companion Dodi Fayed and driver Henri Paul.
Her funeral on September 6, 1997, was broadcast globally and attended by an estimated 2.5 billion people.
Posthumously awarded the United Nations Humanitarian of the Year in 1997 for her global charity work.
Inspired the creation of the Diana Award in 1999, which recognizes young people for their social action or humanitarian efforts.
Remains a cultural icon, often cited as one of the most influential women of the 20th century for her humanitarian impact.

summary.fr: Cette femme britannique, née en 1961, était une figure emblématique de la famille royale. Princesse de Galles, elle a marqué l’histoire par son engagement humanitaire, notamment dans la lutte contre le sida et les mines antipersonnel. Son influence et son héritage caritatif restent célèbres dans le monde entier.
summary.en: This British woman, born in 1961, was a prominent member of the royal family. As Princess of Wales, she became globally known for her humanitarian work, especially in HIV/AIDS awareness and landmine eradication. Her legacy continues to inspire charitable efforts worldwide.

name: Pelé
facts: Widely regarded as one of the greatest football players of all time, often called 'The King of Football'.
Born on October 23, 1940, in Três Corações, Minas Gerais, Brazil.
Scored a total of 1,283 goals in 1,363 games during his professional career, a Guinness World Record.
Won three FIFA World Cup titles with Brazil in 1958, 1962, and 1970, the only player to achieve this feat.
Made his professional debut for Santos FC at the age of 15 in 1956.
Scored two goals in the 1958 World Cup final at age 17, helping Brazil defeat Sweden 5-2.
Played for the New York Cosmos in the North American Soccer League from 1975 to 1977, boosting soccer's popularity in the U.S.
Named FIFA Player of the Century in 2000, sharing the honor with Diego Maradona.
Served as Brazil's Minister of Sports from 1995 to 1998, advocating for sports development and anti-corruption laws.
Scored his 1,000th career goal on November 19, 1969, in a match against Vasco da Gama at the Maracanã Stadium.
Retired from international football in 1971 after 92 appearances and 77 goals for Brazil.
Underwent hip surgery in 2012 and later used a wheelchair due to mobility issues in his final years.
Passed away on December 29, 2022, at the age of 82 due to complications from colon cancer.
Inducted into the American National Soccer Hall of Fame in 1993.
Featured in the documentary 'Pelé: Birth of a Legend' (2016), which chronicles his early life and rise to fame.

summary.fr: Ce footballeur brésilien est considéré comme l'un des plus grands joueurs de l'histoire. Surnommé 'Le Roi du Football', il a remporté trois Coupes du Monde avec le Brésil entre 1958 et 1970. Il a marqué plus de 1 000 buts au cours de sa carrière légendaire.
summary.en: This Brazilian footballer is widely regarded as one of the greatest players in history. Known as 'The King of Football', he won three FIFA World Cups with Brazil between 1958 and 1970. He scored over 1,000 goals during his legendary career.

name: Coco Chanel
facts: Founded the luxury fashion house Chanel in 1910, initially as a millinery shop in Paris.
Revolutionized women's fashion by introducing casual chic as the feminine standard in the post-World War I era.
Popularized the little black dress in 1926, which became a timeless wardrobe staple.
Designed the iconic Chanel No. 5 perfume, launched in 1921, which remains one of the best-selling fragrances in the world.
Introduced the Chanel suit in the 1920s, featuring a collarless jacket and fitted skirt, redefining women's professional attire.
Pioneered the use of jersey fabric in high fashion, previously associated with undergarments, to create comfortable yet elegant designs.
Closed her fashion house during World War II but reopened it in 1954 at the age of 71.
Created the quilted Chanel 2.55 handbag in February 1955, named after its launch date, which became a symbol of luxury.
Was the first fashion designer to be named on Time magazine's list of the 100 most influential people of the 20th century.
Popularized the concept of costume jewelry, mixing faux pearls and gemstones with real ones to make fashion accessible.
Spent part of her early life in an orphanage after her mother's death, where she learned to sew from nuns.
Had a brief career as a cabaret singer before entering the fashion industry, performing under the stage name 'Coco.'
Maintained a long-term relationship with British aristocrat Hugh Grosvenor, 2nd Duke of Westminster, among other high-profile romances.
Was accused of collaborating with the Nazis during World War II, which led to her temporary exile in Switzerland after the war.

summary.fr: Cette créatrice française a révolutionné la mode féminine au XXe siècle. Elle a fondé la maison Chanel et popularisé des pièces intemporelles comme la petite robe noire ou le tailleur en tweed. Son influence dans la haute couture et les parfums, comme le célèbre Chanel N°5, reste inégalée.
summary.en: This French designer transformed women's fashion in the 20th century. She founded the Chanel brand and introduced iconic pieces like the little black dress and the Chanel suit. Her impact on haute couture and fragrances, including Chanel No. 5, remains legendary.

name: Stephen Hawking
facts: Diagnosed with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) at the age of 21, defying doctors' predictions of a short lifespan.
Held the prestigious Lucasian Professorship of Mathematics at the University of Cambridge from 1979 to 2009, a position once held by Isaac Newton.
Developed groundbreaking theories on black holes, including the discovery that they emit radiation, now known as Hawking radiation.
Co-authored a seminal paper in 1970 proving that the universe began as a singularity, a point of infinite density and temperature.
Published the best-selling popular science book *A Brief History of Time* in 1988, which sold over 25 million copies worldwide.
Proposed the no-boundary theory of the universe with James Hartle, suggesting the universe has no distinct beginning or end in time.
Won the Albert Einstein Award in 1978, one of the highest honors in theoretical physics.
Appeared in numerous television shows, including *The Simpsons*, *Star Trek: The Next Generation*, and *The Big Bang Theory*.
Lost the ability to speak in 1985 after a tracheotomy and communicated using a speech-generating device controlled by cheek movements.
Advocated for the colonization of space, warning that humanity must expand beyond Earth to survive long-term threats.
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 2009, the highest civilian award in the United States.
Collaborated with Roger Penrose to develop singularity theorems, which describe the conditions under which singularities form in spacetime.
Delivered a zero-gravity flight in 2007, experiencing weightlessness despite his physical disabilities.
Wrote several children's books with his daughter Lucy Hawking, including *George's Secret Key to the Universe*.
Passed away on March 14, 2018, coincidentally the same date as Albert Einstein's birthday and Pi Day.

summary.fr: Ce scientifique britannique du XXe siècle est célèbre pour ses travaux en physique théorique et sur les trous noirs. Malgré une maladie dégénérative qui l'a privé de mobilité, il a révolutionné notre compréhension de l'univers et rendu la science accessible au grand public grâce à ses livres. Il a occupé une chaire prestigieuse à Cambridge, autrefois détenue par Isaac Newton.
summary.en: This 20th-century British scientist is renowned for his work in theoretical physics and black holes. Despite a degenerative illness that left him immobile, he transformed our understanding of the universe and made science accessible through his popular books. He held a prestigious professorship at Cambridge once held by Isaac Newton.

name: John Lennon
facts: Co-founded the Beatles, one of the most commercially successful and critically acclaimed bands in the history of popular music.
Wrote or co-wrote 22 number-one singles on the US Hot 100 chart, more than any other artist in history at the time of his death.
Born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool, England, during a German air raid in World War II.
Met Paul McCartney in 1957, leading to one of the most famous songwriting partnerships in music history.
Married Cynthia Powell in 1962, with whom he had a son, Julian Lennon, before divorcing in 1968.
Released the album 'John Lennon/Plastic Ono Band' in 1970, a deeply personal and raw work reflecting his primal therapy experiences.
Wrote the song 'Imagine' in 1971, which became an anthem for peace and one of the best-selling singles of his solo career.
Moved to New York City in 1971, where he became involved in political activism, particularly against the Vietnam War.

Was awarded an MBE (Member of the Order of the British Empire) with the Beatles in 1965, but returned it in 1969 as a protest against Britain's involvement in the Biafran War and support for America's war in Vietnam.
Spent five years in a self-imposed retirement from 1975 to 1980 to focus on raising his second son, Sean Lennon, with Yoko Ono.
Released the album 'Double Fantasy' in 1980, marking his return to music shortly before his death.
Was assassinated on December 8, 1980, outside his home at the Dakota apartment building in New York City by Mark David Chapman.
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame twice: as a member of the Beatles in 1988 and as a solo artist in 1994.
Known for his wit, rebellious nature, and outspoken views on politics, war, and social issues, often using his music as a platform for activism.
Collaborated extensively with Yoko Ono, both musically and artistically, producing avant-garde works and conceptual art pieces throughout their relationship.

image.author: Jack Mitchell

summary.fr: Ce musicien britannique est l'un des membres fondateurs des Beatles, le groupe de rock le plus célèbre au monde. Il a écrit des chansons emblématiques comme Imagine et a marqué l'histoire de la musique pop. Né en 1940 à Liverpool, il est aussi connu pour son engagement pour la paix.
summary.en: This British musician co-founded The Beatles, one of the most famous rock bands in history. He wrote iconic songs like Imagine and shaped pop music in the 1960s and 1970s. Born in 1940 in Liverpool, he was also a prominent peace activist.

name: Serena Williams
facts: Won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most by any player in the Open Era.
Held the world No. 1 ranking in singles on eight separate occasions between 2002 and 2017.
Won four Olympic gold medals, one in singles and three in women's doubles with sister Venus Williams.
Began playing tennis at the age of three under the coaching of her father, Richard Williams.
Turned professional in 1995 at the age of 14.
Won her first Grand Slam singles title at the 1999 US Open, defeating Martina Hingis in the final.
Completed the
Serena Slam
by holding all four Grand Slam singles titles simultaneously from 2002 to 2003.
Won 14 Grand Slam doubles titles, all partnering with her older sister Venus Williams.
Known for her powerful serve, which has been recorded at speeds up to 128.6 mph (207 km/h).
Founded the Serena Williams Foundation to promote education and assist victims of violent crimes.
Launched a fashion line called S by Serena in 2018.
Gave birth to her daughter, Alexis Olympia Ohanian Jr., in September 2017.
Returned to professional tennis in March 2018, reaching the Wimbledon and US Open finals later that year.
Wrote a memoir titled 'On the Line,' published in 2009, detailing her life and career.
Advocates for gender and racial equality, often speaking out on social issues.
Starred in the 2021 documentary 'King Richard,' which focuses on her father and coach, Richard Williams.

summary.fr: Cette sportive américaine est l'une des plus grandes joueuses de tennis de l'histoire. Elle a remporté 23 titres du Grand Chelem en simple, un record à l'ère Open. Connue pour son service puissant et son jeu explosif, elle a dominé le tennis féminin pendant plus de deux décennies aux côtés de sa sœur Venus.
summary.en: This American athlete is one of the greatest tennis players in history. She has won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most in the Open Era. Known for her powerful serve and dominant play, she has been a leading figure in women's tennis for over two decades, often teaming up with her sister Venus.

name: Tom Hanks
facts: Born on July 9, 1956, in Concord, California.
Won back-to-back Best Actor Oscars for *Philadelphia* (1993) and *Forrest Gump* (1994).
Starred in iconic films like *Cast Away* (2000), *Saving Private Ryan* (1998), and *The Green Mile* (1999).
Voiced the beloved character Woody in the *Toy Story* franchise (1995–present).
Made his film debut in the low-budget horror movie *He Knows You're Alone* (1980).
Began his career in television with the sitcom *Bosom Buddies* (1980–1982).
Directed and starred in *That Thing You Do!* (1996), a film about a one-hit-wonder band in the 1960s.
Co-founded Playtone, a production company responsible for projects like *Band of Brothers* (2001) and *The Pacific* (2010).
Suffered from Type 2 diabetes, which was diagnosed in the early 2010s.
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Barack Obama in 2016.
Married actress Rita Wilson in 1988, with whom he has two children.
Collaborated with director Steven Spielberg on multiple projects, including *Catch Me If You Can* (2002) and *The Post* (2017).
Published a collection of short stories titled *Uncommon Type* in 2017.
Known for his philanthropic work, including donations to space exploration and veterans' causes.

image.commons_file: Tom Hanks at the Elvis Premiere 2022.jpg
image.author: Eva Rinaldi from Abbotsford, Australia

summary.fr: Cet acteur américain est l'un des plus célèbres du cinéma moderne. Il a remporté deux Oscars consécutifs dans les années 1990 pour ses rôles dans *Philadelphia* et *Forrest Gump*. Connu pour ses collaborations avec Steven Spielberg et sa voix emblématique dans *Toy Story*, il a aussi produit des séries historiques comme *Band of Brothers*.
summary.en: This American actor is one of the most iconic figures in modern cinema. He won back-to-back Oscars in the 1990s for *Philadelphia* and *Forrest Gump*. Famous for his roles in Spielberg films and as the voice of Woody in *Toy Story*, he also produced acclaimed historical series like *Band of Brothers*.

name: Rihanna
facts: Born on February 20, 1988, in Saint Michael, Barbados, to a Barbadian mother and a Guyanese father.
Discovered at the age of 15 by American record producer Evan Rogers while he was vacationing in Barbados.
Released her debut album, *Music of the Sun*, in 2005 at the age of 17, which included the hit single 'Pon de Replay'.
Achieved global fame with the 2007 album *Good Girl Gone Bad*, featuring hits like 'Umbrella' featuring Jay-Z, which topped charts worldwide.
Won her first Grammy Award in 2008 for Best Rap/Sung Collaboration for 'Umbrella' with Jay-Z.
Has released eight studio albums, including *Loud* (2010), *Talk That Talk* (2011), and *Anti* (2016), all of which debuted at number one on the Billboard 200.
Holds the record for the most number-one singles (14) on the Billboard Hot 100 chart by a solo artist, surpassing Michael Jackson.
Founded the Clara Lionel Foundation in 2012, named after her grandparents, to support global education and emergency response programs.
Launched her cosmetics brand, Fenty Beauty, in 2017, which revolutionized the industry with its inclusive range of 40 foundation shades.
Created Savage X Fenty in 2018, a lingerie line celebrated for its inclusivity in size, gender, and race, challenging industry norms.
Became the wealthiest female musician in the world, with an estimated net worth of over $1.7 billion as of 2023, largely due to her business ventures.
Received the NAACP President’s Award in 2020 for her activism and contributions to the Black community, including donations to COVID-19 relief and racial justice causes.
Starred in and executive produced the 2022 film *Bates Motel* spin-off series *The Woman King*, showcasing her expanding influence in Hollywood.
Named a National Hero of Barbados in 2021, becoming the first person to receive the title while still alive, and was honored with a national holiday in her name.
Collaborated with artists like Drake, Eminem, Calvin Harris, and Coldplay, and has sold over 250 million records worldwide, making her one of the best-selling music artists of all time.

image.commons_file: Rihanna visits U.S. Embassy in Barbados 2024 (cropped).jpg
image.author: U.S. Embassy Bridgetown

summary.fr: Cette chanteuse barbadienne est devenue une star mondiale grâce à des tubes comme Umbrella. Elle a révolutionné l'industrie de la beauté avec sa marque Fenty Beauty, connue pour son inclusivité. Connue pour son engagement humanitaire, elle est aussi l'une des musiciennes les plus riches du monde.
summary.en: This Barbadian singer rose to global fame with hits like Umbrella. She transformed the beauty industry with her inclusive Fenty Beauty brand. Also known for her humanitarian work, she is one of the wealthiest musicians in the world.

name: Zinedine Zidane
facts: Born on June 23, 1972, in Marseille, France, to Algerian immigrant parents.
Won the FIFA World Player of the Year award three times (1998, 2000, 2003).
Scored two goals in the 1998 FIFA World Cup final, leading France to a 3-0 victory over Brazil.
Played for top clubs including Juventus and Real Madrid during his professional career.
Won the UEFA Champions League with Real Madrid in 2002, scoring a legendary volley in the final.
Retired from professional football after the 2006 FIFA World Cup, where he famously headbutted Marco Materazzi in the final.
Won the Ballon d'Or in 1998, awarded to the best football player in Europe.
Began his career at AS Cannes before moving to Bordeaux, where he gained prominence.
Managed Real Madrid to three consecutive UEFA Champions League titles from 2016 to 2018.
Known for his exceptional ball control, vision, and elegance on the field, earning the nickname 'Zizou'.
Played a pivotal role in France's victory at UEFA Euro 2000, scoring in the final against Italy.
Inducted into the FIFA 100 list of the greatest living footballers in 2004, selected by Pelé.
Worked as a football advisor for Real Madrid before transitioning into coaching in 2014.
Led Real Madrid to a La Liga title in the 2016-2017 season as head coach.

summary.fr: Ce footballeur français d'origine algérienne est l'un des plus grands joueurs de l'histoire. Il a mené l'équipe de France à la victoire lors de la Coupe du Monde 1998 en marquant deux buts en finale. Connu pour son élégance et sa maîtrise du ballon, il a aussi brillé avec des clubs comme la Juventus et le Real Madrid.
summary.en: This French footballer of Algerian descent is one of the greatest players in history. He led France to victory in the 1998 FIFA World Cup, scoring two goals in the final. Renowned for his elegance and ball control, he also excelled at top clubs like Juventus and Real Madrid.

name: Drake
facts: Born on October 24, 1986, in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.
Began his career as an actor, starring in the teen drama series *Degrassi: The Next Generation* from 2001 to 2009.
Released his debut mixtape, *Room for Improvement*, in 2006, followed by *Comeback Season* in 2007.
Signed to Lil Wayne’s Young Money Entertainment label in 2009 after gaining attention with the mixtape *So Far Gone*.
Released his first studio album, *Thank Me Later*, in 2010, which debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200.
Holds the record for the most *Billboard* Hot 100 top 10 hits, surpassing Madonna in 2021.
Won his first Grammy Award in 2013 for Best Rap Album with *Take Care*.
Co-founded the OVO Sound record label in 2012, which includes artists like PartyNextDoor and Majid Jordan.
Collaborated with global artists such as Rihanna, Future, and Travis Scott on multiple chart-topping tracks.
Released the album *Scorpion* in 2018, which included the hit singles *God’s Plan* and *In My Feelings*.
Became the first artist to surpass 50 billion streams on Spotify in 2021.
Owns a luxury mansion in Toronto nicknamed *The Embassy*, which features a basketball court and recording studio.
Launched a clothing line called OVO (October’s Very Own) in collaboration with Canada Goose and Nike.
Known for his philanthropy, including donating $1 million to COVID-19 relief efforts in 2020.
Set a Guinness World Record in 2016 for the most number-one singles on the *Billboard* R&B/Hip-Hop chart.

summary.fr: Ce rappeur canadien est né en 1986 à Toronto. Il a commencé sa carrière comme acteur avant de devenir l’un des artistes les plus influents de la musique hip-hop. Il détient le record du plus grand nombre de titres classés dans le top 10 du *Billboard* Hot 100.
summary.en: This Canadian rapper was born in 1986 in Toronto. He started his career as an actor before becoming one of the most influential figures in hip-hop music. He holds the record for the most top 10 hits on the *Billboard* Hot 100.

name: Dwayne Johnson
facts: Began his career as a professional wrestler in the WWE, where he became one of the most iconic figures in the sport's history.
Won 10 world championships in WWE, including the WWF/WWE Championship and WCW Championship.
Transitioned to acting in the early 2000s, debuting in *The Mummy Returns* (2001) as the Scorpion King.
Starred in the *Fast & Furious* franchise as Luke Hobbs, a role that significantly boosted his mainstream acting career.
Founded Seven Bucks Productions, a film and television production company, in 2012.
Played the titular character in the *Jumanji* reboot films, *Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle* (2017) and *Jumanji: The Next Level* (2019).
Hosted and executive produced the HBO series *Ballers* (2015-2019), a comedy-drama about NFL players and their financial managers.
Voiced the character Maui in Disney's animated film *Moana* (2016).
Co-starred with Kevin Hart in multiple films, including *Central Intelligence* (2016), *Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle* (2017), and *DC League of Super-Pets* (2022).
Released an autobiography titled *The Rock Says...* in 2000, which became a *New York Times* bestseller.
Graduated from the University of Miami with a Bachelor of General Studies and a focus on criminology and physiology.
Was the first third-generation wrestler in WWE history, following in the footsteps of his father Rocky Johnson and grandfather Peter Maivia.
Starred in the HBO series *Young Rock* (2021), a semi-autobiographical comedy based on his early life and wrestling career.
Received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame in 2023 for his contributions to the entertainment industry.
Is known for his philanthropic work, including partnerships with the Make-A-Wish Foundation and his own charity, The Dwayne Johnson Rock Foundation.

image.author: Harald Krichel

summary.fr: Cette personne est une star américaine connue pour avoir commencé sa carrière comme catcheur professionnel à la WWE. Il est devenu une icône du cinéma grâce à des films comme Fast & Furious et Jumanji. Aujourd'hui, il est aussi producteur et acteur très populaire.
summary.en: This American celebrity started as a professional wrestler in the WWE before becoming a famous actor. He is best known for roles in the Fast & Furious and Jumanji films. He is also a successful producer and one of Hollywood’s most recognizable stars.

name: Eminem
facts: Born on October 17, 1972, in St. Joseph, Missouri, as Marshall Bruce Mathers III.
Released the debut album 'Infinite' in 1996, which sold fewer than 1,000 copies initially.
Gained mainstream fame with the 1999 album 'The Slim Shady LP,' which won a Grammy Award for Best Rap Album.
Won 15 Grammy Awards out of 44 nominations throughout his career.
Holds the Guinness World Record for the fastest rap in a hit single with 'Godzilla,' rapping 10.65 syllables per second.
Co-founded Shady Records, a label that signed artists like 50 Cent and D12.
Starred in the semi-autobiographical film '8 Mile' in 2002, winning an Academy Award for Best Original Song for 'Lose Yourself.'
Released the album 'The Marshall Mathers LP' in 2000, which sold 1.76 million copies in its first week, setting a record for the fastest-selling rap album.
Known for his alter egos Slim Shady and Marshall Mathers, which represent different facets of his personality and music.
Published an autobiography titled 'The Way I Am' in 2008, detailing his struggles with poverty, addiction, and fame.
Overcame an addiction to prescription drugs, which he discussed openly in songs like 'Not Afraid' and 'Going Through Changes.'
Returned to music in 2009 after a hiatus with the album 'Relapse,' which debuted at number one on the Billboard 200.
Collaborated with artists across genres, including Rihanna on 'Love the Way You Lie' and Elton John at the 2001 Grammy Awards.
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 2022 as part of the class of inductees.
Has sold over 220 million records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling music artists of all time.

summary.fr: Ce rappeur américain est l'un des artistes les plus influents de l'histoire du hip-hop. Né en 1972, il a remporté 15 Grammy Awards et détient un record du monde pour son flow ultra-rapide. Connu pour ses albums comme *The Slim Shady LP* et son rôle dans le film *8 Mile*, il a marqué la musique par son style unique et ses textes percutants.
summary.en: This American rapper is one of the most influential artists in hip-hop history. Born in 1972, he has won 15 Grammy Awards and holds a world record for his rapid-fire rap delivery. Famous for albums like *The Slim Shady LP* and his role in *8 Mile*, he revolutionized music with his raw lyrics and distinctive style.

name: Isaac Newton
facts: Formulated the three fundamental laws of motion, which became the cornerstone of classical mechanics.
Discovered the law of universal gravitation, explaining the force that governs the motion of planets and objects on Earth.
Published 'Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica' in 1687, one of the most influential books in the history of science.
Developed the mathematical field of calculus independently of Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz, leading to a bitter dispute over priority.
Invented the reflecting telescope in 1668, which used mirrors to reduce optical distortions.
Served as the Lucasian Professor of Mathematics at the University of Cambridge from 1669 to 1701.
Was elected President of the Royal Society in 1703, a position held until his death in 1727.
Conducted groundbreaking experiments in optics, demonstrating that white light is composed of a spectrum of colors.
Published 'Opticks' in 1704, detailing theories on light, color, and the behavior of light particles.
Spent much of his later life studying alchemy and theology, though these works remained unpublished during his lifetime.
Was knighted by Queen Anne in 1705, becoming Sir Isaac Newton, primarily for his political rather than scientific contributions.
Suffered a nervous breakdown in 1693, possibly due to mercury poisoning from his alchemical experiments.
Served as Warden and later Master of the Royal Mint from 1696 to 1727, reforming England's currency system.
Was born on January 4, 1643, in Woolsthorpe, Lincolnshire, England, according to the Gregorian calendar.
Died on March 31, 1727, in London, and was buried in Westminster Abbey with honors reserved for eminent figures.

image.author: James Thronill after Sir Godfrey Kneller

summary.fr: Ce scientifique anglais du XVIIe siècle a révolutionné la physique avec ses lois du mouvement et la théorie de la gravitation universelle. Il a également contribué aux mathématiques en développant le calcul infinitésimal et a marqué l'histoire de l'optique en étudiant la décomposition de la lumière.
summary.en: This 17th-century English scientist transformed physics with his laws of motion and the theory of universal gravitation. He also advanced mathematics by developing calculus and made groundbreaking discoveries in optics, including the nature of light and color.

name: Nikola Tesla
aliases: The Man Who Invented the Twentieth Century
facts: Was born on July 10, 1856, in Smiljan, Austrian Empire (modern-day Croatia).
Developed the alternating current (AC) electrical system, which is the predominant system used worldwide today.
Held over 300 patents for inventions in various fields, including electrical engineering and mechanical engineering.
Invented the Tesla coil in 1891, a device that produces high-voltage, low-current, high-frequency alternating-current electricity.
Worked for Thomas Edison early in his career but left due to disputes over payment and differing views on electrical systems.
Designed the first hydroelectric power plant at Niagara Falls in 1895, utilizing AC power.
Envisioned wireless transmission of energy and conducted experiments in Colorado Springs in 1899 to explore this concept.
Proposed the idea of a
world wireless system
that could provide free energy and communication globally.
Suffered from obsessive-compulsive disorder and had an extraordinary memory, reportedly able to recall entire books.
Claimed to have developed a particle beam weapon, which he called the 'death ray,' though no working model was ever produced.
Filed a patent for a vertical take-off and landing aircraft in 1928, one of the earliest designs for such a vehicle.
Lived the last decade of his life in relative obscurity at the New Yorker Hotel, where he fed pigeons daily.
Died penniless on January 7, 1943, in New York City at the age of 86.
The SI unit of magnetic flux density, the tesla (T), was named in his honor in 1960.

image.author: Napoleon Sarony

summary.fr: Ce scientifique serbe du XIXe siècle a révolutionné l'électricité en développant le système à courant alternatif, utilisé aujourd'hui dans le monde entier. Inventeur prolifique, il a imaginé des technologies comme la bobine Tesla et travaillé sur la transmission sans fil d'énergie, tout en collaborant brièvement avec Thomas Edison.
summary.en: This 19th-century Serbian scientist transformed electricity by developing the alternating current system, now used globally. A prolific inventor, he created the Tesla coil and pioneered wireless energy transmission, while briefly working with Thomas Edison before their famous rivalry.

name: Galileo Galilei
facts: Born on February 15, 1564, in Pisa, Italy, into a family of seven children.
Improved the design of the telescope in 1609, increasing its magnification to 20x.
Discovered the four largest moons of Jupiter, now known as the Galilean moons, in January 1610.
Observed and documented the phases of Venus, providing evidence for the heliocentric model of the solar system.
Published 'Sidereus Nuncius' (Starry Messenger) in 1610, detailing his astronomical discoveries.
Formulated the law of falling bodies, demonstrating that objects fall at the same rate regardless of mass.
Invented the hydrostatic balance, a device for measuring the density of objects.
Faced opposition from the Catholic Church for supporting Copernican heliocentrism, leading to his trial in 1633.
Was sentenced to house arrest by the Inquisition after being found 'vehemently suspect of heresy'.
Published 'Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems' in 1632, comparing the Ptolemaic and Copernican systems.
Developed the concept of inertia, later refined by Isaac Newton into his first law of motion.
Made significant contributions to the scientific method, emphasizing experimentation and observation.
Lost his sight in later years, possibly due to glaucoma or cataracts, but continued working until his death.
Died on January 8, 1642, in Arcetri, near Florence, Italy, at the age of 77.
Was posthumously pardoned by the Catholic Church in 1992, 350 years after his trial.

image.author: Justus Sustermans

summary.fr: Je suis un scientifique italien du XVIe siècle, connu pour avoir amélioré la lunette astronomique et découvert les quatre plus grandes lunes de Jupiter. Mes observations ont soutenu l'idée que la Terre tourne autour du Soleil, ce qui a provoqué des tensions avec l'Église. Mon travail en physique a aussi posé les bases des lois du mouvement.
summary.en: I was a 16th-century Italian scientist who improved the telescope and discovered Jupiter’s four largest moons. My observations supported the idea that Earth orbits the Sun, leading to conflict with the Church. I also made key contributions to physics, including early concepts of motion.

name: Aristotle
facts: Born in 384 BCE in Stagira, a small town in northern Greece, to a family of physicians.
Became a student of Plato at the age of 17 and studied at his Academy in Athens for nearly 20 years.
Tutored Alexander the Great from 343 BCE, shaping the mind of one of history's greatest military leaders.
Founded the Lyceum in Athens, a school where he taught and conducted research for over a decade.
Wrote an estimated 200 works, of which only 31 survive, covering topics like logic, metaphysics, ethics, and biology.
Developed the concept of syllogism, a form of logical argument that became the foundation of Western logic.
Introduced the idea of the
Golden Mean,
advocating for moderation between extremes in ethics.
Classified animals into species and genera, laying early groundwork for the field of biology and taxonomy.
Nicomachean Ethics,
a seminal work on virtue, happiness, and moral character.
Proposed the four causes (material, formal, efficient, and final) as a framework for understanding existence.
Argued that the Earth was spherical and stationary at the center of the universe in his cosmological work.
Influenced medieval Islamic and Christian scholastic philosophy, particularly through thinkers like Thomas Aquinas.
Died in 322 BCE in Chalcis, Greece, reportedly from a stomach illness at the age of 62.
His works were lost to the Western world for centuries but rediscovered in the 12th century through Arabic translations.
Considered one of the founding figures of Western philosophy, alongside his teacher Plato and Socrates.

image.author: After Lysippos

summary.fr: Ce philosophe grec de l'Antiquité est l'un des penseurs les plus influents de l'histoire. Élève de Platon et précepteur d'Alexandre le Grand, il a fondé le Lycée à Athènes et écrit sur la logique, l'éthique et la biologie. Ses idées, comme la théorie du juste milieu, ont marqué la philosophie occidentale.
summary.en: This ancient Greek philosopher is one of history's most influential thinkers. A student of Plato and tutor to Alexander the Great, he founded the Lyceum in Athens and wrote on logic, ethics, and biology. His ideas, like the Golden Mean, shaped Western philosophy and science.

name: Alexander the Great
facts: Became king of Macedon at the age of 20 following the assassination of his father, Philip II, in 336 BCE.
Created one of the largest empires in history by the age of 30, stretching from Greece to northwestern India.
Undefeated in battle, considered one of history's most successful military commanders.
Tutored by the philosopher Aristotle until the age of 16, which greatly influenced his intellectual development.
Crossed the Hellespont into Asia in 334 BCE with an army of approximately 48,100 soldiers to begin his conquest of the Persian Empire.
Won the Battle of Issus in 333 BCE against Darius III, securing control over Anatolia.
Founded over 20 cities named Alexandria, the most famous being Alexandria in Egypt, established in 331 BCE.
Conquered Egypt without a fight and was declared pharaoh, seen as a liberator from Persian rule.
Visited the Oracle of Siwa in Egypt, where he was allegedly declared the son of Zeus-Ammon.
Defeated Darius III decisively at the Battle of Gaugamela in 331 BCE, effectively ending the Persian Empire.
Burned the Persian capital of Persepolis in 330 BCE, a controversial act seen as revenge for the Persian invasion of Greece.
Married Roxana, a Sogdian princess, in 327 BCE to solidify alliances in Central Asia.
Led his army across the Hindu Kush into India, winning the Battle of the Hydaspes against King Porus in 326 BCE.
His troops mutinied at the Hyphasis River, refusing to march further east, forcing a return to Babylon.
Died in Babylon in 323 BCE at the age of 32, possibly from typhoid fever, poisoning, or excessive drinking.

summary.fr: Ce grand conquérant de l'Antiquité est devenu roi de Macédoine à seulement 20 ans. Il a créé l'un des plus vastes empires de l'histoire, s'étendant de la Grèce jusqu'à l'Inde, sans jamais perdre une bataille. Célèbre pour ses stratégies militaires, il fonda aussi plusieurs villes, dont Alexandrie en Égypte.
summary.en: This ancient Macedonian king became ruler at just 20 years old and built one of history's largest empires, stretching from Greece to India. Known as one of the greatest military leaders, he never lost a battle and founded cities like Alexandria in Egypt.

name: Julius Caesar
facts: Born on July 12 or 13, 100 BCE, in Rome, into the prestigious Julian clan, which claimed descent from the goddess Venus.
Played a critical role in the fall of the Roman Republic and the rise of the Roman Empire through a series of political and military campaigns.
Formed the First Triumvirate, a political alliance with Pompey the Great and Marcus Licinius Crassus, in 60 BCE to dominate Roman politics.
Conquered Gaul (modern-day France and Belgium) between 58 and 50 BCE, expanding Rome's territory and securing immense wealth and loyalty from his legions.
Wrote the influential *Commentarii de Bello Gallico* (*Commentaries on the Gallic War*), a firsthand account of his military campaigns in Gaul.
Crossed the Rubicon River in 49 BCE with his army, declaring war on the Roman Senate and famously uttering, 'The die is cast.'
Defeated Pompey in the civil war (49–45 BCE), pursuing him to Egypt where Pompey was assassinated by agents of King Ptolemy XIII.
Installed Cleopatra VII as the ruler of Egypt and had a son with her, Caesarion, in 47 BCE.
Reformed the Roman calendar in 46 BCE, creating the Julian calendar, which became the basis for the modern Gregorian calendar.
Named *dictator perpetuo* (dictator in perpetuity) in 44 BCE, centralizing power and alarming senators who feared the end of the Republic.
Assassinated on the Ides of March (March 15), 44 BCE, by a group of senators led by Brutus and Cassius, who stabbed him 23 times in the Theatre of Pompey.
His death led to another civil war, ultimately resulting in the rise of his adopted heir, Octavian (Augustus), and the establishment of the Roman Empire.
Introduced land reforms to provide for veterans and the poor, reducing unemployment and increasing his popularity among the lower classes.
Expanded Roman citizenship to provinces outside Italy, integrating conquered peoples into the Roman political system.
His name, 'Caesar,' became synonymous with imperial authority, evolving into titles like 'Kaiser' and 'Tsar' in later European history.

image.author: Ángel M. Felicísimo from Mérida, España

summary.fr: Né en 100 av. J.-C. à Rome, Jules César fut un général et homme d'État romain célèbre pour ses conquêtes militaires, notamment en Gaule. Il joua un rôle clé dans la chute de la République romaine et la naissance de l'Empire, avant d'être assassiné par des sénateurs en 44 av. J.-C. Son nom est associé à des réformes politiques et au calendrier julien, encore utilisé aujourd'hui.
summary.en: Born in 100 BCE in Rome, Julius Caesar was a Roman general and statesman known for his military campaigns, including the conquest of Gaul. He played a pivotal role in transforming the Roman Republic into the Roman Empire before being assassinated by senators in 44 BCE. His legacy includes political reforms and the Julian calendar, which influenced the modern calendar system.

name: Genghis Khan
facts: Founded the Mongol Empire in 1206, which became the largest contiguous land empire in history.
United the nomadic tribes of Northeast Asia under a single leadership, adopting the title 'Genghis Khan' meaning 'Universal Ruler'.
Implemented the Yassa, a code of law that promoted meritocracy and religious tolerance within the empire.
Introduced a messenger system called the Yam, which facilitated rapid communication across vast territories.
Led military campaigns that conquered much of Central Asia and China, including the Khwarezmian Empire in 1219–1221.
Employed psychological warfare and innovative tactics, such as feigned retreats and the use of spies, to outmaneuver enemies.
Promoted trade and cultural exchange along the Silk Road by ensuring safe passage for merchants and travelers.
Established a merit-based promotion system, rewarding loyalty and skill rather than noble birth.
Died in 1227 under mysterious circumstances, with theories ranging from illness to battle injuries.
His empire was divided among his sons and grandsons, leading to the formation of the Yuan Dynasty in China and the Ilkhanate in Persia.
Ordered the creation of the first Mongol written language, based on the Uyghur script.
His military campaigns resulted in the deaths of an estimated 40 million people, significantly reducing the global population.
Despite his ruthless reputation, he granted religious freedom to his subjects, exempting them from taxation.
His burial site remains unknown, as his followers reportedly took steps to conceal it.
Legacy includes the spread of technologies, such as gunpowder and paper, across Eurasia through Mongol conquests.

image.author: Unknown authorUnknown author

summary.fr: Ce chef militaire mongol a fondé l'Empire mongol en 1206, le plus vaste empire terrestre contigu de l'histoire. Connu pour ses stratégies innovantes et son code de lois, il a unifié les tribus nomades d'Asie et favorisé les échanges culturels le long de la Route de la soie. Son héritage a marqué durablement l'histoire politique et militaire de l'Asie.
summary.en: This Mongol military leader founded the Mongol Empire in 1206, the largest contiguous land empire in history. Renowned for his innovative tactics and legal code, he united nomadic tribes across Asia and promoted trade along the Silk Road. His legacy profoundly shaped the political and military history of Asia.

name: Marco Polo
facts: Born in 1254 in Venice, Republic of Venice, to a wealthy merchant family.
Embarked on a 24-year journey to Asia with his father and uncle in 1271 at the age of 17.
Traveled along the Silk Road, reaching China in 1275 and meeting Kublai Khan, the Mongol ruler.
Served as an envoy and official in Kublai Khan’s court for 17 years, traveling extensively across China and Southeast Asia.
Documented detailed observations of Asian cultures, economies, and technologies in the book *The Travels of Marco Polo*.
Introduced Europeans to many Asian innovations, including paper money, coal, and the postal system.
Spent time in prison in Genoa in 1298, where he dictated his travel stories to Rustichello da Pisa.
Described the wealth and grandeur of Hangzhou, calling it 'the finest and most splendid city in the world.'
Claimed to have visited regions as far as India, Sri Lanka, and the Indonesian archipelago during his travels.
Returned to Venice in 1295, bringing back jewels, spices, and exotic goods that astonished Europeans.
Fought in the naval Battle of Curzola between Venice and Genoa in 1298, where he was captured.
His book, *Il Milione*, became one of the most famous travelogues in history, inspiring later explorers like Christopher Columbus.
Died in Venice on January 8, 1324, at the age of 69, leaving behind a legacy as one of history’s greatest explorers.
Some scholars debate the accuracy of his accounts, though many of his descriptions have been verified by later research.
The Marco Polo Bridge in Beijing is named in his honor, commemorating his travels in China.

image.commons_file: Marco Polo Mosaic from Palazzo Tursi.jpg
image.author: Salviati

summary.fr: Je suis un marchand et explorateur vénitien du XIIIe siècle. J'ai voyagé pendant 24 ans en Asie, notamment en Chine, où j'ai servi le grand Kublai Khan. Mon livre *Le Devisement du monde* a fait découvrir l'Asie aux Européens et inspiré de nombreux explorateurs.
summary.en: I was a 13th-century Venetian merchant and explorer who spent 24 years traveling across Asia, including China, where I served Kublai Khan. My book *The Travels of Marco Polo* introduced Europeans to Asian cultures and inspired later explorers like Christopher Columbus.

name: Edgar Allan Poe
facts: Born on January 19, 1809, in Boston, Massachusetts, to actors David and Elizabeth Poe.
Orphaned at the age of two after both parents died, and subsequently taken in by John and Frances Allan of Richmond, Virginia.
Attended the University of Virginia in 1826 but left due to gambling debts and lack of financial support from his foster father.
Enlisted in the U.S. Army under the name Edgar A. Perry in 1827 and published his first poetry collection, *Tamerlane and Other Poems*.
Published *Al Aaraaf, Tamerlane, and Minor Poems* in 1829, which gained minor recognition.
Discharged from the Army in 1829 and later entered the U.S. Military Academy at West Point but was expelled in 1831 for neglecting duties.
Moved to Baltimore in 1831 to live with his aunt Maria Clemm and her daughter Virginia, whom he later married when she was 13 years old.
Won a literary contest in 1833 with his short story *MS. Found in a Bottle*, which brought him early fame.
Became an editor for the *Southern Literary Messenger* in 1835, where he gained a reputation for his sharp literary criticism.
Published *The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym of Nantucket* in 1838, his only complete novel.
Wrote *The Fall of the House of Usher* in 1839, a seminal work in the Gothic fiction genre.
Created the modern detective story with *The Murders in the Rue Morgue* in 1841, featuring the detective C. Auguste Dupin.
Published *The Raven* in 1845, which became an instant success and remains one of the most famous poems in American literature.
Struggled with alcoholism and financial instability throughout his life, often moving between cities like New York, Philadelphia, and Richmond.
Died under mysterious circumstances on October 7, 1849, in Baltimore, at the age of 40, after being found delirious in a tavern.

image.commons_file: Edgar Allan Poe, circa 1849, restored, squared off.jpg
image.author: Unknown authorUnknown author; Restored by Yann Forget and Adam Cuerden

summary.fr: Cet écrivain américain du XIXe siècle est célèbre pour ses poèmes sombres et ses nouvelles gothiques. Il a révolutionné la littérature avec des œuvres comme *Le Corbeau* et *La Chute de la maison Usher*. Il est aussi considéré comme l’inventeur du roman policier avec *Double assassinat dans la rue Morgue*.
summary.en: This 19th-century American writer is renowned for his dark poetry and Gothic short stories. He transformed literature with works like *The Raven* and *The Fall of the House of Usher*. He is also credited with creating the modern detective story with *The Murders in the Rue Morgue*.

name: Mark Twain
facts: Born on November 30, 1835, in Florida, Missouri, as Samuel Langhorne Clemens.
Worked as a typesetter and contributor to his brother Orion’s newspaper before becoming a full-time writer.
Adopted the pen name 'Mark Twain,' a riverboat term meaning 'two fathoms deep,' indicating safe water.
Gained fame with the short story 'The Celebrated Jumping Frog of Calaveras County,' published in 1865.
Wrote 'The Adventures of Tom Sawyer' in 1876, a novel inspired by his childhood in Hannibal, Missouri.
Published 'Adventures of Huckleberry Finn' in 1885, often called 'The Great American Novel' for its social critique.
Known for his sharp wit and satire, often targeting hypocrisy, racism, and social injustices in his works.
Traveled extensively, including a trip to the Middle East and Europe, which inspired 'The Innocents Abroad' (1869).
Filed for bankruptcy in 1894 due to poor investments, particularly in the Paige Compositor, a typesetting machine.
Recovered financially by embarking on a worldwide lecture tour, paying off debts through public speaking.
Received an honorary doctorate from the University of Oxford in 1907 for his contributions to literature.
Died on April 21, 1910, in Redding, Connecticut, leaving behind an unfinished autobiography published in 2010.
His works remain widely read and studied, with 'Huckleberry Finn' frequently appearing on banned books lists due to its language and themes.
Was a close friend of Nikola Tesla and often visited his laboratory, expressing fascination with science and invention.
Predicted his own death, noting he arrived with Halley’s Comet in 1835 and expected to depart with it in 1910.

summary.fr: Cet écrivain américain du XIXe siècle est célèbre pour ses romans comme 'Les Aventures de Tom Sawyer' et 'Les Aventures de Huckleberry Finn'. Connu sous le pseudonyme de Mark Twain, il a marqué la littérature par son humour et sa satire sociale. Ses œuvres critiquent souvent les injustices et l'hypocrisie de son époque.
summary.en: This 19th-century American author is best known for novels like The Adventures of Tom Sawyer and Adventures of Huckleberry Finn. Writing under the pen name Mark Twain, he became a literary icon for his wit and social satire. His works often challenged racism and hypocrisy in American society.

name: Celine Dion
facts: Born on March 30, 1968, in Charlemagne, Quebec, Canada, as the youngest of 14 children in a musically inclined family.
Began singing professionally at the age of 12 after being discovered by manager René Angélil, who mortgaged his home to finance her debut album.
Released her first English-language album, *Unison*, in 1990, which included the hit single *Where Does My Heart Beat Now*.
Won the Eurovision Song Contest in 1988 representing Switzerland with the song *Ne partez pas sans moi*.
Achieved global stardom with the 1997 Titanic soundtrack hit *My Heart Will Go On*, composed by James Horner, which won an Academy Award and a Golden Globe.
Has sold over 250 million records worldwide, making her one of the best-selling artists of all time.
Won five Grammy Awards, including Album of the Year for *Falling into You* in 1997 and Record of the Year for *My Heart Will Go On*.
Performed the theme song *The Power of the Dream* at the opening ceremony of the 1996 Atlanta Summer Olympics.
Headlined a residency show titled *A New Day...* at Caesars Palace in Las Vegas from 2003 to 2007, grossing over $400 million.
Took a hiatus from music in 2014 to care for her husband, René Angélil, who was battling throat cancer until his death in 2016.
Returned to the stage in 2016 with a new residency, *Celine*, at The Colosseum at Caesars Palace, which ran until 2019.
Diagnosed with Stiff-Person Syndrome, a rare neurological disorder, in late 2022, leading to the postponement of her 2023 tour dates.
Known for her powerful vocal range, spanning five octaves, and her emotive, technically precise singing style.
Has released 27 studio albums, including 16 French-language and 11 English-language albums, with *D'eux* being the best-selling French-language album of all time.
Inducted into the Canadian Music Hall of Fame in 1998 and received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame in 2004.

summary.fr: Cette artiste canadienne est l'une des chanteuses les plus célèbres au monde, avec plus de 250 millions de disques vendus. Elle a marqué la musique pop des années 1990 et 2000, notamment avec la chanson *My Heart Will Go On* du film Titanic. Originaire du Québec, elle a commencé sa carrière très jeune et a remporté cinq Grammy Awards.
summary.en: This Canadian artist is one of the most famous singers in the world, with over 250 million records sold. She defined pop music in the 1990s and 2000s, especially with the *Titanic* hit *My Heart Will Go On*. Born in Quebec, she began her career at a young age and has won five Grammy Awards.

name: Freddie Mercury
facts: Born on September 5, 1946, in Stone Town, Sultanate of Zanzibar (now Tanzania) to Parsi-Indian parents.
Moved to England at the age of 17 and studied graphic design at Ealing Art College.
Co-founded the rock band Queen in 1970 with guitarist Brian May and drummer Roger Taylor.
Wrote the iconic song 'Bohemian Rhapsody,' released in 1975, which spent nine weeks at number one on the UK charts.
Known for a four-octave vocal range and dynamic stage presence, captivating audiences worldwide.
Performed at Live Aid in 1985, a performance often regarded as one of the greatest in rock history.
Wrote and composed numerous Queen hits, including 'We Will Rock You,' 'Somebody to Love,' and 'Don't Stop Me Now.'
Released two solo albums: 'Mr. Bad Guy' in 1985 and 'Barcelona' in 1988, the latter in collaboration with opera singer Montserrat Caballé.
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2001 as a member of Queen.
Received a Brit Award for Outstanding Contribution to British Music in 1990.
Diagnosed with AIDS in 1987 but publicly confirmed the illness only one day before his death on November 24, 1991.
The Freddie Mercury Tribute Concert, held in 1992, raised millions for AIDS awareness and featured performances by major artists like David Bowie and Elton John.
Known for his flamboyant and charismatic persona, often performing in tight, colorful outfits and a signature mustache.
Posthumously awarded the Brit Award for Outstanding Contribution to Music in 1992, accepted by the remaining members of Queen.

image.commons_file: Freddie Mercury performing in New Haven, CT, November 1977.jpg
image.author: Carl Lender

summary.fr: Ce musicien britannique d'origine parsie est célèbre pour avoir été le chanteur charismatique du groupe de rock Queen. Il a marqué l'histoire de la musique avec des tubes comme Bohemian Rhapsody et son incroyable voix couvrant quatre octaves. Figure emblématique des années 1970-1980, il est aussi connu pour son engagement contre le sida.
summary.en: This British musician of Parsi-Indian descent was the iconic lead singer of the rock band Queen. He left a lasting mark on music with hits like Bohemian Rhapsody and his extraordinary four-octave vocal range. A legendary figure of the 1970s and 1980s, he also became a symbol in the fight against AIDS.

name: David Bowie

attributes.subfield: rock, glam rock, art rock, pop

facts: Born David Robert Jones on January 8, 1947, in Brixton, London.
Released the album 'The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars' in 1972, which became a defining work of glam rock.
Created the androgynous alter ego Ziggy Stardust, which became a cultural icon in the 1970s.
Known for hits like 'Space Oddity,' 'Life on Mars?,' 'Heroes,' and 'Let's Dance.'
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1996.
Won six Grammy Awards and was nominated for many more throughout his career.
Collaborated with artists such as Queen, Iggy Pop, and Brian Eno.
Released the album 'Blackstar' on January 8, 2016, just two days before his death from liver cancer.
Pioneered the use of theatricality and visual aesthetics in music, influencing countless artists across genres.
Starred in films like 'Labyrinth' (1986), 'The Man Who Fell to Earth' (1976), and 'The Prestige' (2006).
Known for his distinctive voice, which spanned a wide range of styles and octaves.
Sold over 100 million records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling music artists of all time.
Received the Brit Award for Best British Male Artist multiple times and a Lifetime Achievement Award in 1996.
Experimented with various musical styles, including electronic, soul, and avant-garde, throughout his five-decade career.
Passed away on January 10, 2016, leaving behind a legacy as one of the most innovative and influential musicians in history.

summary.fr: Ce musicien britannique, né en 1947, a marqué l’histoire du rock et de la pop avec son style unique et son alter ego Ziggy Stardust. Pionnier du glam rock dans les années 1970, il a influencé des générations d’artistes grâce à son approche théâtrale et ses tubes comme *Space Oddity* ou *Heroes*. Il est aussi connu pour ses rôles au cinéma et son impact culturel mondial.
summary.en: This British musician, born in 1947, shaped rock and pop history with his innovative style and the iconic alter ego Ziggy Stardust. A pioneer of 1970s glam rock, he influenced countless artists with his theatrical performances and hits like *Space Oddity* and *Heroes*. He also starred in films and left a lasting cultural legacy.

name: Jimi Hendrix

attributes.subfield: rock, blues, psychedelic rock, electric guitar

facts: Revolutionized electric guitar playing with innovative techniques like feedback, distortion, and wah-wah pedal usage.
Born on November 27, 1942, in Seattle, Washington, under the name Johnny Allen Hendrix before it was changed to James Marshall Hendrix.
Performed at the iconic Woodstock Festival in 1969, delivering a legendary rendition of 'The Star-Spangled Banner'.
Formed the Jimi Hendrix Experience in 1966, a band that included Noel Redding on bass and Mitch Mitchell on drums.
Released the groundbreaking debut album 'Are You Experienced' in 1967, featuring hits like 'Purple Haze' and 'Hey Joe'.
Known for playing a right-handed Fender Stratocaster guitar upside-down and restrung for left-hand playing.
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1992, over two decades after his death.
Set his guitar on fire during a performance at the Monterey Pop Festival in 1967, creating one of rock's most iconic moments.
Wrote and recorded the song 'Voodoo Child (Slight Return),' which became one of his most celebrated tracks.
Died on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27, joining the infamous '27 Club' of musicians who died at that age.
Influenced countless guitarists, including Eric Clapton, Stevie Ray Vaughan, and Prince, with his virtuosic playing style.
Posthumously released albums like 'The Cry of Love' and 'Rainbow Bridge' showcased his unfinished work and continued popularity.
Ranked as the greatest guitarist of all time by Rolling Stone magazine in 2003 and 2011.
Served in the U.S. Army as a paratrooper before pursuing music full-time, though he was discharged after breaking his ankle on a jump.

summary.fr: Ce musicien américain a marqué l'histoire du rock et du blues dans les années 1960 avec son jeu de guitare électrique révolutionnaire. Il est célèbre pour ses techniques innovantes et ses performances légendaires, comme à Woodstock en 1969. Son influence sur les générations suivantes de guitaristes reste immense.
summary.en: This American musician revolutionized rock and blues in the 1960s with his groundbreaking electric guitar techniques. He is best known for iconic performances, like his Woodstock set in 1969, and his lasting impact on guitarists worldwide. His innovative style redefined modern music.

name: Prince
aliases: The Artist Formerly Known as Prince
facts: Born on June 7, 1958, in Minneapolis, Minnesota, to a jazz singer mother and a pianist father.
Wrote his first song, "Funk Machine," at the age of seven on his father's piano.
Released his debut album, "For You," in 1978 at the age of 19, playing most of the instruments himself.
Achieved worldwide fame with the 1982 album "1999," which included hits like the title track and "Little Red Corvette."
Won an Academy Award for Best Original Song Score for the 1984 film "Purple Rain," which also starred him as "The Kid."
The "Purple Rain" album spent 24 consecutive weeks at number one on the Billboard 200 and sold over 25 million copies worldwide.
Known for his flamboyant stage presence, often performing in elaborate costumes, high heels, and makeup.
Changed his name to an unpronounceable symbol (⚥) in 1993 to protest his record label, Warner Bros., leading to the nickname "The Artist Formerly Known as Prince."
Wrote and produced songs for numerous artists, including "Nothing Compares 2 U" for Sinéad O'Connor and "Manic Monday" for The Bangles under the pseudonym "Christopher."
Inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2004, his first year of eligibility.
Performed a legendary guitar solo during the 2004 Rock and Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony, later released as "While My Guitar Gently Weeps."
Released 39 studio albums during his lifetime, with his final album, "Hit n Run Phase Two," coming out in 2015.
Known for his prolific output, with a vault of unreleased material reportedly containing thousands of songs.
Died on April 21, 2016, at the age of 57 from an accidental fentanyl overdose at his Paisley Park estate.
Posthumously won a Grammy Award for Best Album of Original Score Written for Visual Media for "Purple Rain" in 2017.

summary.fr: Ce musicien américain, né en 1958, a marqué l’histoire de la pop, du funk et du rock avec des tubes comme Purple Rain. Connu pour son talent multiforme et son style flamboyant, il a influencé des générations d’artistes. Il a remporté un Oscar pour la bande originale du film du même nom.
summary.en: This American musician, born in 1958, shaped pop, funk, and rock with hits like Purple Rain. A multi-instrumentalist and iconic performer, he left a lasting legacy in music. He won an Academy Award for the film’s soundtrack and inspired countless artists.

name: Adele
facts: Born on May 5, 1988, in Tottenham, London, England.
Released her debut album, *19*, in 2008, which included hits like 'Chasing Pavements' and 'Hometown Glory'.
Won two Grammy Awards in 2009 for Best New Artist and Best Female Pop Vocal Performance.
Her second album, *21*, released in 2011, became one of the best-selling albums of all time.
Held the record for the longest-running number-one album by a female solo artist in the UK with *21*.
Won six Grammy Awards in 2012, including Album of the Year and Record of the Year for 'Rolling in the Deep'.
Performed the theme song 'Skyfall' for the James Bond film of the same name, winning an Academy Award in 2013.
Took a three-year hiatus from music before releasing her third album, *25*, in 2015.
*25* broke first-week sales records in multiple countries, including the US and UK.
Won five Grammy Awards in 2017, including Album of the Year for *25* and Record of the Year for 'Hello'.
Known for her powerful, soulful voice and emotionally resonant lyrics about heartbreak and relationships.
Underwent vocal cord surgery in 2011 and 2012 to address issues that threatened her singing career.
Set a record for the most Grammy Awards won by a female artist in a single night (six in 2012).
Her 2016 world tour, *Adele Live 2016*, grossed over $167 million and sold out stadiums globally.
Released her fourth studio album, *30*, in 2021, which debuted at number one in multiple countries.

summary.fr: Cette artiste britannique est l'une des chanteuses les plus célèbres du 21e siècle, connue pour sa voix puissante et émouvante. Elle a marqué la musique pop et soul avec des albums comme *21* et *25*, battant des records de ventes. Son talent lui a valu de nombreux prix, dont plusieurs Grammy Awards.
summary.en: This British singer is one of the most famous artists of the 21st century, renowned for her powerful and soulful voice. She revolutionized pop and soul music with albums like *21* and *25*, breaking sales records worldwide. Her talent has earned her multiple awards, including several Grammys.

name: Morgan Freeman
facts: Born on June 1, 1937, in Memphis, Tennessee.
Began acting career on the stage, earning an Obie Award for his role in *The Gospel at Colonus* in 1984.
Gained widespread recognition for his role as Fast Black in the 1987 film *Street Smart*, earning an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actor.
Starred as Hoke Colburn in *Driving Miss Daisy* (1989), a role that earned him a Golden Globe nomination.
Played Detective Somerset in *Se7en* (1995), a critically acclaimed psychological thriller directed by David Fincher.
Portrayed Red, the wise and compassionate inmate, in *The Shawshank Redemption* (1994), a role that became iconic.
Voiced the character of God in the 2003 comedy *Bruce Almighty* and its sequel, *Evan Almighty* (2007).
Won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor for his role as Eddie Scrap-Iron Dupris in *Million Dollar Baby* (2004).
Narrated the documentary series *Through the Wormhole* (2010–2017), exploring scientific and philosophical questions.
Founded Revelations Entertainment, a film production company, in 1996 with business partner Lori McCreary.
Received the Kennedy Center Honor in 2008 for his contributions to American culture through the performing arts.
Suffered a serious car accident in 2008, which resulted in injuries to his left hand and arm, requiring surgery.
Known for his distinctive deep, calming voice, which has led to numerous narration and voiceover roles.
Starred in *Invictus* (2009) as Nelson Mandela, earning an Academy Award nomination for Best Actor.
Received the Screen Actors Guild Life Achievement Award in 2018 for his outstanding career in film and television.

image.commons_file: Academy Award-winning actor Morgan Freeman narrates for the opening ceremony (26904746425) (cropped) 3.jpg
image.author: DoD News Features

summary.fr: Cet acteur américain, né en 1937, est célèbre pour sa voix grave et son talent à incarner des personnages sages et charismatiques. Il a marqué le cinéma avec des rôles comme celui de Red dans *Les Évadés* ou de Scrap-Iron dans *Million Dollar Baby*, pour lequel il a remporté un Oscar. Son parcours inclut aussi le théâtre et la narration de documentaires scientifiques.
summary.en: This American actor, born in 1937, is renowned for his deep voice and ability to portray wise and compelling characters. He left a lasting impact on cinema with roles like Red in *The Shawshank Redemption* and Scrap-Iron in *Million Dollar Baby*, which earned him an Oscar. His career spans theater, film, and narrating science documentaries.

name: Meryl Streep
facts: Holds the record for the most Academy Award nominations of any actor, with a total of 21.
Won three Oscars: Best Supporting Actress for *Kramer vs. Kramer* (1979) and Best Actress for *Sophie’s Choice* (1982) and *The Iron Lady* (2011).
Graduated from Vassar College and later earned an MFA from the Yale School of Drama.
Made her Broadway debut in 1975 in *Trelawny of the 'Wells'* before transitioning to film.
Delivered a critically acclaimed performance as Joanna Kramer in *Kramer vs. Kramer*, earning her first Oscar win.
Played Holocaust survivor Sophie Zawistowski in *Sophie’s Choice*, a role widely regarded as one of the greatest in cinema history.
Portrayed British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher in *The Iron Lady*, earning her third Academy Award.
Known for her mastery of accents, including Polish in *Sophie’s Choice*, Danish in *Out of Africa*, and Australian in *A Cry in the Dark*.
Received the AFI Life Achievement Award in 2004 for her contributions to film.
Starred in *The Devil Wears Prada* (2006) as Miranda Priestly, a role that became iconic in pop culture.
Has collaborated with director Steven Spielberg multiple times, including in *The Post* (2017) and *Lincoln* (2012).
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Barack Obama in 2014.
Founded the Silver Mountain Foundation for the Arts, which supports emerging artists and filmmakers.
Has been married to sculptor Don Gummer since 1978 and has four children.
Delivered a memorable commencement speech at Barnard College in 2010, emphasizing the importance of empathy and resilience.

image.commons_file: Meryl Streep interview at Festival de Cannes 2024 (cropped 2).jpg
image.author: Kevin Payravi

summary.fr: Cette actrice américaine, née en 1949, est l'une des plus célébrées du cinéma. Elle détient le record de nominations aux Oscars avec 21 mentions et a remporté trois statuettes pour ses rôles marquants. Connue pour sa polyvalence et ses accents impeccables, elle a marqué l'histoire du cinéma moderne.
summary.en: This American actress, born in 1949, is one of the most acclaimed in cinema history. She holds the record for the most Oscar nominations (21) and has won three Academy Awards for unforgettable performances. Renowned for her versatility and mastery of accents, she remains a legend in modern film.

name: Alfred Hitchcock
facts: Born on August 13, 1899, in Leytonstone, London, England.
Directed over 50 feature films during a career spanning six decades.
Known for pioneering techniques in the suspense and psychological thriller genres.
Directed the first British sound film, *Blackmail*, in 1929.
Created the iconic film *Psycho* in 1960, which revolutionized horror cinema with its famous shower scene.
Introduced the
plot device, a term for an object or goal that drives the plot but holds little inherent meaning.
Hosted and produced the television anthology series *Alfred Hitchcock Presents* from 1955 to 1965.
Received five Academy Award nominations for Best Director but never won a competitive Oscar.
Awarded the Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award in 1968 for lifetime achievement in filmmaking.
Famous for making cameo appearances in nearly all of his films, often in humorous or subtle roles.
Directed *Vertigo* (1958), which was later ranked as the greatest film of all time by *Sight & Sound* in 2012.
Known for his meticulous storyboarding of scenes before filming, earning him the nickname 'The Master of Suspense.'
Knighted by Queen Elizabeth II in 1980, shortly before his death on April 29 of that year.
Influenced generations of filmmakers, including Brian De Palma, Martin Scorsese, and Steven Spielberg.

summary.fr: Ce réalisateur britannique, né en 1899, est une figure majeure du cinéma du XXe siècle. Il a révolutionné les genres du suspense et du thriller psychologique avec des films comme *Psychose* et *Vertigo*. Connu pour ses caméos et ses techniques innovantes, il a marqué l’histoire du 7e art malgré ses cinq nominations aux Oscars sans victoire.
summary.en: This British filmmaker, born in 1899, is a key figure in 20th-century cinema. He pioneered suspense and psychological thrillers with iconic films like *Psycho* and *Vertigo*. Famous for his cameos and innovative techniques, he shaped film history despite never winning a competitive Oscar.

name: Steven Spielberg
facts: Born on December 18, 1946, in Cincinnati, Ohio, to a Jewish family.
