Directed his first independent film, *Firelight*, at the age of 16, which was later screened at a local theater.
Attended California State University, Long Beach, but dropped out to pursue filmmaking full-time.
Directed the 1971 TV movie *Duel*, which gained critical acclaim and launched his career in Hollywood.
Released *Jaws* in 1975, which became the highest-grossing film of its time and established the summer blockbuster trend.
Co-founded Amblin Entertainment in 1981, a production company responsible for many iconic films.
Directed *E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial* in 1982, which became one of the most beloved films in cinema history.
Won his first Academy Award for Best Director for *Schindler’s List* in 1994, a film also awarded Best Picture.
Founded DreamWorks SKG in 1994 alongside Jeffrey Katzenberg and David Geffen, a major film studio.
Directed *Saving Private Ryan* in 1998, renowned for its realistic portrayal of World War II and its opening D-Day sequence.
Received the Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award in 1987 for his consistent high-quality filmmaking.
Has been nominated for the Academy Award for Best Director nine times, winning twice (*Schindler’s List* and *Saving Private Ryan*).
Directed *Lincoln* in 2012, a historical drama starring Daniel Day-Lewis, who won an Oscar for his portrayal of Abraham Lincoln.
Known for his long-standing collaboration with composer John Williams, who scored many of his most famous films.
Inducted into the Science Fiction Hall of Fame in 2005 for his contributions to the genre through films like *Close Encounters of the Third Kind* and *A.I. Artificial Intelligence*.

summary.fr: Ce réalisateur américain est l'un des plus influents du cinéma moderne. Né en 1946, il a révolutionné le cinéma avec des films comme *Les Dents de la mer* et *E.T. l'extra-terrestre*. Il a remporté deux Oscars pour ses œuvres marquantes sur l'Histoire, comme *La Liste de Schindler*.
summary.en: This American filmmaker is one of the most influential directors in modern cinema. Born in 1946, he revolutionized the industry with blockbusters like *Jaws* and *E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial*. He has won two Academy Awards for impactful historical films, including *Schindler’s List*.

name: Quentin Tarantino
facts: Born on March 27, 1963, in Knoxville, Tennessee, to a nurse and an aspiring musician.
Dropped out of Narbonne High School in Harbor City, California, at the age of 15 to pursue acting.
Worked as a clerk at Video Archives, a video rental store in Manhattan Beach, where he honed his film knowledge.
Wrote the screenplay for *True Romance* (1993), directed by Tony Scott, while working at the video store.
Directed his first feature film, *Reservoir Dogs*, in 1992, which became a cult classic and launched his career.
Won the Palme d'Or at the 1994 Cannes Film Festival for *Pulp Fiction*, which also earned seven Oscar nominations.
Known for his nonlinear storytelling, stylized violence, and extensive use of pop culture references in films.
Created the character of Jules Winnfield in *Pulp Fiction*, delivering the iconic Ezekiel 25:17 monologue.
Directed *Kill Bill: Volume 1* (2003) and *Kill Bill: Volume 2* (2004), a two-part revenge epic starring Uma Thurman.
Wrote and directed *Inglourious Basterds* (2009), a alternate-history World War II film that earned eight Oscar nominations.
Received two Academy Awards for Best Original Screenplay, for *Pulp Fiction* (1994) and *Django Unchained* (2012).
Announced in 2015 that he would retire from filmmaking after directing his tenth film, though this plan has wavered.
Directed *Once Upon a Time in Hollywood* (2019), a film set in 1969 Los Angeles, which earned 10 Oscar nominations.
Known for casting actors in multiple films, including Samuel L. Jackson, Uma Thurman, and Christoph Waltz.
Published his first novel, *Once Upon a Time in Hollywood*, in 2020, a novelization of his 2019 film.

summary.fr: Ce réalisateur américain est célèbre pour ses films aux récits non linéaires et ses scènes de violence stylisées. Il a remporté la Palme d'Or à Cannes en 1994 pour *Pulp Fiction* et deux Oscars pour ses scénarios originaux. Son œuvre, marquée par des références à la culture populaire, a marqué le cinéma des années 1990 et 2000.
summary.en: This American filmmaker is known for his nonlinear storytelling and stylized violence in movies. He won the Palme d'Or at Cannes in 1994 for *Pulp Fiction* and two Oscars for Best Original Screenplay. His work, filled with pop culture references, defined 1990s and 2000s cinema.

name: Roger Federer
facts: Holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles (20) until being surpassed by Rafael Nadal and Novak Djokovic.
Spent a record 310 weeks as the world No. 1 in the ATP rankings, including 237 consecutive weeks.
Won a record 8 Wimbledon men's singles titles, the most by any player in history.
Claimed 6 Australian Open, 5 US Open, and 1 French Open singles titles during his career.
Won the ATP Finals a record 6 times, showcasing dominance in the season-ending tournament.
Earned over $130 million in prize money, making him one of the highest-earning athletes in history.
Known for his elegant playing style, exceptional footwork, and precise shot-making, particularly his one-handed backhand.
Won the Olympic gold medal in men's doubles at the 2008 Beijing Games alongside Stan Wawrinka.
Founded the Roger Federer Foundation in 2003, which supports educational projects for children in southern Africa and Switzerland.
Retired from professional tennis in September 2022 after a final doubles match at the Laver Cup.
Holds the record for the most consecutive Grand Slam semifinals (23) and finals (10) appearances.
Won the Laureus World Sportsman of the Year award a record 5 times (2005–2008, 2018).
Born on August 8, 1981, in Basel, Switzerland, to a Swiss father and South African mother.
Turned professional in 1998 and won his first Grand Slam title at Wimbledon in 2003 at age 21.
Married former tennis player Mirka Vavrinec in 2009, and the couple has four children: twin daughters and twin sons.

summary.fr: Ce sportif suisse est l'un des plus grands joueurs de tennis de l'histoire. Il a dominé les courts pendant plus de deux décennies, remportant 20 titres du Grand Chelem et passant 310 semaines en tant que numéro 1 mondial. Connu pour son élégance et son jeu précis, il a marqué le sport par son talent et son fair-play.
summary.en: This Swiss athlete is one of the greatest tennis players in history. He dominated the sport for over two decades, winning 20 Grand Slam titles and spending 310 weeks as world No. 1. Renowned for his elegant style and precision, he left a lasting legacy in tennis with his skill and sportsmanship.

name: Rafael Nadal
facts: Born on June 3, 1986, in Manacor, Mallorca, Spain.
Holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in history with 22, tied with Novak Djokovic as of 2023.
Won a record 14 French Open titles, the most by any player at a single Grand Slam event.
Achieved the Career Grand Slam by winning all four major titles: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open.
Won two Olympic gold medals, one in singles at the 2008 Beijing Olympics and one in doubles at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
Known for his unparalleled dominance on clay courts, earning the nickname 'The King of Clay'.
Became the youngest player in the Open Era to complete the Career Grand Slam at the age of 24 in 2010.
Holds the record for the longest winning streak on a single surface, with 81 consecutive wins on clay from 2005 to 2007.
Founded the Rafa Nadal Academy in 2016, a high-performance tennis training center in Mallorca, Spain.
Has a long-standing rivalry with Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic, often considered one of the greatest in tennis history.
Won the 2008 Wimbledon final against Roger Federer in a match widely regarded as one of the greatest in tennis history.
Suffers from chronic knee injuries, which have frequently impacted his career and tournament participation.
Known for his intense on-court demeanor, including his meticulous pre-point routines and habit of biting trophies.
Published an autobiography titled 'Rafa' in 2011, detailing his life and career up to that point.
Actively involved in charitable work through the Rafa Nadal Foundation, focusing on education and social integration for disadvantaged youth.

summary.fr: Ce sportif espagnol est l'un des plus grands joueurs de tennis de l'histoire. Surnommé le 'Roi de la Terre Battue', il détient le record de 14 titres à Roland-Garros. Il a remporté 22 titres du Grand Chelem, un exploit rare dans ce sport.
summary.en: This Spanish athlete is one of the greatest tennis players in history. Known as the 'King of Clay', he holds the record for 14 French Open titles. He has won 22 Grand Slam titles, a remarkable achievement in the sport.

name: Mike Tyson
aliases: The Baddest Man on the Planet
facts: Became the youngest heavyweight boxing champion in history at the age of 20 years and 4 months in 1986.
Won his first 19 professional fights by knockout, 12 of them in the first round.
Held the WBC, WBA, and IBF heavyweight titles simultaneously in the late 1980s.
Defended his undisputed heavyweight title nine times before losing to Buster Douglas in 1990.
Was trained by legendary boxing trainer Cus D'Amato, who became his legal guardian after Tyson's mother passed away.
Served three years in prison from 1992 to 1995 after being convicted of rape charges.
Returned to boxing in 1995 and regained the WBC and WBA titles in 1996.
Fought a famous bout against Evander Holyfield in 1997, where he was disqualified for biting Holyfield's ear.
Declared bankruptcy in 2003 despite earning over $300 million during his boxing career.
Inducted into the International Boxing Hall of Fame in 2011.
Starred in the 2009 film 'The Hangover,' showcasing his comedic side and earning critical acclaim.
Launched a one-man show titled 'Mike Tyson: Undisputed Truth' on Broadway in 2012, directed by Spike Lee.
Became a cannabis entrepreneur, launching Tyson Ranch, a cannabis resort and production facility in California in 2019.
Fought in an exhibition match against Roy Jones Jr. in 2020, which ended in a draw.
Published his memoir 'Undisputed Truth' in 2013, detailing his rise, fall, and redemption in and out of the ring.

image.commons_file: Mike Tyson Photo Op GalaxyCon Austin 2023.jpg
image.author: Super Festivals

summary.fr: Ce boxeur américain est devenu le plus jeune champion du monde des poids lourds à seulement 20 ans en 1986. Surnommé 'Iron Mike', il a marqué l'histoire de la boxe par sa puissance et ses victoires impressionnantes dans les années 1980 et 1990. Connu aussi pour sa vie tumultueuse, il a inspiré autant par ses exploits sportifs que par ses défis personnels.
summary.en: This American boxer became the youngest heavyweight champion in history at age 20 in 1986. Nicknamed 'Iron Mike,' he dominated the sport in the 1980s and 1990s with his explosive power and record-breaking knockouts. Beyond boxing, he is known for his controversial life and later career in entertainment.

name: Neymar
facts: Born on February 5, 1992, in Mogi das Cruzes, São Paulo, Brazil.
Began playing football at a young age and joined Santos FC's youth academy at 11 years old.
Made a professional debut for Santos FC at the age of 17 in 2009.
Helped Santos FC win the 2011 Copa Libertadores, scoring six goals in the tournament.
Won the FIFA Puskás Award in 2011 for scoring a stunning solo goal against Flamengo.
Signed with FC Barcelona in 2013, forming a formidable attacking trio with Lionel Messi and Luis Suárez known as 'MSN'.
Won the treble (La Liga, Copa del Rey, and UEFA Champions League) with Barcelona in the 2014-2015 season.
Became the most expensive football player in history when Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) activated his €222 million release clause in 2017.
Has won multiple Ligue 1 titles with PSG, including five consecutive championships from 2018 to 2022.
Scored over 100 goals for the Brazilian national team, becoming one of the country's top scorers of all time.
Played a key role in Brazil's victory at the 2013 FIFA Confederations Cup, winning the Golden Ball as the tournament's best player.
Suffered a severe injury during the 2014 FIFA World Cup quarterfinals, which ruled Brazil out of the tournament and led to a 7-1 defeat to Germany.
Captained Brazil to a gold medal at the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro, scoring the winning penalty in the final against Germany.
Known for his dribbling skills, creativity, and flair, often drawing comparisons to Brazilian football legends like Pelé and Ronaldinho.

Has been involved in numerous philanthropic efforts, including donating a significant portion of his earnings to charity and founding the Instituto Projeto Neymar Jr. to support underprivileged children in Brazil.

image.commons_file: Neymar Jr. with Al Hilal, 3 October 2023 - 04 (cropped).jpg
image.author: مقداد مددی

summary.fr: Ce footballeur brésilien est l'un des attaquants les plus talentueux de sa génération. Né en 1992, il a marqué l'histoire du football en jouant pour des clubs comme le FC Barcelone et le PSG. Il est aussi une star de l'équipe nationale du Brésil, avec plus de 100 buts à son actif.
summary.en: This Brazilian footballer is one of the most skilled forwards of his generation. Born in 1992, he made history playing for clubs like FC Barcelona and PSG. He is also a key player for Brazil’s national team, scoring over 100 goals for his country.

name: Kylian Mbappe
facts: Became the youngest French goal scorer in a World Cup at age 18 during the 2018 FIFA World Cup.
Won the FIFA World Cup with the French national team in 2018, scoring four goals in the tournament.
Signed with Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) in 2017 for a reported fee of €180 million, making him the most expensive teenager in football history.
Scored a hat-trick in the 2018 FIFA World Cup final against Croatia, becoming the second teenager to do so after Pelé.
Won the Ligue 1 Player of the Year award in 2019 and 2021.
Led PSG to their first-ever UEFA Champions League final in 2020, finishing as the tournament's top scorer with 12 goals.
Began his professional career at AS Monaco, making his debut at age 16 in 2015.
Became the youngest player to score 100 Ligue 1 goals, achieving the milestone at age 22 in 2021.
Won the Kopa Trophy in 2018, awarded to the best player under 21 in the world.
Scored 4 goals in a single match against Lyon in Ligue 1 in October 2018, a record for PSG.
Donated his entire earnings from the 2018 World Cup (approximately €400,000) to a charity supporting disabled children.
Named in the FIFA FIFPro World XI in 2018, 2019, and 2021.
Holds the record for the most goals scored in a single UEFA Champions League season by a teenager (6 goals in 2016-17).
Won the Golden Boot as the top scorer in the 2022 FIFA World Cup with 8 goals, despite France finishing as runners-up.
Known for his exceptional speed, often clocked at over 36 km/h (22 mph) during matches.

image.commons_file: 2019-07-17 SG Dynamo Dresden vs. Paris Saint-Germain by Sandro Halank–129 (cropped).jpg
image.author: Carlo Bruil Fotografie

summary.fr: Ce footballeur français est l'un des meilleurs attaquants au monde. Il a marqué l'histoire en devenant le plus jeune buteur français en Coupe du Monde à 18 ans en 2018 et a remporté le trophée avec l'équipe de France. Connu pour sa vitesse et son talent, il joue actuellement pour le Paris Saint-Germain et a battu plusieurs records en Ligue 1.
summary.en: This French footballer is one of the world's top strikers. He made history as France's youngest World Cup goal scorer at 18 in 2018 and won the tournament with his national team. Known for his speed and skill, he currently plays for Paris Saint-Germain and has broken multiple records in Ligue 1.

name: LeBron James
facts: Born on December 30, 1984, in Akron, Ohio, to a 16-year-old single mother.
Selected as the first overall pick in the 2003 NBA Draft by the Cleveland Cavaliers at age 18.
Won NBA Rookie of the Year in 2004 after averaging 20.9 points, 5.5 rebounds, and 5.9 assists per game.
Became the youngest player in NBA history to score 40 points in a game at age 18 years and 333 days.
Led the Cleveland Cavaliers to their first NBA Finals appearance in 2007, though they were swept by the San Antonio Spurs.
Won two NBA championships with the Miami Heat in 2012 and 2013, earning Finals MVP both times.
Returned to the Cleveland Cavaliers in 2014 and led them to an NBA championship in 2016, ending a 52-year title drought for the city.
Delivered a historic 41-point performance in Game 5 of the 2016 NBA Finals, helping the Cavaliers overcome a 3-1 deficit against the Golden State Warriors.
Signed with the Los Angeles Lakers in 2018 and won his fourth NBA championship in 2020, earning his fourth Finals MVP.
Became the NBA's all-time leading scorer on February 7, 2023, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's record of 38,387 points.
Has been selected to the NBA All-Star team 19 times as of 2023, the most in league history.
Founded the I PROMISE School in Akron, Ohio, in 2018, providing education and support to at-risk children.
Won two Olympic gold medals with the U.S. men's basketball team in 2008 and 2012, and a bronze medal in 2004.
Has been named NBA MVP four times (2009, 2010, 2012, 2013), tying him for the second-most in history.
Starred in the 2021 film *Space Jam: A New Legacy*, a sequel to the 1996 original, playing a fictionalized version of himself.

summary.fr: Ce joueur américain est une légende du basketball moderne. Il a marqué l'histoire de la NBA en devenant le meilleur marqueur de tous les temps et a remporté quatre championnats. Connu pour son engagement social, il a aussi fondé une école pour aider les enfants défavorisés.
summary.en: This American athlete is a modern basketball legend. He made NBA history by becoming the all-time leading scorer and has won four championships. Known for his social commitment, he also founded a school to support at-risk children.

name: Tiger Woods
facts: Became the youngest golfer to win the Masters Tournament at age 21 in 1997, setting a record with a 12-stroke victory.
Holds the record for the most consecutive weeks (281) as the world's number one golfer in the Official World Golf Ranking.
Won 15 major championships, second only to Jack Nicklaus's 18, with victories spanning from 1997 to 2019.
Completed the
Career Grand Slam by age 24, winning all four major championships (Masters, U.S. Open, The Open Championship, PGA Championship) at least once.
Won the PGA Player of the Year award a record 11 times between 1997 and 2013.
Earned over $120 million in prize money on the PGA Tour, making him the highest-earning golfer in history as of 2023.
Founded the Tiger Woods Foundation in 1996, which focuses on education and youth development programs.
Hosts the annual Hero World Challenge, a charity golf tournament benefiting his foundation.
Underwent multiple back surgeries, including a spinal fusion in 2017, which led to a highly publicized comeback in 2018 and 2019.
Won the 2019 Masters Tournament after an 11-year major championship drought, marking one of the most celebrated comebacks in sports history.
Inducted into the California Hall of Fame in 2007 and awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 2019 by President Donald Trump.
Known for popularizing golf among younger and more diverse audiences, significantly increasing the sport's global viewership and participation.
Co-wrote a bestselling book, 'How I Play Golf,' published in 2001, offering insights into his techniques and career.
Faced a highly publicized personal scandal in 2009 involving infidelity, which led to a temporary hiatus from golf and a divorce from his then-wife, Elin Nordegren.

image.author: The White House from Washington, DC

summary.fr: Ce sportif américain est l'un des plus grands golfeurs de l'histoire. Il a remporté 15 tournois majeurs et détenu le record de semaines en tant que numéro un mondial. Connu pour son talent précoce et son retour triomphal après des blessures, il a marqué le golf moderne.
summary.en: This American athlete is one of the greatest golfers in history. He won 15 major championships and held the record for most weeks as the world's number one golfer. Famous for his early success and remarkable comeback after injuries, he shaped modern golf.

name: Simone Biles
facts: Won a combined total of 32 Olympic and World Championship medals, making her the most decorated American gymnast.
Became the first woman to win five World all-around titles (2013, 2014, 2015, 2018, 2019).
Executed the first Yurchenko double pike in competition by a woman, a vault so difficult it was named after her.
Won four gold medals at the 2016 Rio Olympics in the team, all-around, vault, and floor exercise events.
Took a two-year break from competition after the 2016 Olympics to focus on mental health and personal well-being.
Returned to competition in 2018 and won six medals at the World Championships, including four golds.
Became the first gymnast to land a triple-double (two flips with three twists) in competition during her floor routine in 2019.
Withdrew from several events at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics due to the
twisties", a mental block affecting spatial awareness during routines.
Advocated for mental health awareness and received widespread support for prioritizing well-being over competition.
Won a silver medal in the team event and a bronze on the balance beam at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics.
Holds the record for the most World Championship gold medals (19) in gymnastics history.
Was adopted by her grandparents as a child and grew up in Spring, Texas.
Began gymnastics at the age of six during a day-care field trip and was homeschooled to accommodate her training schedule.
Signed with the sportswear brand Nike and has been a vocal advocate for athlete empowerment and social justice issues.

summary.fr: Cette gymnaste américaine est considérée comme l'une des plus grandes de l'histoire. Elle a remporté un total de 32 médailles aux Jeux Olympiques et aux Championnats du Monde, un record pour une gymnaste américaine. Connue pour ses figures ultra-difficiles, elle a aussi sensibilisé le public à l'importance de la santé mentale dans le sport.
summary.en: This American gymnast is one of the most decorated in history, with 32 Olympic and World Championship medals. She revolutionized artistic gymnastics with groundbreaking skills and became a vocal advocate for mental health awareness in sports. Her dominance and resilience have made her a global icon.

name: Thomas Edison
facts: Holds 1,093 US patents in his name, as well as many patents in the UK, France, and Germany.
Invented the phonograph in 1877, the first device to record and reproduce sound.
Developed the first commercially practical incandescent light bulb in 1879, which lasted 13.5 hours in its initial test.
Established the world's first industrial research laboratory in Menlo Park, New Jersey, in 1876.
Created the first power station in 1882, providing electricity to parts of New York City.
Improved the telegraph system, leading to the quadruplex telegraph in 1874, which could send four messages simultaneously.
Founded the Edison Electric Light Company in 1878, which later became General Electric.
Developed the kinetoscope in 1891, an early motion picture exhibition device.
Was nearly deaf, which he claimed helped him concentrate on his work without distractions.
Worked up to 20 hours a day and slept only a few hours, famously saying, 'Genius is one percent inspiration and ninety-nine percent perspiration.'
Lost the so-called 'War of the Currents' to Nikola Tesla and George Westinghouse, who promoted alternating current (AC) over his direct current (DC) system.
Received the Congressional Gold Medal in 1928 for his contributions to the development of the nation.
Filed his first patent at the age of 21 for an electric vote recorder in 1868.
Married twice and had six children, three with each wife.
Died on October 18, 1931, in West Orange, New Jersey, at the age of 84.

image.author: Louis Bachrach, Bachrach Studios, restored by Michel Vuijlsteke

summary.fr: Cet inventeur américain du XIXe siècle a marqué l'histoire avec plus de 1000 brevets. Il a révolutionné la vie quotidienne en créant l'ampoule électrique et le phonographe, tout en fondant le premier laboratoire de recherche industriel. Son travail acharné a changé la façon dont le monde utilise l'énergie et la technologie.
summary.en: This 19th-century American inventor holds over 1,000 patents and transformed modern life with the light bulb and phonograph. He pioneered industrial research and electricity distribution, shaping how we use technology today. His relentless work ethic made him a symbol of innovation in science and engineering.

name: Alexander Graham Bell
facts: Invented the first practical telephone in 1876, revolutionizing global communication.
Was awarded the first US patent for the invention of the telephone, Patent No. 174,465.
Co-founded the American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T) in 1885.
Developed the photophone in 1880, a device that transmitted sound on a beam of light, a precursor to fiber-optic communication.
Conducted early experiments in aeronautics, including work on the tetrahedral kite in the 1890s.
Was born on March 3, 1847, in Edinburgh, Scotland, into a family with a strong background in speech and elocution.
Moved to Canada in 1870 and later to the United States, where he became a naturalized citizen in 1882.
Worked extensively with the deaf community, influenced by his mother and wife, who were both hearing-impaired.
Established the Volta Laboratory in Washington, D.C., in 1880, focusing on research in sound and recording.
Invented the metal detector in 1881 in an attempt to locate a bullet lodged in President James Garfield’s body.
Received the French Volta Prize in 1880, which included 50,000 francs, funding further research.
Published over 100 scientific papers and held 18 patents in his name, along with 12 shared with collaborators.
Founded the journal *Science* in 1880, which later became the official publication of the American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS).
Died on August 2, 1922, in Beinn Bhreagh, Nova Scotia, Canada, at the age of 75.

summary.fr: Ce scientifique écossais est célèbre pour avoir inventé le premier téléphone pratique en 1876, changeant à jamais la communication mondiale. Pionnier des télécommunications, il a aussi travaillé avec la communauté sourde et contribué à d’autres inventions comme le photophone. Né en 1847, il a marqué l’histoire des sciences et des technologies.
summary.en: This Scottish scientist is best known for inventing the first practical telephone in 1876, transforming global communication forever. A pioneer in telecommunications, he also worked with the deaf community and contributed to inventions like the photophone. Born in 1847, he left a lasting impact on science and technology.

name: Sigmund Freud
facts: Founded psychoanalysis, a clinical method for treating psychopathology through dialogue between a patient and a psychoanalyst.
Developed the theory of the unconscious mind, which posits that much of human behavior is influenced by unconscious thoughts and desires.
Introduced the concept of defense mechanisms, such as repression, which protect the conscious mind from anxiety-provoking thoughts.
Proposed the structural model of the psyche, dividing it into the id, ego, and superego.
Published 'The Interpretation of Dreams' in 1899, a seminal work that explores the significance of dreams in understanding the unconscious.
Formulated the Oedipus complex, a theory suggesting that children experience unconscious sexual desires for the parent of the opposite sex.
Coined the term 'libido' to describe the energy of the sexual drive as a component of the life instinct.
Developed the technique of free association, encouraging patients to verbalize thoughts without censorship to uncover unconscious conflicts.
Wrote 'Beyond the Pleasure Principle' in 1920, introducing the concept of the death drive (Thanatos) alongside the life drive (Eros).
Fled Austria in 1938 to escape Nazi persecution, settling in London where he spent his final year.
Diagnosed with oral cancer in 1923 due to his lifelong cigar smoking habit, undergoing over 30 surgeries.
Received the Goethe Prize in 1930 for his contributions to psychology and German literary culture.
Collaborated with Josef Breuer on the case of Anna O., which laid the groundwork for psychoanalytic theory.
Published 'Civilization and Its Discontents' in 1930, exploring the tension between individual desires and societal constraints.
Died on September 23, 1939, in London, after requesting a lethal dose of morphine from his physician due to unbearable pain from cancer.

summary.fr: Ce scientifique autrichien du début du XXe siècle est le père de la psychanalyse, une méthode pour explorer l'inconscient. Il a révolutionné la psychologie en introduisant des concepts comme le ça, le moi et le surmoi, ou encore le complexe d'Œdipe. Ses travaux sur les rêves et les mécanismes de défense ont marqué durablement la science et la culture.
summary.en: This Austrian scientist from the early 20th century founded psychoanalysis, a method to explore the unconscious mind. He transformed psychology with concepts like the id, ego, and superego, as well as the Oedipus complex. His theories on dreams and defense mechanisms left a lasting impact on science and culture.

name: Louis Pasteur
facts: Born on December 27, 1822, in Dole, France, into a family of tanners.
Developed the germ theory of disease, revolutionizing the understanding of infections and public health.
Discovered the process of pasteurization in 1864, which kills harmful microbes in food and beverages like milk and wine.
Proved that microorganisms cause fermentation and spoilage, debunking the theory of spontaneous generation.
Created the first vaccines for rabies and anthrax, saving countless lives and laying the foundation for immunology.
Successfully treated a 9-year-old boy, Joseph Meister, with an experimental rabies vaccine in 1885, marking a medical breakthrough.
Founded the Pasteur Institute in Paris in 1887, which remains a leading research center for infectious diseases.
Earned a doctorate in sciences in 1847 with a thesis on crystallography, contributing to the field of chemistry.
Suffered a stroke in 1868 that left him partially paralyzed but continued his scientific work with determination.
Developed a method to attenuate (weaken) pathogens, enabling the creation of safe and effective vaccines.
Awarded the Leeuwenhoek Medal in 1895, one of the highest honors in microbiology, for his contributions to the field.
Died on September 28, 1895, in Marnes-la-Coquette, France, and was given a state funeral in recognition of his achievements.
His work on chirality in molecules laid the groundwork for modern stereochemistry.
Advocated for strict hygiene practices in hospitals, significantly reducing post-surgical infections and mortality rates.

image.commons_file: Louis Pasteur (1822 - 1895), microbiologist and chemist Wellcome V0026980.jpg
image.author: Paul Nadar

summary.fr: Ce scientifique français du XIXe siècle a révolutionné la médecine en découvrant que les microbes causent les maladies. Il a inventé la pasteurisation pour conserver les aliments et créé les premiers vaccins contre la rage et le charbon. Ses travaux ont sauvé des millions de vies et fondé l’immunologie moderne.
summary.en: This 19th-century French scientist transformed medicine by proving germs cause disease. He invented pasteurization to preserve food and developed the first vaccines for rabies and anthrax. His discoveries saved countless lives and laid the foundation for modern immunology.

name: Ada Lovelace
facts: Was born on December 10, 1815, in London, England, as Augusta Ada Byron.
Is widely regarded as the world's first computer programmer due to her work on Charles Babbage's early mechanical general-purpose computer, the Analytical Engine.
Wrote the first algorithm intended to be processed by a machine, making her contributions foundational to modern computer science.
Collaborated closely with Charles Babbage, often referred to as the 'father of the computer.'
Translated an article by Italian engineer Luigi Menabrea on the Analytical Engine, adding extensive notes that tripled the length of the original text.
Predicted that computers could be used for more than just calculations, envisioning applications in music and graphics.
Was the only legitimate child of the poet Lord Byron and his wife, Anne Isabella Byron (née Milbanke).
Never met her famous father, who left England when she was just one month old and died when she was eight years old.
Was educated privately in mathematics and science, which was unusual for women of her time.
Married William King-Noel, the Earl of Lovelace, in 1835, becoming the Countess of Lovelace.
Had three children: Byron, Anne Isabella, and Ralph Gordon.
Died of uterine cancer on November 27, 1852, at the age of 36.
Her notes on the Analytical Engine were published in 1843 under the initials 'A.A.L.' to conceal her identity as a woman.
The programming language Ada, created on behalf of the United States Department of Defense, was named in her honor in 1980.
Is celebrated annually on Ada Lovelace Day, an international event highlighting the achievements of women in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM).

image.author: Margaret Sarah Carpenter

summary.fr: Cette scientifique britannique du XIXe siècle est considérée comme la première programmeuse informatique au monde. Elle a travaillé sur la machine analytique de Charles Babbage et a écrit le premier algorithme destiné à être exécuté par une machine. Ses contributions ont jeté les bases de l'informatique moderne.
summary.en: This 19th-century British mathematician is widely regarded as the world's first computer programmer. She worked on Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine and wrote the first algorithm meant to be processed by a machine. Her work laid the foundation for modern computer science.

name: Alan Turing
facts: Born on June 23, 1912, in Maida Vale, London, England.
Developed the concept of the Turing machine in 1936, a foundational model for modern computers.
Studied at King's College, Cambridge, where he was elected a Fellow in 1935.
Played a crucial role in breaking the German Enigma code during World War II at Bletchley Park.
Invented the Bombe, an electromechanical device used to decrypt Enigma-encrypted messages.
Published the seminal paper 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' in 1950, introducing the Turing Test for artificial intelligence.
Worked on early computer designs, including the Automatic Computing Engine (ACE) at the National Physical Laboratory.
Proposed the chemical theory of morphogenesis in 1952, explaining pattern formation in biological organisms.
Arrested in 1952 for homosexual acts, which were then illegal in the UK, and underwent chemical castration as an alternative to prison.
Received a posthumous royal pardon in 2013 for his 1952 conviction.
Died on June 7, 1954, from cyanide poisoning, officially ruled as suicide.
Featured on the UK's £50 note, issued in 2021, honoring his contributions to science and computing.
The Turing Award, often referred to as the 'Nobel Prize of Computing,' is named in his honor.
Known for his eccentricities, such as chaining his tea mug to a radiator to prevent theft.

summary.fr: Ce scientifique britannique a marqué l'histoire de l'informatique en inventant la machine de Turing, un modèle fondamental des ordinateurs modernes. Pendant la Seconde Guerre mondiale, il a joué un rôle clé dans le décryptage du code Enigma, aidant les Alliés à gagner la guerre. Ses travaux sur l'intelligence artificielle et la morphogenèse biologique restent influents aujourd'hui.
summary.en: This British scientist revolutionized computer science by developing the Turing machine, a foundational model for modern computers. During World War II, he played a pivotal role in breaking the German Enigma code, helping the Allies secure victory. His work on artificial intelligence and biological pattern formation continues to inspire today.

name: Charles de Gaulle
facts: Born on November 22, 1890, in Lille, France, into a devoutly Catholic and patriotic family.
Graduated from the prestigious Saint-Cyr military academy and served as an infantry officer during World War I.
Was captured by the Germans in 1916 during the Battle of Verdun and spent 32 months as a prisoner of war.
Advocated for the use of armored divisions in warfare, publishing 'Vers l'Armée de Métier' (Toward a Professional Army) in 1934.
Led the Free French Forces during World War II after delivering the famous Appeal of 18 June 1940 from London, urging resistance against Nazi occupation.
Established the French National Committee in 1941 and later the French Committee of National Liberation in 1943 to govern Free French territories.
Served as the President of the Provisional Government of the French Republic from 1944 to 1946, overseeing post-war reconstruction.
Resigned from politics in 1946 due to disagreements with the proposed constitution for the Fourth Republic.
Founded the political party Rassemblement du Peuple Français (RPF) in 1947 to promote his vision of a strong executive branch.
Returned to power in 1958 during the Algerian Crisis, drafting a new constitution that established the Fifth Republic.
Served as the first President of the Fifth Republic from 1959 to 1969, implementing policies to strengthen France's global standing.
Granted independence to Algeria in 1962 after a prolonged and bloody war, despite strong opposition from French settlers and the military.
Oversaw France's development of its own nuclear deterrent, known as the Force de frappe, in the 1960s.
Vetoed Britain's entry into the European Economic Community twice, in 1963 and 1967, citing concerns over its commitment to European integration.
Survived multiple assassination attempts, including the 1962 Petit-Clamart attack by the Organisation armée secrète (OAS).

image.author: The National Archives UK

summary.fr: Né en 1890, ce militaire et homme d'État français a marqué l'histoire en dirigeant la Résistance pendant la Seconde Guerre mondiale. Il a fondé la Cinquième République en 1958 et été président jusqu'en 1969, renforçant le rôle de la France sur la scène internationale. Son leadership pendant les crises, comme la guerre d'Algérie, reste célèbre.
summary.en: Born in 1890, this French military leader and statesman became famous for leading the Free French Forces during World War II. He established the Fifth Republic in 1958 and served as its first president until 1969, shaping modern France. His leadership during crises, like the Algerian War, left a lasting legacy.

name: Margaret Thatcher
facts: Served as the first female Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1979 to 1990.
Was the longest-serving British prime minister of the 20th century.
Earned the nickname 'The Iron Lady' for her uncompromising politics and leadership style.
Studied chemistry at Somerville College, Oxford, and later trained as a barrister.
Won the Conservative Party leadership election in 1975, becoming the first woman to lead a major political party in the UK.
Implemented a series of economic policies known as 'Thatcherism,' emphasizing deregulation, privatization, and free-market capitalism.
Led the UK during the Falklands War in 1982, which resulted in a British victory over Argentina.
Survived an assassination attempt by the IRA in 1984 during the Conservative Party conference in Brighton.
Played a key role in the end of the Cold War, forming a strong alliance with U.S. President Ronald Reagan.
Introduced the controversial Community Charge, commonly known as the 'poll tax,' which contributed to her political downfall.
Resigned as Prime Minister in November 1990 after losing the support of her party.
Was appointed to the Order of the Garter, the highest order of chivalry in the UK, in 1995.
Published two volumes of memoirs: *The Downing Street Years* (1993) and *The Path to Power* (1995).
Suffered from dementia in her later years and passed away on April 8, 2013, at the age of 87.

image.author: derivative work by Begoon

summary.fr: Cette femme britannique a marqué l'histoire en devenant la première femme Premier ministre du Royaume-Uni, de 1979 à 1990. Surnommée la 'Dame de fer' pour son leadership intransigeant, elle a profondément transformé l'économie du pays avec des politiques libérales. Figure majeure de la politique européenne du XXe siècle, elle a aussi joué un rôle clé dans la fin de la Guerre froide.
summary.en: This British woman made history as the first female Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, serving from 1979 to 1990. Known as the 'Iron Lady' for her resolute leadership, she reshaped the UK's economy with free-market policies. A key 20th-century political figure, she also influenced the end of the Cold War through her alliance with the U.S.

name: Che Guevara
facts: Born on June 14, 1928, in Rosario, Argentina, into a middle-class family of Spanish-Irish descent.
Studied medicine at the University of Buenos Aires and graduated as a physician in 1953.
Traveled extensively across South America on a motorcycle in 1952, an experience that radicalized his political views.
Joined Fidel Castro’s 26th of July Movement in Mexico in 1955 to overthrow Cuban dictator Fulgencio Batista.
Played a key role in the Cuban Revolution as a military strategist and commander, leading guerrilla forces to victory in 1959.
Served as president of the National Bank of Cuba and later as Minister of Industry following the revolution.
Advocated for the creation of a
new socialist man
through moral incentives rather than material ones.
Authored the influential manual
Guerrilla Warfare
in 1960, outlining strategies for revolutionary movements.
Delivered a famous speech at the United Nations in 1964, condemning imperialism and advocating for global revolution.
Resigned from all positions in Cuba in 1965 to spread revolutionary ideals abroad, first in Congo and later in Bolivia.
Captured by Bolivian forces on October 8, 1967, with the assistance of the CIA, and executed the following day.
Became a global symbol of rebellion and anti-imperialism, often depicted in iconic photographs and artwork.
Posthumously published diaries, including
The Motorcycle Diaries
The Bolivian Diary
, provided insights into his life and ideology.
Inspired numerous leftist movements worldwide, though his methods and legacy remain highly controversial.
Remains a cultural icon, with his image appearing on posters, clothing, and murals as a symbol of resistance.

summary.fr: Médecin argentin devenu une figure majeure de la révolution cubaine, il a combattu aux côtés de Fidel Castro pour renverser le régime de Batista. Connu pour ses théories sur la guérilla et son engagement pour le socialisme, il a marqué l’histoire politique de l’Amérique latine dans les années 1950-1960. Son image est devenue un symbole mondial de la rébellion et de l’idéal révolutionnaire.
summary.en: An Argentine doctor turned key figure in the Cuban Revolution, he fought alongside Fidel Castro to overthrow Batista’s regime. Famous for his guerrilla warfare theories and socialist ideals, he shaped Latin American politics in the 1950s and 1960s. His legacy remains a global symbol of rebellion and revolutionary ideals.

name: Dalai Lama
facts: Was recognized as the 14th Dalai Lama at the age of two in 1937, following Tibetan Buddhist tradition.
Born on July 6, 1935, in Taktser, a small village in northeastern Tibet.
Assumed full political power as the head of the Tibetan government in 1950 at the age of 15.
Fled to India in 1959 following a failed Tibetan uprising against Chinese rule and established a government-in-exile in Dharamsala.
Advocates for the Middle Way Approach, seeking genuine autonomy for Tibet within the People's Republic of China.
Received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1989 for his non-violent struggle for the liberation of Tibet.
Authored over 100 books, including 'The Art of Happiness' and 'Freedom in Exile,' sharing Buddhist teachings and personal philosophy.
Met with numerous world leaders, including Pope John Paul II, Nelson Mandela, and Barack Obama, to discuss peace and interfaith dialogue.
Founded the Mind & Life Institute in 1987 to explore the intersection of science and Buddhism.
Promotes secular ethics and compassion through initiatives like the Compassion in Action program.
Underwent monastic training from a young age, studying Buddhist philosophy, logic, and meditation under prominent teachers.
Delivered the prestigious Reith Lectures for the BBC in 1999, discussing the role of ethics in the new millennium.
Established the Tibetan Children's Villages to provide education and care for Tibetan refugee children.
Advocates for environmental protection and has spoken at the United Nations on climate change and sustainability.
Continues to travel globally, giving teachings and public talks on peace, happiness, and universal responsibility.

summary.fr: Ce leader spirituel tibétain est le 14e d'une lignée importante dans le bouddhisme tibétain. Né en 1935, il a été reconnu dès l'âge de deux ans et a dû fuir son pays en 1959 pour s'exiler en Inde. Prix Nobel de la paix en 1989, il est connu pour son engagement non violent en faveur de l'autonomie du Tibet et son dialogue interreligieux.
summary.en: This Tibetan spiritual leader is the 14th in a significant lineage of Tibetan Buddhism. Born in 1935, he was recognized at the age of two and later fled to India in 1959. A Nobel Peace Prize laureate in 1989, he is renowned for his non-violent advocacy for Tibet's autonomy and his efforts in interfaith dialogue.

name: Mother Teresa
facts: Was born on August 26, 1910, in Skopje, now the capital of North Macedonia, then part of the Ottoman Empire.
Joined the Sisters of Loreto at the age of 18 and was sent to India, where she began her novitiate in Darjeeling.
Taught at St. Mary’s High School in Calcutta from 1931 to 1948, becoming its principal in 1944.
Experienced a call within a call on September 10, 1946, during a train ride to Darjeeling, urging her to serve the poorest of the poor.
Left the Loreto convent in 1948 to live among the poor in the slums of Calcutta, wearing a simple white sari with a blue border.
Founded the Missionaries of Charity in 1950, a congregation dedicated to serving the destitute and dying in Calcutta.
Opened the first home for the dying, Nirmal Hriday (Pure Heart), in 1952, providing care for those abandoned by society.
Established Shanti Nagar (City of Peace) in 1955 for those suffering from leprosy, offering medical care and dignity.
Expanded the Missionaries of Charity globally, with over 4,500 sisters operating in 133 countries by the time of her death.
Received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1979 for her work in bringing help to suffering humanity.
Was beatified by Pope John Paul II in 2003, less than six years after her death, a record for modern times.
Was canonized as a saint by Pope Francis on September 4, 2016, in a ceremony attended by thousands in Vatican City.
Wrote numerous letters and books, including *Come Be My Light*, which revealed her decades-long experience of spiritual darkness.
Advocated for the dignity of all human life, famously saying, 'The greatest destroyer of peace is abortion.'
Died on September 5, 1997, in Calcutta, India, at the age of 87, leaving behind a legacy of compassion and service.

summary.fr: Cette femme albanaise a consacré sa vie aux plus pauvres en Inde. Religieuse catholique, elle a fondé les Missionnaires de la Charité pour aider les malades et les mourants. Elle a reçu le prix Nobel de la paix en 1979 pour son engagement humanitaire.
summary.en: This Albanian woman dedicated her life to serving the poorest in India. A Catholic nun, she founded the Missionaries of Charity to care for the sick and dying. She was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1979 for her humanitarian work.

name: Jeff Bezos
facts: Founded Amazon in 1994 as an online bookstore, which later expanded into the world's largest e-commerce and cloud computing company.
Stepped down as Amazon's CEO in 2021, transitioning to the role of Executive Chairman.
Launched Blue Origin in 2000, a private aerospace manufacturer and suborbital spaceflight services company.
Became the world's richest person in 2017, with a net worth surpassing $100 billion for the first time in 2018.
Purchased *The Washington Post* in 2013 for $250 million, revitalizing its digital journalism efforts.
Introduced Amazon Prime in 2005, a subscription service offering free shipping, streaming, and other benefits.
Graduated from Princeton University in 1986 with degrees in electrical engineering and computer science.
Worked on Wall Street in the 1980s and 1990s, including at D.E. Shaw & Co., before founding Amazon.
Announced the Climate Pledge in 2019, committing Amazon to reach net-zero carbon emissions by 2040.
Donated $10 billion in 2020 to launch the Bezos Earth Fund, aimed at combating climate change.
Flew to space aboard Blue Origin's New Shepard rocket on July 20, 2021, in the company's first crewed flight.
Faced criticism for Amazon's labor practices, including warehouse working conditions and anti-union efforts.
Divorced MacKenzie Scott in 2019, transferring a 4% stake in Amazon to her as part of the settlement, worth over $35 billion at the time.
Acquired Whole Foods Market in 2017 for $13.7 billion, expanding Amazon's presence in the grocery industry.
Published a memoir-style shareholder letter in 2018 outlining Amazon's leadership principles, including 'customer obsession' and 'invent and simplify.'

image.commons_file: Jeff Bezos at Amazon Spheres Grand Opening in Seattle - 2018 (39074799225) (cropped).jpg
image.author: Seattle City Council from Seattle

summary.fr: Cet entrepreneur américain a fondé Amazon en 1994, transformant une simple librairie en ligne en un géant du commerce électronique et du cloud. Il a également créé Blue Origin, une entreprise spatiale, et est devenu l'une des personnes les plus riches du monde grâce à ses innovations technologiques et ses investissements.
summary.en: This American entrepreneur founded Amazon in 1994, turning a small online bookstore into a global e-commerce and cloud computing leader. He also launched Blue Origin, a space exploration company, and became one of the world’s wealthiest individuals through technology and innovation.

name: Mark Zuckerberg
facts: Co-founded Facebook in February 2004 with college roommates Eduardo Saverin, Andrew McCollum, Dustin Moskovitz, and Chris Hughes while attending Harvard University.
Dropped out of Harvard University in 2004 to focus full-time on developing Facebook.
Launched Facebook initially as a social networking platform exclusively for Harvard students before expanding to other universities and eventually the general public.
Became the world's youngest self-made billionaire at the age of 23 in 2007.
Acquired Instagram for approximately $1 billion in cash and stock in April 2012.
Purchased WhatsApp for $19 billion in February 2014, one of the largest tech acquisitions at the time.
Testified before the U.S. Congress in April 2018 regarding Facebook's handling of user data and privacy concerns following the Cambridge Analytica scandal.
Announced the rebranding of Facebook's parent company to Meta Platforms, Inc. in October 2021, focusing on building the metaverse.
Ranked among the top 10 richest people in the world, with a net worth fluctuating around $100 billion as of recent years.
Pledged to donate 99% of his Facebook shares to charitable causes through the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, a philanthropic organization founded with his wife, Priscilla Chan.
Known for wearing a signature gray T-shirt and hoodie, simplifying his wardrobe to reduce decision fatigue.
Delivered a commencement speech at Harvard University in 2017, 13 years after dropping out, and was awarded an honorary degree.
Faced criticism for Facebook's role in the spread of misinformation and its impact on elections worldwide.
Launched Internet.org, later rebranded as Free Basics, aiming to provide internet access to underserved communities globally.

image.author: Anthony Quintano from Westminster, United States

summary.fr: Cet entrepreneur américain a cofondé Facebook en 2004 alors qu'il était étudiant à Harvard. Il est devenu l'un des plus jeunes milliardaires au monde grâce à cette plateforme de réseaux sociaux. Aujourd'hui, il dirige Meta, l'entreprise derrière Facebook, Instagram et WhatsApp, et s'investit dans des projets philanthropiques avec son épouse.
summary.en: This American entrepreneur co-founded Facebook in 2004 while a student at Harvard University. He became one of the world’s youngest billionaires through the social media platform. Now leading Meta, the company behind Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, he also focuses on philanthropy with his wife.

name: Bill Gates
facts: Co-founded Microsoft in 1975 with Paul Allen, which became the world's largest personal-computer software company.
Developed the MS-DOS operating system, which was the foundation for Microsoft's early success in the 1980s.
Created Windows, the dominant operating system for personal computers, first released in 1985.
Became the youngest billionaire in history at age 31 in 1987.
Stepped down as Microsoft CEO in 2000 but remained as chairman and chief software architect until 2008.
Launched the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation in 2000, one of the largest private charitable foundations in the world.
Pledged to donate the majority of his wealth to philanthropy through The Giving Pledge, co-founded with Warren Buffett in 2010.
Played a key role in eradicating polio and reducing malaria through global health initiatives funded by his foundation.
Authored the books *The Road Ahead* (1995) and *Business @ the Speed of Thought* (1999), both focused on technology and business.
Invested in numerous companies and technologies, including TerraPower, a nuclear energy venture, and Breakthrough Energy, a clean energy fund.
Known for his competitive nature, famously stating, 'Your most unhappy customers are your greatest source of learning.'
Regularly ranked among the wealthiest individuals in the world, with a net worth exceeding $100 billion as of recent years.
Advocated for improved education systems, particularly through the Gates Foundation's support of charter schools and teacher training programs.
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Barack Obama in 2016 for his philanthropic work.
Hosts an annual letter outlining global health and development priorities, often co-written with his ex-wife, Melinda French Gates.

image.commons_file: Bill Gates at the European Commission - 2025 - P067383-987995 (cropped).jpg
image.author: Bogdan Hoyaux / European Union

summary.fr: Cet homme d'affaires américain a cofondé Microsoft en 1975, révolutionnant l'industrie du logiciel avec des systèmes comme MS-DOS et Windows. Philanthrope engagé, il a créé une fondation majeure pour améliorer la santé mondiale et réduire les inégalités, tout en promouvant l'innovation technologique.
summary.en: This American businessman co-founded Microsoft in 1975, shaping the software industry with systems like MS-DOS and Windows. A dedicated philanthropist, he established a leading foundation to advance global health and education while driving technological progress.

name: Edith Piaf
facts: Born Édith Giovanna Gassion on December 19, 1915, in Paris, France.
Raised in a brothel by her grandmother after her mother abandoned her and her father, a street acrobat, was often absent.
Discovered singing on the streets of Paris by nightclub owner Louis Leplée in 1935, who gave her the nickname 'La Môme Piaf' (The Little Sparrow).
Wrote and performed the iconic song 'La Vie en Rose' in 1946, which became one of the most famous French songs of all time.
Survived a near-fatal car accident in 1951, which led to a lifelong struggle with morphine addiction and chronic pain.
Had a tumultuous love affair with French boxer Marcel Cerdan, who died in a plane crash in 1949 while traveling to meet her.
Composed and performed the song 'Non, Je Ne Regrette Rien' in 1960, which became an anthem of resilience and defiance.
Performed to sold-out crowds at the Paris Olympia in 1961, a venue where she had previously been booed off stage early in her career.
Collaborated with prominent French composers and lyricists, including Michel Emer, Charles Dumont, and Marguerite Monnot.
Received the Légion d'honneur, France's highest order of merit, in 1961 for her contributions to French culture.
Struggled with alcoholism, drug addiction, and multiple health issues throughout her life, including arthritis and liver disease.
Died of liver cancer on October 10, 1963, at the age of 47, in Plascassier, near Grasse, France.
Buried at Père Lachaise Cemetery in Paris, where her grave remains one of the most visited.
Inspired countless artists, including Charles Aznavour, who began his career as her opening act.

image.author: Studio Harcourt

summary.fr: Née à Paris en 1915, cette chanteuse française est devenue une icône de la chanson grâce à sa voix émouvante et ses textes poignants. Surnommée 'La Môme Piaf', elle a marqué l’histoire avec des titres comme 'La Vie en Rose' et 'Non, Je Ne Regrette Rien'. Son talent et sa résilience ont fait d’elle une légende de la musique française du XXe siècle.
summary.en: Born in Paris in 1915, this French singer became a chanson icon with her powerful voice and heartfelt lyrics. Known as 'La Môme Piaf', she left a lasting legacy with songs like 'La Vie en Rose' and 'Non, Je Ne Regrette Rien'. Her talent and resilience made her a defining figure in 20th-century French music.

name: Frank Sinatra
facts: Born on December 12, 1915, in Hoboken, New Jersey, to Italian immigrant parents.
Sold over 150 million records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling music artists of all time.
Won 11 Grammy Awards, including the Grammy Trustees Award, Grammy Legend Award, and Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award.
Began his musical career in the swing era as a singer with big bands led by Harry James and Tommy Dorsey.
Released his first album, *The Voice of Frank Sinatra*, in 1946, which became a commercial success.
Starred in over 60 films, including *From Here to Eternity* (1953), for which he won an Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor.
Founded Reprise Records in 1960, a label that signed artists like Dean Martin, Sammy Davis Jr., and Jimi Hendrix.
Performed the iconic song *My Way* in 1969, which became one of his signature tracks and a global anthem.
Was a member of the Rat Pack, a group of entertainers that included Dean Martin, Sammy Davis Jr., Peter Lawford, and Joey Bishop.
Headlined the Sands Hotel and Casino in Las Vegas during the 1950s and 1960s, helping to establish the city as an entertainment hub.
Received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Ronald Reagan in 1985 and the Congressional Gold Medal in 1997.
Known for his impeccable phrasing and timing, which influenced generations of singers, including Tony Bennett and Michael Bublé.
Had a tumultuous personal life, including high-profile marriages to Ava Gardner, Mia Farrow, and Barbara Marx.
Passed away on May 14, 1998, at the age of 82 due to a heart attack in Los Angeles, California.
Inducted into the Big Band and Jazz Hall of Fame in 1980 and the New Jersey Hall of Fame in 2008.

summary.fr: Ce chanteur et acteur américain, né en 1915, a marqué l'histoire de la musique avec son style unique dans le jazz et la pop traditionnelle. Surnommé *The Voice*, il a vendu plus de 150 millions de disques et remporté 11 Grammy Awards. Il a aussi brillé au cinéma, notamment avec un Oscar pour *Tant qu'il y aura des hommes*.
summary.en: This American singer and actor, born in 1915, shaped the music world with his iconic jazz and traditional pop style. Known as *The Voice*, he sold over 150 million records and won 11 Grammy Awards. He also starred in films, earning an Oscar for *From Here to Eternity*.

name: Louis Armstrong
facts: Born on August 4, 1901, in New Orleans, Louisiana, to a poor family in a rough neighborhood known as 'The Battlefield.'
Learned to play the cornet while living at the Colored Waifs' Home for Boys after being arrested for firing a gun on New Year's Eve in 1912.
Became a foundational influence in jazz, shifting the focus of the music from collective improvisation to solo performance.
Joined King Oliver's Creole Jazz Band in Chicago in 1922, which marked the beginning of his professional career.
Made his first recordings with Oliver in 1923, which are considered some of the most important early jazz recordings.
Married pianist and bandleader Lil Hardin in 1924, who encouraged him to pursue a solo career and develop his own style.
Formed the Hot Five and Hot Seven bands in the 1920s, producing groundbreaking recordings like 'West End Blues' (1928) and 'Potato Head Blues.'
Pioneered scat singing with the 1926 recording of 'Heebie Jeebies,' popularizing the vocal technique in jazz.
Became one of the first African American entertainers to 'cross over' to a wide white audience during the segregation era.
Starred in over 30 films, including 'High Society' (1956) alongside Bing Crosby and Frank Sinatra.
Recorded the iconic song 'What a Wonderful World' in 1967, which became one of his most enduring hits and a symbol of hope.
Awarded the Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award posthumously in 1972 for his contributions to music.
Known for his charismatic stage presence, gravelly voice, and distinctive trumpet playing, which influenced generations of musicians.
Traveled extensively as a goodwill ambassador for the U.S. State Department during the Cold War, earning the nickname 'Ambassador Satch.'
Died of a heart attack on July 6, 1971, in New York City, leaving behind a legacy as one of the most important figures in jazz history.

image.commons_file: Louis Armstrong, by Harry Warnecke and Gus Schoenbaechler, 1947.jpg
image.author: Harry Warnecke, Gus Schoenbaechler

summary.fr: Ce musicien américain, né en 1901 à La Nouvelle-Orléans, a révolutionné le jazz en mettant l'accent sur l'improvisation solo. Surnommé 'Satchmo', il a popularisé le scat et marqué l'histoire avec des titres comme *What a Wonderful World*. Pionnier, il a brisé les barrières raciales dans la musique.
summary.en: Born in 1901 in New Orleans, this American jazz musician transformed the genre by shifting focus to solo improvisation. Known as 'Satchmo,' he pioneered scat singing and became a cultural icon with songs like *What a Wonderful World*. He broke racial barriers in segregated America.

name: Andy Warhol
facts: Born on August 6, 1928, in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, to Slovakian immigrant parents.
Studied commercial art at the Carnegie Institute of Technology (now Carnegie Mellon University) in Pittsburgh.
Moved to New York City in 1949 to pursue a career as a commercial illustrator.
Gained fame in the 1960s as a leading figure in the visual art movement known as pop art.
Created iconic works such as *Campbell's Soup Cans* (1962), featuring 32 canvases each depicting a different flavor.
Produced the famous *Marilyn Diptych* (1962) shortly after Marilyn Monroe's death, using silkscreen printing techniques.
Founded *The Factory*, a New York City studio that became a hub for artists, celebrities, and counterculture figures.
Directed and produced experimental films, including *Empire* (1964), an 8-hour static shot of the Empire State Building.
Published *The Philosophy of Andy Warhol* in 1975, a book blending autobiography and musings on fame and consumerism.
Survived an assassination attempt on June 3, 1968, by radical feminist writer Valerie Solanas.
Designed the famous *Banana* album cover for The Velvet Underground & Nico in 1967.
Created the *Silver Clouds* installation in 1966, featuring floating metallic balloons filled with helium.
Died on February 22, 1987, due to complications following gallbladder surgery.
Posthumously awarded the Grammy Award for Best Album Package in 1988 for the cover of *Thelonious Monk: Underground*.
Founded *Interview* magazine in 1969, which became a platform for celebrity culture and avant-garde art.

image.commons_file: Andy Warhol at the Jewish Museum (by Bernard Gotfryd) – LOC.jpg
image.author: Bernard Gotfryd

summary.fr: Cet artiste américain, né en 1928, est une figure majeure du pop art dans les années 1960. Il est célèbre pour ses œuvres colorées inspirées par la culture populaire, comme les boîtes de soupe Campbell ou les portraits de Marilyn Monroe. Son atelier new-yorkais, *The Factory*, était un lieu emblématique de la contre-culture.
summary.en: This American artist, born in 1928, was a leading figure in the 1960s pop art movement. He is best known for his vibrant works inspired by consumer culture, such as Campbell's Soup Cans and Marilyn Monroe portraits. His New York studio, *The Factory*, became a cultural hub for artists and celebrities.

name: Salvador Dali
aliases: Salvador Domingo Felipe Jacinto Dalí i Domènech
facts: Born on May 11, 1904, in Figueres, Catalonia, Spain, into a middle-class family.
Studied at the Real Academia de Bellas Artes de San Fernando in Madrid, where he met Federico García Lorca and Luis Buñuel.
Expelled from the academy in 1926, shortly before his final exams, for declaring that none of the faculty were competent to examine him.
Became a leading figure in the Surrealist movement, known for his technical skill and striking, bizarre imagery.
Collaborated with filmmaker Luis Buñuel on the 1929 short film *Un Chien Andalou*, a landmark in Surrealist cinema.
Married Gala Diakonova, his muse and future wife, in 1934, after she left her first husband, poet Paul Éluard.
Created one of his most famous works, *The Persistence of Memory* (1931), featuring melting clocks in a dreamlike landscape.
Published his autobiography, *The Secret Life of Salvador Dalí*, in 1942, detailing his eccentric life and artistic philosophy.
Designed the dream sequence in Alfred Hitchcock’s 1945 film *Spellbound*, showcasing his influence beyond traditional art forms.
Moved to the United States in 1940, where he lived for eight years, gaining fame and commercial success.
Created the *Mae West Lips Sofa* (1937), a functional piece of furniture inspired by the actress’s lips, blending art and design.
Returned to Spain in 1948 and embraced Catholicism, incorporating religious themes into his later works, such as *The Sacrament of the Last Supper* (1955).
Designed the logo for Chupa Chups lollipops in 1969, demonstrating his versatility in commercial art.
Known for his flamboyant mustache, which became an iconic part of his public persona and was inspired by 17th-century Spanish master Diego Velázquez.
Died on January 23, 1989, in Figueres, Spain, and is buried in a crypt beneath the stage of his Theatre-Museum in the same city.

summary.fr: Cet artiste espagnol du XXe siècle est célèbre pour ses peintures surréalistes aux images étranges et oniriques. Connu pour des œuvres comme La Persistance de la mémoire avec ses montres molles, il a marqué l'art moderne par son imagination débordante et son style unique. Il a également collaboré avec des cinéastes et designers, élargissant son influence au-delà de la peinture.
summary.en: This 20th-century Spanish artist is famous for his surrealist paintings featuring bizarre and dreamlike imagery. Best known for works like The Persistence of Memory with its melting clocks, he shaped modern art with his vivid imagination and distinctive style. He also collaborated with filmmakers and designers, extending his influence beyond traditional painting.

name: Claude Monet
facts: Was born on November 14, 1840, in Paris, France, and grew up in Le Havre, Normandy.
Is considered the founder of French Impressionist painting and a key precursor to modernism.
Developed a painting technique that emphasized the play of natural light with broken color and rapid brushstrokes.
Created the iconic series *Water Lilies*, consisting of approximately 250 oil paintings depicting his flower garden at Giverny.
Painted *Impression, Sunrise* (1872), the work that gave the Impressionist movement its name.
Spent the last 43 years of his life in Giverny, where he designed and cultivated a water garden that became the subject of many of his works.
Suffered from cataracts in his later years, which significantly altered his color perception and painting style.
Was mentored by Eugène Boudin, who introduced him to plein-air (outdoor) painting techniques.
Served in the military in Algeria from 1861 to 1862, an experience that influenced his use of light and color.
Faced financial struggles early in his career but gained recognition and success later in life, particularly after the 1880s.
Married his first wife, Camille Doncieux, in 1870, who was a frequent model for his paintings, including *The Woman in the Green Dress*.
Had two sons, Jean and Michel, with Camille, who died of tuberculosis in 1879 at the age of 32.
Later married Alice Hoschedé in 1892, who had previously been married to one of his patrons, Ernest Hoschedé.
Died on December 5, 1926, at the age of 86, from lung cancer, and was buried in the Giverny church cemetery.

image.author: Nadar

summary.fr: Peintre français du XIXe siècle, il est le fondateur du mouvement impressionniste. Connu pour ses séries comme *Nymphéas*, il a révolutionné l'art par son usage de la lumière et des couleurs. Ses œuvres, comme *Impression, soleil levant*, ont marqué l'histoire de la peinture moderne.
summary.en: A 19th-century French painter, he founded the Impressionist movement. Famous for series like *Water Lilies*, he transformed art with his use of light and color. Works such as *Impression, Sunrise* defined modern painting.

name: Agatha Christie
facts: Born on September 15, 1890, in Torquay, Devon, England, to a wealthy upper-middle-class family.
Wrote her first novel, *The Mysterious Affair at Styles*, in 1916, introducing the iconic detective Hercule Poirot.
Created another famous detective, Miss Marple, who first appeared in *The Murder at the Vicarage* in 1930.
Authored 66 detective novels, 14 short story collections, and the world's longest-running play, *The Mousetrap*.
Published under the pseudonym Mary Westmacott for six romance novels, exploring different literary styles.
Disappeared for 11 days in December 1926, sparking a nationwide search and media frenzy before being found in a hotel.
Worked as a pharmacy assistant during World War I, gaining knowledge of poisons that later featured in her novels.
Married archaeologist Max Mallowan in 1930, accompanying him on digs in the Middle East, which inspired several novels.
Wrote *Murder on the Orient Express* in 1934, one of her most famous works, set on the luxurious train.
Received a Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire (DBE) in 1971 for her contributions to literature.
Her novel *And Then There Were None* is one of the best-selling books of all time, with over 100 million copies sold.
Died on January 12, 1976, in Wallingford, Oxfordshire, leaving behind an unfinished manuscript featuring Hercule Poirot.
Her works have been translated into over 100 languages, making her the most-translated individual author in history.
The Guinness World Records lists her as the best-selling novelist of all time, with estimated sales of over 2 billion copies.
Her play *The Mousetrap* opened in London's West End in 1952 and has been running continuously ever since, holding the record for the longest initial run.

image.commons_file: Agatha Christie in Nederland (detectiveschrijfster), bij aankomst op Schiphol me, Bestanddeelnr 916-8898 (cropped).jpg
image.author: Joop van Bilsen for Anefo

summary.fr: Cette auteure britannique du XXe siècle est célèbre pour ses romans policiers. Elle a créé des détectives emblématiques comme Hercule Poirot et Miss Marple. Ses œuvres, parmi les plus lues au monde, ont marqué la littérature à énigmes.
summary.en: This British author from the 20th century is renowned for her mystery novels. She created iconic detectives like Hercule Poirot and Miss Marple. Her works, some of the most widely read in the world, shaped detective fiction.

name: Ernest Hemingway
facts: Born on July 21, 1899, in Oak Park, Illinois, to a physician father and a musician mother.
Worked as a reporter for *The Kansas City Star* before enlisting as an ambulance driver in World War I.
Served on the Italian front in 1918 and was seriously wounded, an experience that inspired *A Farewell to Arms*.
Published his first novel, *The Sun Also Rises*, in 1926, which became a defining work of the Lost Generation.
Lived in Paris during the 1920s, associating with other expatriate writers like F. Scott Fitzgerald and Gertrude Stein.
Won the Pulitzer Prize in 1953 for *The Old Man and the Sea*, a novella about an aging fisherman’s struggle with a marlin.
Received the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1954 for his mastery of narrative art and influence on contemporary style.
Known for his concise, direct prose style, often referred to as the
Wrote seven novels, six short story collections, and two non-fiction works during his lifetime.
Survived two plane crashes in Africa in 1954, which left him in chronic pain for the rest of his life.
Was an avid big-game hunter, fisherman, and bullfighting enthusiast, themes that appear in his works like *Death in the Afternoon*.
Suffered from depression, alcoholism, and declining health in his later years before dying by suicide on July 2, 1961.
His posthumously published memoir, *A Moveable Feast*, recounts his years in Paris during the 1920s.
Influenced generations of writers with his minimalist style and themes of courage, stoicism, and existential struggle.

summary.fr: Cet écrivain américain du XXe siècle est célèbre pour son style d'écriture sobre et direct. Il a marqué la littérature avec des œuvres comme *Le Vieil Homme et la Mer* et *L'Adieu aux armes*, inspirées par ses expériences de guerre. Lauréat du prix Nobel de littérature en 1954, il incarne la génération perdue.
summary.en: This 20th-century American author is renowned for his concise and direct writing style. He shaped modern literature with works like *The Old Man and the Sea* and *A Farewell to Arms*, drawing from his wartime experiences. A Nobel Prize winner in 1954, he became a defining figure of the Lost Generation.

name: Victor Hugo
facts: Born on February 26, 1802, in Besançon, France, to Joseph Léopold Sigisbert Hugo and Sophie Trébuchet.
Published his first novel, *Han d'Islande*, at the age of 20 in 1823, marking the beginning of a prolific literary career.
Wrote *The Hunchback of Notre-Dame* (1831), a novel that revived interest in Gothic architecture and the cathedral itself.
Served as a member of the Académie française from 1841, a prestigious institution dedicated to the French language.
Exiled from France in 1851 after opposing Napoleon III’s coup d'état, living in Guernsey for nearly 20 years.
Completed *Les Misérables* in 1862, a monumental work exploring themes of justice, redemption, and social inequality.
Wrote *Les Contemplations* (1856), a collection of poetry reflecting personal grief, including the death of his daughter Léopoldine.
Advocated for social and political reforms, including the abolition of the death penalty and universal education.
Known for his dramatic plays, such as *Hernani* (1830), which sparked a famous literary battle between Romantics and Classicists.
Spent his final years in Paris, where he died on May 22, 1885, at the age of 83, prompting a national mourning period.
Received a state funeral attended by over two million people, with his body lying in state under the Arc de Triomphe.
Buried in the Panthéon in Paris, alongside other French luminaries like Voltaire and Émile Zola.
His works have been adapted into countless films, musicals, and stage productions, including the famous musical *Les Misérables*.
Wrote *Toilers of the Sea* (1866), a novel inspired by his time in exile on the Channel Islands, exploring human struggle against nature.
Considered a leading figure of the Romantic movement in French literature, known for his vivid imagination and emotional depth.

image.commons_file: Victor Hugo by Étienne Carjat 1876 - full.jpg
image.author: Étienne Carjat

summary.fr: Écrivain français du XIXe siècle, il est l’un des plus grands auteurs de la littérature mondiale. Il a marqué son époque avec des œuvres comme *Les Misérables* et *Notre-Dame de Paris*, qui mêlent engagement social et puissance romanesque. Ses poèmes et pièces de théâtre ont aussi influencé le mouvement romantique.
summary.en: A 19th-century French writer, he is one of the most celebrated figures in world literature. Best known for masterpieces like *Les Misérables* and *The Hunchback of Notre-Dame*, he combined social commentary with compelling storytelling. His poetry and plays also shaped the Romantic movement.

name: J.K. Rowling
facts: Born on July 31, 1965, in Yate, Gloucestershire, England.
Created the best-selling book series in history, *Harry Potter*, which has sold over 500 million copies worldwide.
Wrote the first *Harry Potter* book, *Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone*, in Edinburgh cafés while living on state benefits as a single mother.
Published the *Harry Potter* series under the pen name J.K. Rowling, with the 'K' standing for Kathleen, her grandmother’s name, added at the publisher’s request.
The *Harry Potter* series has been translated into 80 languages and adapted into eight blockbuster films.
Founded the charity Lumos in 2005, which works to end the institutionalization of children globally.
Wrote *The Casual Vacancy* in 2012, her first novel for adults, under her own name.
Published the *Cormoran Strike* detective series under the pseudonym Robert Galbraith, which was later revealed to be her work.
Received the Order of the British Empire (OBE) in 2001 for services to literature.
Donated millions to multiple sclerosis research, a disease her mother suffered from before passing away in 1990.
Wrote the screenplay for the *Fantastic Beasts* film series, expanding the *Harry Potter* universe.
Was the first person to become a billionaire by writing books, though she lost billionaire status due to charitable donations.
Faced rejection from 12 publishers before *Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone* was accepted by Bloomsbury in 1997.
Delivered a commencement speech at Harvard University in 2008, titled *The Fringe Benefits of Failure, and the Importance of Imagination*.
Established the Volant Charitable Trust in 2000, which supports causes related to multiple sclerosis, poverty, and social inequality.

summary.fr: Cette auteure britannique est célèbre pour avoir créé la série de livres la plus vendue au monde, *Harry Potter*. Elle a écrit le premier tome dans des cafés d’Édimbourg alors qu’elle était mère célibataire et vivait avec des aides sociales. Son œuvre, traduite en 80 langues, a marqué la littérature fantastique et inspiré des millions de lecteurs.
summary.en: This British author is best known for creating the best-selling book series in history, *Harry Potter*. She wrote the first book in Edinburgh cafés while living on state benefits as a single mother. Her fantasy series, translated into 80 languages, has shaped modern literature and captivated readers worldwide.

name: Stephen King
